tooltip. For information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management server details and
to download the latest schema updates, see Enabling Automapping and Choosing the AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management Model.

Advanced Options for Association Attributes

You can set the association owner for the element through the gateway. The following options are available:

•  This object: The object on which the association attribute is defined is consider the owner of the association.
For example, if the association "is part of" is defined for a Nozzle, then the association is published as (for
example)"Nozzle is a part of Equipment".

•  Referenced Object: The object which is referenced in the association is considered the owner of the

association. For example, if the association "has parts" is referencing an Equipment, then association is
published as (for example) "Equipment has parts Nozzle"

•  Is external reference: Select the check box if you want the association to be present in the output regardless

of whether the referenced object exists or not.

Adding IF and FOR EACH Statements

You can specify IF and FOR EACH statements to fine-tune your configuration.

Note: The drag-and-drop feature is currently not supported for these statements.

Adding IF Conditions

If you want to specify any conditions based on which the objects and their attributes must be exported, you can
do so by adding an IF condition.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 56

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

To add an IF condition:

1.  Click the Grids, DB Views, Drawings, Datasheets or Reports tab depending on for what you want to define

the mapping.

2.  Select the required class from the Current Mapping list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, do one of the following:

4.

5.

If you want to add the condition to the attributes of an object, select the root of the object tree for which
you want to add the condition, right-click and select Add control > If Condition.

If you want to add the condition on the objects, right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM
Object Mapping area, and select Add If Condition.

6.  The IF condition is added to the tree and the area to add the condition appears.

7.  Click the Add icon. The PML Condition Expression box appears.

8.  Type the condition in the box. An example condition is as follows: NAME eq 'TRA001-RECT'

9.  After providing the condition, to specify what needs to be done when the condition is met, add a member to
the If statement. To do this, in the EIWM Object Mapping area, select If, right-click and select Add EIWM,
and then select the attribute that you want to add. For information on how to add attributes, see Adding
Attributes to Classes.

10.

If you want to specify what needs to be done when the condition is not met, you must add an Else member
to If. To do this, select If, right-click and select Add control > Else.

11.  After adding the Else member, select Else, right-click and select Add EIWM, and then select the attribute

that you want to add. An example IF condition is as follows:

Note: You can also add nested conditions using IF-ELSE IF, IF-IF-ELSE IF, IF-FOR EACH-ELSE and so
on. You can also use regular expressions to add these conditions.

Adding FOR EACH Statements

A FOR EACH statement enables you to specify the actions to be taken for every ARRAY item of an attribute or
PML expression (which returns an ARRAY result).

To add a FOR EACH condition:

1.  Click the Grids, DB Views, Drawings, Datasheets or Reports tab depending on for what you want to define

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

the mapping.

2.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a FOR EACH
condition, right-click and select Add control > For Each. The For Each condition is added to the object tree
and the Variable name and PML expression with array result areas appear.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

a.  Enter a variable name in the Variable name box.

Note: The variable name must only contain alphabets, numbers and an underscore ("_").

b.  Enter a PML expression that results an array of values in the PML expression with array result box. The

FOR EACH condition is added.

Adding Unmapped Attributes

If you want to export all the attributes of a class but have mapped only a few of them, you can use the All not
mapped attributes option to export all the attributes that have not been explicitly mapped. When you use this
option, the gateway loads all the attributes that are currently defined for the class into the EIWM Object
Mapping area and considers them for export.

To include unmapped attributes:

1.  Click the Grids, DB Views, Drawings, Datasheets or Reports tab depending on for what you want to add the

unmapped attributes.

2.  Select the required class from the Current Mapping list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add the
unmapped attributes, right-click and select Tools > Append all not mapped attributes. The list of all
unmapped attributes is loaded.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 58

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

You can review the list of attributes and modify or delete the mappings based on your need.

Selecting Output Format

You can specify the format for your output. Currently, XML (also known as EIWM), JSON, and CSV formats are
supported for grids, DB views, drawings and datasheets. For reports, you can generate the report output in PDF,
XLSX and CSV formats and the engineering output in XML (also known as EIWM), JSON, and CSV formats. Output
is generated based on the mappings specified and the files are placed in the staging area for both formats.

To specify the output format for grids, DB Views, drawings and datasheets:

1.  Click the Grids, DB Views, Drawings or Datasheets tab depending on for what you want to specify the

output format.

2.  Click Options.

3.

In the Engineering Output Format area, select the required output format:

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 59

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

a.  From the Available Column names list, select a column name for the CSV file. The column name is

added to the Column names area.

Note: The list displays column names generated from characteristics and properties defined in EIWM
class mappings for all selected elements.

b.  Repeat the above step to add more column names.

c.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the

arrow keys.

d.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column
names for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view,
drawing, and so on).

4.

In the Datasheets tab, additional Datasheet data sources options are available in the simplified mode. This
option group gathers settings related with controlling output engineering information when publishing
datasheets.

Note: This group is only available when configuration is not set to Advanced Mode.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 60

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Following options are part of datasheet data sources group:

•  Use database view sources: When checked, all datasheet referenced database views will be used to
generate engineering information in the output. When unchecked, no database view will be used for
generating engineering information. By default, this options is unchecked.

•  Include related engineering items: When checked, all engineering items referenced from within

datasheet will be included in the output engineering information. When unchecked, only engineering
items directly assigned to datasheet will be included in output engineering information. By default, this
option is checked.

To specify the output format for reports:

1.  On the Reports tab, click Options.

2.

In the Report output format area, select the required output format for report data:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 61

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate report data.

•  PDF: Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the PDF format.

•  CSV: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the CSV format.

•  XLSX: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the XLSX format.

3.

In the Engineering output format area, select the required output format for engineering data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None:Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

Note: To avoid overwriting the output files, the CSV option for engineering output format cannot be
used together with the CSV option for report output format.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

a.  From the Available Column names list, select a column for the CSV file. The column is added to the

Column names area.

Note: The list displays column names generated from characteristics and properties defined in EIWM
class mappings for all selected elements.

a.  Repeat the above step to add more column names.

b.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the

arrow keys.

c.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column
names for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view,
drawing, and so on).

Integration service output options

1.  Click the Grids or DB Views, tab depending on for what you want to specify the output format.

2.

In the Append units of measurement area, select the required option:

AVEVA™ Gateways

The following options are available:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to add any unit of measurement.

Generating Graphical Data

Graphical data can be generated when you are exporting drawings. You can specify the presentation settings and
the mapping for tagging the classes.

Tagging Classes in SVG Files

When you are exporting drawings, you can specify how the tag ID of class must appear in the SVG file through
the SVG tab in the Class Mappings page. The tag ID for the class is retrieved from the mapping specified here
and the same is displayed on the SVG file when the class is selected.

Note: Tagging is supported only for those classes that can be selected in the SVG file. Accordingly, the settings in
the SVG tab are applicable to only those element types.

To map the tag ID of a class:

1.  Click the Drawings tab.

2.  Select the required class in the Current Mapping list.

3.  On the SVG tab, do one of the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 64

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the tag ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is used as the tag ID of the class.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the tag ID is also
deleted.

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option and

select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute value is used as the tag ID for the class.

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a PML
expression in the PML Expression box. The result of the PML expression is used as the tag ID for the
class.

Note: The tag IDs for the classes without a mapping are not displayed in the SVG file.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 65

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Specifying Presentation Settings

Through the Presentation page on the Drawings tab, you can specify the settings for the SVG file. You can also
import the settings from an XML file created using the legacy gateway. For more information about the
compatibility with the legacy gateway, see .

To import the settings from an existing file:

1.  On the Presentation Settings page, click Import.

2.  Browse to the file that contains the presentation settings, and click Open. The presentation settings are

loaded to the page.

To specify the presentation settings:

1.  Click the Drawings tab.

2.  On the Presentation page, do one or more of the following based on your requirements:

•  In the Miscellaneous area, do the following based on your requirements:

•  For the Line thickness scale factor box, specify the thickness for the lines in the SVG file. The default

value is 1.

•  For the Background Color box, click Pick Color and then choose a color for the background.

Note: You can add custom colors to the color palette. Use the Define Custom Colors button available on
the Colors dialog box to define custom colors.

•  In the Color Mappings area, do the following based on your requirements:

a.  Click the Add icon to add a row.

b.

In the From column, enter the RGB value or the name of the color that needs to be mapped.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 66

c.

In the To column, enter the RGB value or the name of the color to define the mapping.

Note: You can use the Delete icon to delete the currently selected mapping.

•  In the Font Mappings area, do the following based on your requirements:

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

a.  Click the Add icon to add a row.

b.

In the From column, select or enter a font from the list that needs to be mapped.

c.

In the To column, select or enter a font to define the mapping.

Note: You can use the Delete icon to delete the currently selected mapping.

Saving the Configuration

The Save configuration dialog box appears if you are creating a new configuration and have clicked Save.

Enter a name for the configuration in the Name box and click OK.

Note: The configuration name cannot contain white spaces and special characters.

Specify Common Settings for Export

You can use the Settings dialog box to specify the common export options that will be applicable to all exports
irrespective of the configuration. The gateway uses the export options specified in the Other tab of the Settings
dialog box during the export.

To specify the common export options:

1.  Click Settings.

2.  Click the Other tab.

3.

In the Context Configuration area, do the following based on your requirements:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 67

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

•  Enter a context name in the Context Name box. You can use the context name to specify a context for

the exported data. You can provide a nested context by providing the highest context first followed by a
"+" or "|" symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or ROOT|PARENT|CHILD). By default, the
context is PROJECTCONTEXT.

Note: The Context Name box cannot be empty and must not contain white spaces.

•  For the File Suffix box, enter a file suffix. Based on the file suffix you provide, AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management picks up the relevant XSLT file to process the exported EIWM file. However, before you
specify a file suffix, make sure that the relevant XSLT file is present with your AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management server. By default, the file suffix is _null which means that AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management will process the EIWM file without any XSLT. For more information about XSLT files, refer to
the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Note: The File Suffix box cannot be empty and must not contain invalid characters for file names (\ / : * ?
" < > |) or white spaces.

•  Select the Generate 'folder.vnet' file check box if you want a folder.vnet file to be generated. The

folder.vnet file contains information about the class in which the generated SVG file must be placed
and the context in which the object must be saved. By default, this check box is cleared.

•  Select the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box if the output files (EIWM and SVG files) of the exported

elements must be picked automatically by the Import Controller for processing in AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management. By default, the check box is cleared. If you select the check box, make sure

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 68

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

that the staging area of the output files is listed as a watch folder in the Import Controller. For more
information about the Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub
and Dashboard Installation Guide.

4.

In the Performance Settings area, do the following based on your requirements:

•  Select the Optimise Memory Usage option, if you want to reduce excessive memory usage and improve

the performance of the Gateway when it exports large datasets.

•  Under Grids and Database Views section, Partition threshold and Rows per partition has pre-defined
values to improve memory consumption while publishing large grids and database views. These values
are configurable. If the published grid or database view row count exceeds Partitions threshold then,
gateway will export rows data in parts and each part contains maximum number of rows defined with
rows per partition property.

Provide Filters and Mappings Using Regular Expressions

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) provides three PML functions related to Regular Expressions
that can be used when applying filters and configuring mappings.

This section describes the three PML functions.

define function !!regexmatches(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is BOOLEAN

--  Description:

--  Function to test if given text string matches particular regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Text to test with given regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  TRUE  If text string matches the given regular expression.

--  FALSE  Otherwise.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexmatches(:DisplayName, 'P-[0-9]*')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> FALSE

‘P-067’ -> TRUE

‘P-23’ -> TRUE

‘S-067’ -> FALSE

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 69

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

‘D-23’ -> FALSE

define function !!regexreplaceall(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING, !replacement is
STRING) is STRING

--  Description:

--  Function that returns a string after it replaces each substring of that matches the
given regular expression with the given replacement.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to replace matches using regular expressions

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

--  3  R  STRING  Replacement string which will be used to replace all matches

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  STRING  Text with all matched regular expression patterns replaced

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexreplaceall(:DisplayName, '-[0-9]*', '-XXX')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S-XXX’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D-XXX’

define function !!regexsplit(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is ARRAY

--  Description:

--  Function that returns an array of strings which were separated using delimiter
substring or regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to split using delimiter substring or regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  Delimiter substring or regular expression to use for splitting input text

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 70

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  ARRAY  Array of string

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexsplit(:DisplayName, '-')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P’, ‘067’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P’, ‘23’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S’, ‘067’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D’, ‘23’

Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to upgrade configurations using command line and PML
macros.

Manage Configurations Using PML Macros

The gateway provides the IEDConfigurationManager PML object that contains the following methods.

Method

GETAVAILABLE()

ISSUPPORTED(STRING)

REQUIRESUPGRADE(STRING)

UPGRADE(STRING)

UPGRADE(ARRAY)

UPGRADEALL()

Description

Returns an array of available configurations names

Returns true if configurations is supported; else,
returns false

Returns true if configuration requires upgrade; else,
returns false

Upgrades a single configuration

Upgrades multiple configurations

Upgrades all available configurations

The following is an example of the IEDConfigurationManager object.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!manager= object IEDConfigurationManager()

!manager.upgrade(CfgName1)

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 71

Manage Configurations Using Command Line

The gateway provides a new command config upgrade for upgrading configurations. The command can be
used with the following options.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Option

-select "string"

-selectall "bool"

Examples:

Description

Enables you to pass configuration names for upgrade

Enables you to specify if all available configurations
must be upgraded. Overrides anything that was
passed with '-select' option.

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Engineering.exe config upgrade TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -selectall true

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Engineering.exe config upgrade -TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -select

"CfgName1 CfgName2 CfgName3"

For information on how to upgrade a configuration using the user interface, see Load a Configuration.

Use Environment Variables to Specify Locations

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering supports usage of environment variables for specifying locations in the
following instances:

•  While providing Staging Area location

Example: %aveva_design_user%\IEDGateway\StagingArea

•  When providing Logging Location

Example: %aveva_design_user%IEDGateway\Logs

Modify a Configuration

After creating and saving a configuration, you can modify the configuration.

To modify the configuration:

1.  Load the configuration you want to modify. For information on how to load a configuration, see Loading a

Configuration.

2.  After the configuration is loaded, perform one or more of the following procedures depending on what

needs to be modified.

Note: To delete a configuration, select the configuration in the Load Configuration list and then click the
Delete icon. You cannot delete the default configuration. However, using the Delete icon, you can reset it to
the original state.

3.  After you make the required changes based on your need, click Save to save the configuration. The

configuration is now modified.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 72

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Modify Class Selection

To modify the selection of classes:

•  If you want to add classes, see Selecting Classes.

•  If you want to delete any classes, select the class in the Class selections area and click the Delete icon.

•  If you want to change the selection of classes for export, select the Active check box for the classes you want

to export and clear the check box for the classes that you do not want to export.

Modify Filters

For information about how to apply new filters, see Applying Filters.

To modify existing filters:

1.

In the Class selections area, highlight the class for which the filter needs to be modified. The list of filters
applicable to the class are displayed.

2.  Select the row of the filter which needs to be modified, and then click the Edit Filter icon. The filter is loaded

into the Attribute Filter, Status Filter or PML Expression Filter area.

Note: If you want to modify a filter that has been added to multiple classes, you can do so using the Select
Classes with Same Filter icon. Select the filter and click the Select Classes with Same Filter icon. All the
classes where the filter is applied are highlighted in the Class selections area.

3.  Modify the filter based on your need and then click Update Filter. The updated filter is displayed in the list of

filters.

Note: To delete a filter, highlight the corresponding row and then click the Delete Filter icon. If the filter has
been applied to multiple classes, the filter is deleted from all the classes.

Modify Item Mappings

To modify the item mappings, do the following based on your requirements:

1.

If you want to modify the mapping for the File node, see Adding File Node.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the File node, select the root of the tree File, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the File node along with all its members is deleted.

2.

If you want to modify the mapping for the Object ID, see Mapping Object ID.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the Object ID, select the root of the tree Object, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the Object ID along with all its members is deleted. However, remember
that an element without an Object ID is not exported.

3.

If you want to modify the attributes for the classes, select the attribute in the tree and modify it according to
your need. For more information, see .Adding Attributes to Classes

Note: If you want to delete an attribute, select the attribute in the tree, right-click and select Remove.
However, remember that an attribute that is not mapped is not exported.

4.

If you want to modify the conditions for the attributes, select the condition in the tree and modify it
according to your need. For more information, see Adding IF and FOR EACH Statements.

Note: If you want to delete a condition, select the condition in the tree, right-click and select Remove. The

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 73

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

condition along with all its members is deleted.

5.

If you want to change the presentation mappings, see Specifying Presentation Settings .

6.

If you want to modify the tagging for the element type, see Tagging Classes in SVG Files.

Modify General Information

To modify the general information:

•  On the General tab, modify the description, logging and staging area details based on your requirements. For

more information, see Providing General Information.

Modify Common Settings for Export

To modify the common options:

•  On the Other tab of the Settings dialog box, modify the common export options based on your

requirements. For more information, see Specify Common Settings for Export.

Note: Remember that the common export options that are specified in the Other tab of the Settings dialog
box at the time of performing the export are used by the gateway for performing the export.

Publish Data to AVEVA Asset Information Management (AIM)

You can publish the data from AVEVA™ Engineering through the user interface, the command line and using PML
macros.

Note: The data you want to publish must be saved before you can publish.

The following points must be noted about the output files:

•  The actual output for an export depends both on the classes selected in the configuration and the elements

selected for publishing. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a SITE element for publishing, all the
PIPE elements present in the SITE are exported. In this case, the number of EIWM files generated is
equal to the number of PIPEs in the SITE.

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for publishing, then only

the PIPE element is exported.

•  If you have selected SITE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for the publishing, there

will not be any output.

•  Similarly, the number of SVG files generated for an export also depends on your selection in the

configuration. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you are publishing an EQUIP element and have chosen to generate an SVG file for it, then an EIWM file

and an SVG file for the equipment are generated.

•  If you are publishing a SITE element and have chosen to generate an SVG file at the EQUIP level, then an

EIWM and an SVG for each EQUIP element present in the SITE is generated.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 74

Publishing Using User Interface

Data can be published from AVEVA™ Engineering in a number of ways. All of them display the Gateway window
from where you can choose the publishing options. The Gateway window can also be directly opened by clicking
Manage > Export group > Export > Gateway.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Through this window, you can view the items selected for publishing, change the publishing options, and view
the publishing status.

From the user interface of AVEVA™ Engineering, you can publish the data in the following ways:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 75

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

•  For Grids:

•  If you want to publish a grid, click the Grids tab and do one of the following:

•  Navigate to the required grid in the Grids Explorer, right-click and select Publish. The grid is added to

the Grids tab.

•  Select the required grid in the Grids Explorer and select Current grid from the list on the Grids tab in

the Gateway window, and click the Add icon. The selected grid is added to the Grids tab.

•  If you want to publish selected rows in a grid, do one of the following:

•  Open the grid, select the rows you want to publish, right-click and select Publish. Only the selected

rows are added to the Grids tab.

•  Open the grid, select the rows you want to publish. Then, on the Manage tab, in the Publish group,

click Selected Grid Rows. Only the selected rows are added to the Grids tab.

•  If you want to publish all grids available in a group, do the following:

•  Select the required group in the Grids Explorer and select Current group grid members from the list
in the Gateway window, and click the Add icon. The grids available in the group are added to the
Grids tab.

Note: To publish any grid or any rows of the grid, the Can be published to AVEVA AIM check box
must be selected in the properties of the grid. You can use the Remove and Remove all icons to
remove the selected grids.

•  For Drawings:

•  If you want to publish a drawing, click the Drawings tab and do one of the following:

•  Navigate to the required drawing in the Drawings Explorer, right-click and select Publish. The

drawing is added to the Drawings tab. All the sheets in the drawing are considered for publishing.
The output files are created for each sheet. All connected drawings are also considered for
publishing.

•  Select the drawing in the Drawings Explorer, select Current drawing from the list on the Drawings
tab in the Gateway window, and click the Add icon. The drawing is added to the Drawings tab.

•  If you want to publish an active drawing, do the following:

•  Open the drawing, select Active drawing from the list on the Drawings tab in the Gateway window,
and click the Add icon. The drawing is added to the Drawings tab. All the sheets in the drawing are
considered for publishing. The output files are created for each sheet. All connected drawings are
also considered for publishing.

Note: You can use the Remove and Remove all icons to remove the selected drawings.

•  If you want to publish only the active sheet of the drawing, do the following:

•  Open the required sheet of the drawing you want to publish. Then, on the Manage tab, in the
Publish group, click Active Drawing Sheet. The sheet that is currently open is considered for
publishing. The output files are created only for the active sheet. No connected drawings are
considered for publishing.

•  For Datasheets:

•  If you want to publish a datasheet, click the Datasheets tab and do one of the following:

•  Open the required datasheet. On the Manage tab, in the Publish group, click Active Datasheet. The

datasheet is added to the Datasheets tab. The sheet that is currently open is considered for
publishing.

•  Open the required datasheet. Select Active datasheet from the list on the Datasheets tab in the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 76

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Gateway window. Click the Add icon. The datasheet is added to the Datasheets tab. The sheet that is
currently open is considered for publishing.

Note: You can use the Remove and Remove all icons to remove the selected datasheets.

•  For Reports:

•  If you want to publish a report, click the Reports tab and do the following:

•  Select a report from the Please select an item list. This list contains Product, Project and User
options and resembles the available reports in the Home > Report > Run Reports functionality.

Note: If you want add an additional location to the list, click the folder icon and select the folder of
your choice. The location is added to the list. You can use the Remove and Remove all icons to
remove the selected reports.

After you have selected the required data to be published, you can select the options for publishing.

The Publish options area contains options for processing grids, DB drawings, drawings and datasheets. The
following options are available:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 77

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

•  Related datasheets: Selected by default, this check box enables you to include all the datasheets in which an
object selected for publishing is available. If you do not want to include the related datasheets, clear this
check box.

•  Related drawings: Selected by default, this check box enables you to include all the drawings in which an

object selected for publishing is available. If you do not want to include the related drawings, clear this check
box.

•  Related grids: Selected by default, this check box enables you to include all the object in the grids in which

the object selected for publishing is available. If you do not want to include the related grids, clear this check
box.

•  Grid columns: This option enables to you specify the grid columns you want to include in the EIWM (or

JSON) file. If you want to include all the available grid columns, select the All option. If you want to include
only the columns that are currently visible, select the Only visible option.

•  Connected drawings: This check box enables you to include the drawings that are connected to the current
drawing for publishing. This check box is cleared by default. If you want to include the connected drawings,
select this check box.

Note: The options for Datasheet processing are currently not available.

The Configuration selection area lists all the configurations available. Select the Selected check box
corresponding to the configurations you want to use. You can select more than one configuration. However, you
must make sure that the combination of the configurations is meaningful to the user. Also, to avoid the
overwriting of files, you must make sure that the staging areas within the configurations are not the same.

A tooltip provides the general information about the configuration you have currently selected. Verify the
information for all the selected configurations.

After you have selected all the required options, click Publish to publish the selected data. The data is published
and the output files are generated at the locations mentioned as staging areas.

Note: If you are publishing the data for AVEVA Ingestion Service, on clicking Publish, you will be prompted for
your CONNECT credentials. The data is published only after the credentials are validated.

The Log view area displays the status of the publish operation. After the publish process is complete, a summary
report is generated for each selected configuration and is placed in the same location as the log file. The name of
the summary file is in the following format: Summary - <Configuration Name>. You can also view the
summary report by clicking summary file..

Note: If you do not specify a location for the log file, then the summary file is not generated.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 78

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

Publishing Data from Command Line

Grids, DB Views, drawings, datasheets and reports can be published from the command line.

Note: Starting version 1.4.2 of the gateway, publishing of datasheets from the command line is supported from
AVEVA Engineering 15.7.1 to 15.7.3 onwards and publishing of drawings is supported from AVEVA Engineering
15.4 onwards.

For this, you must first create a token using AVEVA™ Administration. The token must contain the values for
username, password and MDB. Then, the token path must be referred to in the command. For more information
about tokens and how to create them, see the AVEVA Administration User Guide or see Generate Secure Login.

Note: You must ensure that the token contains the values for username, password and MDB. An error message is
displayed if the token does not contain the values for these fields.

If you are using AVEVA's Licensing as a Service (LaaS) to license your product, you must export the data through
the user interface at least once before exporting from the command line. When you export the data through the
user interface using AVEVA's LaaS, you are prompted to provide your credentials. After the credentials are
validated, you can export the data through the command line.

To export the data from the command line, you must either change to the installation folder of AVEVA™
Engineering using the cd command, or provide the full path of the installation location, and then run the
following command:

AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Engineering.exe

An example command is as follows:

C:\AVEVA\Engineering15.3\AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Engineering.exe -proj AFP -TokenPath "C:\AVEVA\
Token.AFP.mac" -SelectedElements " =16392/2958 =24584/1259 =16392/4050" -CfgNames
"DefaultConfiguration" -EIWMFileSuffix "null" -EIWMContext "SRP+VPD "
-GenerateFolderVNETFIle True -GenerateTriggerStartFile True

Unified Engineering :

ue -t:Engineeringgateway -args:-TokenPath "<C:\Users\XXX\Desktop\model Gateway output\New
folder\token path\projectentry.mac>"  -SelectedElements "=23997/1377" -CfgNames "A"
-EIWMContext "avngate" -EIWMFileSuffix "avngate" -ItemProcessGrids "false"
-ItemProcessDatasheets "false" -AutoCfgUpgrade "True" -GenerateTriggerStartFile "true"
 -GenerateFolderVNETFile "True"

Note: Any errors about the export are added to the log file. The location of the log file is displayed on the screen.
You can cancel the command line export using CTRL+C.

The following is an example of the contents of a macro file.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

handle any

endhandle

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!publisher = object IEDPublisher()

--(APS) Centrifugal Pumps

!publisher.SelectElement('=23997/1366')

--(APS) Filters

!publisher.SelectElement('=23997/1330')

--(APS)P1501A_CENTRIPUMP_DST

!publisher.SelectElement('=23997/988')

--(APS)P1501B_CENTRIPUMP_DST

!publisher.SelectElement('=23997/989')

!publisher.SelectConfiguration('T1')

!publisher.SetOption('itemProcessDatasheets', false)

!publisher.SetOption('itemProcessGrids', true)

!publisher.SetOption('itemProcessDrawings', false)

!publisher.SetOption('drawingProcessConnectedDrawings', false)

!publisher.SelectDbView('CENTRIFUGALPUMP_View')

--(APS project) Selects DbView using it's unique name

!publisher.Publish()

!errorsNo = !publisher.ErrorsNo()

Engineering

Security Guidelines

Because the gateway produces data into file system or network folders specified by the user for loading into
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management or AVEVA Ingestion Service, it needs to read, write and modify both files
and folders.

The following are the security recommendations for users using the gateway :

•  Use the principle of least privilege

•  For AVEVA™ Asset Information Management: Grant only Read access to the user account that is used to
run the gateway for all file and folder locations. Change the access to Read/Write only to the specific
files and folders the gateway needs to modify. For example, you can grant Read/Write access to the
Staging Area and Logs folders.

•  For AVEVA Ingestion Service: Grant Read/Write access to target folder (Asset Id) for the specific AVEVA

Connect account user.

•  You do not need to adjust your Firewall settings or User Account Control settings when you install or use the

Gateway.

•  The gateway uses the host product's project defaults location (%APSDFLTS%\IEDGateway) for storing various
configurations and settings. Only project administrators must have Write access to this folder. Other users
must be given Read-only access.

Note: If the above security recommendations are not suitable for your environment, you must investigate
what is the most suitable approach for your environment and apply those practices.

View Data in AVEVA Asset Information Management (AIM)

After an element is exported, the output files are generated at the staging area:

If you have added the staging area as a watch folder in the Import Controller of AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management, the data is automatically imported in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. Else, you must
manually import the data using the Import Controller. For more information about the Import Controller, refer to
the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

After the files have been imported, AVEVA™ Asset Information Management displays the AVEVA™ Engineering
data when queried.

Note: Contact your AVEVA™ Asset Information Management administrator if you are unable to view some of the
data in the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Appendix: Troubleshooting Errors While Publishing Data

Error While Publishing Data

Sometimes, while publishing large amounts of data, the publish operation may fail with the
'System.OutOfMemoryException' error. This error occurs because of insufficient memory to complete the
operation. To resolve this error, try to republish using only selected rows, selected columns or use the filtering
criteria to publish the data in smaller chunks.

Performance Issues While Exporting Data

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 85

AVEVA™ Gateways
Engineering

By default, each export configuration is created with the autoflush option for logging set to true. This enforces
every log message to be stored instantly in the target location without waiting for log buffer to be filled. That
could cause some performance issues on high latency network drives (i.e. Cloud UE solution) when log level is
set to Info or verbose or publishing process is producing many warnings or errors.

To resolve this problem, the configuration administrator could manually edit the configuration files and set
autoFlush attribute to false, in which case the logs are buffered in the application memory and are then
flushed to the log file when buffer size limit (by default 1024 bytes) is reached. Increasing the bufferSize
attribute value (recommended value in such cases is 1048576, which is 1MB of memory ) could also be helpful in
increasing the performance when large amount of information is logged.

The downside of modifying these options is the risk of losing critical error information in the output log file.

Example location for Export common configuration

D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\
ExportCommonCfg.xml

Example location of export configuration:

 D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\E3D\3.1.0\
Integrity\Integrity.xml

You can generate a secure login (token) using AVEVA Administration:

To generate a secure login:

1.  Open AVEVA Administration.

2.  Make sure you are in the required project.

3.  Select Users and then select Create Script. Refer to the following image for specific locations.

is available at the location specified.

Diagrams

This guide provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering for AVEVA™ Diagrams (gateway),
and describes how to use the gateway to create configurations and export the data from AVEVA™ Diagrams to
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Preface

This chapter provides an introduction to this guide, specifies the intended audience, provides the summary of
chapters and lists the related documents for the guide. The chapter also provides information about product
training and how to contact technical support.

About This Guide

This guide provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering for AVEVA™ Diagrams (gateway),
and describes how to use the gateway to create configurations and export the data from AVEVA™ Diagrams to
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Audience

This guide is intended for users of AVEVA™ Diagrams who are responsible for installing and using the gateway.
The guide assumes that you have the functional and technical knowledge of the following:

•  AVEVA™ Diagrams

•  AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

Product Training Information

Technical Support

Introduction

This chapter provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering for AVEVA™ Diagrams
(gateway).

About AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering

The gateway is an add-in to AVEVA™ Diagrams 14.1.3 and later that enables you to publish the data and drawings
from AVEVA™ Diagrams to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. For this, you must first define a
configuration that specifies how the data must be published. After the configuration is defined, you can publish
an element and its associated drawing to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management using the configuration. The
gateway processes and converts data from AVEVA™ Diagrams into a compatible file format that can be loaded
into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Using the Extract, Transform and Load (ETL) process, the gateway extracts the required information from
AVEVA™ Diagrams, applies the specified mappings and filters and displays it in AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management in a user-friendly format. The gateway achieves this by creating an XML file called the Engineering
Information and Workflow Model (EIWM) file that contains the information related to the elements that are
exported. The gateway also creates an SVG file if you choose to generate graphical data for the exported items.
The EIWM and SVG files are then imported into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management using the Import
Controller, and the data is displayed in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Compatibility with AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Gateway

The AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) is an improved version of the AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management Gateway (old gateway) that was previously available with AVEVA™ Diagrams. The old gateway is no
longer available. However, the gateway enables you to use the files generated by old gateway.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 90

The files can be used importing class and attribute mappings and presentation mappings. See the following
sections for more information:

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

•  Import Mappings from an Existing File

•  Specify Presentation Settings

Accessing the Gateway

After installing the gateway, start AVEVA™ Diagrams.The AVEVA AIM Export Setup icon is now available in the
Admin tab. You can use this icon to create configurations based on which the data (and the drawings) can be
published to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Click AVEVA AIM Export Setup. The AVEVA AIM Export Setup window appears and is docked to the right-hand
side of the AVEVA™ Diagrams window.The following image describes the user interface of the AVEVA AIM Export
Setup window.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 91

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

About Configurations

A configuration contains information about how you want to publish your data to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. Primarily, it contains the element types and their attributes that need to be exported. You can also
specify the following information:

•  Filtering criteria, if any

•  Mapping for the classes of the element types in AVEVA™ Diagrams to their corresponding classes in AVEVA™

Asset Information Management

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 92

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

•  Information such as context, characteristics, properties and associations for the elements to be exported

•  Presentation settings such as colour and font mapping, hotspotting for the drawings and so on

•  Exports options such as the location of the staging area, context configuration and logging

The configuration is created or updated when you click Save on the AVEVA AIM Export Setup window. A
configuration may be used for any number of exports. You can also modify and delete the configuration
according to your needs.

The gateway comes with a sample configuration called DefaultConfiguration. This configuration cannot be
deleted. However, you can modify it according to your needs. For information on how to modify a configuration,
see Modify a Configuration.

Important: The gateway does not validate the information you have provided while creating the configuration.
Therefore, while creating the configuration, you must make sure that all values such as the filtering criteria,
mappings, associations, PML expressions, IF conditions and so on are valid and meaningful to the AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management user. Else, data may not be exported correctly or understood in the desired manner.

Create a Configuration

This section describes the high-level process to create a configuration.

To create a configuration:

1.  On the Element Selection and Filters page of the AVEVA AIM Export Setup window, click the Add (+) icon to

create a new configuration.

Note: Alternatively, you can load one of the sample configurations and then modify it according to your
needs. For information on how to load a configuration, see Load a Configuration and for information on how
to modify a configuration, see Modify a Configuration.

2.  Select the required elements and apply the required filters. Click the Next icon to go to the next page. For

information on how to select elements, see Select Elements, and for information on how to apply filters, see
Apply Filters.

3.  On the Element Type Mappings page, provide the mappings for the EIWM and SVG files, and then click the
Next icon. For information on how to provide the mappings for the EIWM and SVG files, see Mapping
Element Types.

4.  On the Presentation Settings page, provide the presentation settings, and then click the Next icon. For

information on how to provide presentation settings, see Specify Presentation Settings.

5.  On the Export Options page, select the export options for the configuration and click Save. For more

information on the export options, see Setting Export Options.

6.  On the Save Configuration dialog box, type a name for the configuration in the Name box and then click OK.

The configuration is created.

Note: The configuration name cannot contain white spaces and special characters.

Load a Configuration

To load a configuration:

•  Select the required configuration from the Load Configuration list. The configuration is loaded into the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 93

AVEVA AIM Export Setup window.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Note: After a loading a configuration, click the Delete icon to delete the currently loaded configuration. The
DefaultConfiguration cannot be deleted.

Select Elements

You can select the element types through the Select Elements area of the Element Selection and Filters page in
the AVEVA AIM Export Setup window. The element types you select here are included in the EIWM file and are
tagged in the SVG file. To select the child element types, you must drill down by clicking the arrow adjacent to
the check box.

The following image shows the user interface of the Select Elements area.

To select the element types:

1.

In the Select Elements area, click the arrow adjacent to the element types to drill down, and then select the
check boxes corresponding to the element types you want to export to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management.

Note: The Document Page element type always is selected and cannot be cleared.

2.  Through the Generate Graphical Data check box, you can specify if you want to export the graphical data. If

you do not want to export the graphical data, select the Document Page element type and clear the
Generate Graphical Data check box. The suffix [SVG] is removed from the element type indicating that no
graphical data will be generated during export. By default, the check box is selected and the suffix [SVG] is
added to the element type indicating that an SVG file will be generated during export.

Note: The Generate Graphical Data check box is available only for the Document Page element type. To
select or clear the check box, the Document Page element type must be highlighted in the Select Elements
area.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 94

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Element Selection Strategies

Due to the complex nature of AVEVA™ Diagrams and the associated project data, it is advised to create the
configurations so as to ensure that the publishing performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering
remains optimal.

Elements in AVEVA™ Diagrams could often have large number of sub element under a parent element. For such
projects, publishing the graphical data of the topmost parent as a single model (single SVG file) can result in
memory-related issues and sub optimal performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering. Therefore,
it is advised to create configurations in such ways that generated SVG drawings would be split into multiple files
of smaller sizes.

Dividing drawings into smaller parts for publishing is also beneficial in case of future model updates. Consider
the case where a large SITE has been published as one model to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management and at
a later point in time, it must be updated due to changes in source model. In such cases, the entire SITE need not
be published once again if the graphical data has been split into multiple files.

Apply Filters

After selecting element types, you can apply filters to narrow down the results. Filters can be applied on
attributes or statuses. You can also provide a filter through a PML expression.

The following image shows the user interface of the Apply Filters area.

Note: The element type for which a filter is applied is highlighted in red in the Select Elements area.

Attribute Filter

To add an attribute filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Attribute Filter section.

2.  Highlight the element type in the Select Elements area. The available attributes of the element type are

displayed in the Attribute list.

3.  Select an attribute from the Attribute list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 95

7.  Repeat steps 2 to 6 to add more attribute filters.

The following image shows the interface of the Attribute Filter section.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Status Filter

To add a status filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Status Filter section.

2.  Highlight the element type in the Select Elements area. The available statuses for the element type are

displayed in the Status Definition list.

3.  Select a status from the Status Definition list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Status Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

7.  Repeat steps 2 to 6 to add more status filters on other element types.

The following image shows the interface of the Status Filter section.

PML Expression Filter

To add a filter through a PML expression:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the PML Expression Filter section.

2.  Highlight the element type in the Select Elements area.

3.  Type an expression in the box.

4.  Click Add filter. The filter is added.

5.  Repeat steps 2 to 4 to add more filters using PML expressions for other element types.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 96

The following image shows the interface of the PML Expression Filter section.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Mapping Element Types

On the Element Type Mappings page, you can define the mappings for various attributes of the element types
such as Object ID, Class ID and Name. The mappings specify how the attributes of an element type must map to
the attributes of AVEVA™ Asset Information Management after they are imported into AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management. All element types come with certain default mappings as mentioned in the Default
Mappings section. You can add to, remove or modify the mappings based on your need.

This section describes the procedures to perform the following tasks.

You can perform the following tasks on this page:

•  Import Mappings from an Existing File

•  Map Object ID

•  Map Class ID

•  Add Attributes to Element Type

•  Add Conditions for Attributes

•  Add Unmapped Attributes

•  Tag Element Types in SVG Files

Note: The mappings defined for the Document Page element type cannot be modified. Also you cannot add,
modify or delete the attributes for this element type.

Import Mappings from an Existing File

Using the Import feature on the Element Type Mappings page, you can import the class and attribute mappings
generated by the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Gateway (old gateway). When you import the
mappings, the class and attribute mappings defined in the respective files are imported for all selected element
types. For more information about the compatibility with the old gateway, see Compatibility with AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management Gateway.

Note: The mappings imported from an existing file are overridden by mappings specified in the EIWM Object
Mapping area of the Element Type Mappings page. For example, if you have already defined a mapping for the
Name attribute before importing the mapping, the mapping mentioned by you is retained.

The following image shows the user interface for importing mappings from an existing file.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 97

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

To import mappings from an existing file:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, click Import, and select From ClassMapping.xml or From
AttributeMapping.xml based on whether you want to import class or attribute mappings.

2.  Browse to the desired location, select the required file, and click Open.

3.  After the import is completed, a message that the import has been completed successfully is displayed. Click

OK.

Map Object ID

All element types have a default mapping for the Object ID. For information on the list of default mappings, see
Default Mappings.

To map the object ID of the element type:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.
The default mappings for the element type are displayed in the EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM
tab.

2.  Select the root of the object tree Object. The Object ID area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the Object ID directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 98

area.

Important: The mapping will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you
move to the next page, make sure that all the element types you have selected have the required
mappings. The objects without a mapping are displayed in red.

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Map Class ID

All element types have a default mapping for the Class ID. For information on the list of default mappings, see
Default Mappings.

To map the class ID of the element type:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.
The default mappings for the element type are displayed in the EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM
tab.

2.  Select ClassID in the object tree. The ClassID area appears.

Note: If the ClassID attribute is not present in the object tree, you can add the attribute by selecting
Object, right-clicking and selecting Add EIWM > ClassID.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the class ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the class ID is also
deleted.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Important: The mapping will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 99

move to the next page, make sure that all the element types you have selected have the required
mappings. The attributes without a mapping are displayed in red.

Add Attributes to Element Type

You can add the following attributes to the element types that have been selected for export:

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

•  Name

•  Characteristic

•  Property

•  Association

The following image shows the user interface for adding attributes.

Adding the Name Attribute

Each element type can have only one Name attribute.

To add a Name attribute to an element type:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add EIWM > Name. The

Name attribute is added to the object tree and the Name area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Name attribute directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 100

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from a PML expression, select the From PML

option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Important: The value you provide will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before
you move to the next page, make sure that all the attributes you have selected have the required
mappings. The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red.

Adding a Characteristic Attribute

You can add any number of Characteristic attributes to an element type.

To add a Characteristic attribute:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add EIWM >

Characteristic. The Characteristic attribute is added to the object tree and the Name and Value areas
appear.

3.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the characteristic name directly, select the Constant option and enter
a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name field of the Characteristic attribute
in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic name from the value of an attribute, select the

From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the
Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic name from a PML expression, select the From PML
option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

4.  For the Value area, do one of the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 101

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

•  If you want to specify the value of the characteristic directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Value field of the Characteristic attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from the value of an attribute, select the From

Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Value
field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from a PML expression, select the From PML option
and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Value field
of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Important: The value you provide will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you
move to the next page, make sure that all the attributes you have selected have the required value or mapping.
The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red.

Adding a Property Attribute

You can add any number of Property attributes to an element type.

To add a Property attribute:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add EIWM > Property. The

Property attribute is added to the object tree and the Name, Value and Units areas appear.

3.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the property name directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name field of the Property attribute in the
EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object

tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property name from the value of an attribute, select the From

field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property name from a PML expression, select the From PML
option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Name field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

4.  For the Value area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value of property directly, select the Constant option and enter a value in the
Text box. The specified value is added to the Value field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute
option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Value field of the
Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property from a PML expression, select the From PML option and
enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Value field of the
Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the Units area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the property units directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Units field of the Property attribute in the
EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property units from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Units
field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property units from a PML expression, select the From PML

option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Units
field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Important: The value you provide will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you

Adding an Association Attribute

You can add any number of Association attributes to an element type.

To add an Association attribute:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add EIWM > Association.

The Association attribute is added to the object tree and the Type and SourceID areas appear.

3.  For the Type area, do one of the following:
