Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the association type from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute

option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the Type field of
the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the association type from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter

a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Type field of the
Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

4.  For the SourceID area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the source ID of the association directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the SourceID field of the Association attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute
option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the SourceID field
of the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and
enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the SourceID field of
the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Important: The value you provide will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you
move to the next page, make sure that all the attributes you have selected have the required value or mapping.
The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red.

Add Conditions for Attributes

If you want to specify any conditions based on which the attributes must be exported, you can do so by adding
an IF condition to the object tree.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 104

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

To add an IF condition:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add Other > If Condition.

The IF condition is added to the tree and the area to add the condition appears.

3.  Click the Add icon. The PML Condition Expression box appears.

4.  Type the condition in the box. An example condition is as follows: NAME eq 'TRA001-RECT'

5.  After providing the condition, to specify what needs to be done when the condition is met, add a member to
the If statement. To do this, select If, right-click and select Add EIWM, and then select the attribute that
you want to add. For information on how to add attributes, see Add Attributes to Element Type.

6.

If you want to specify what needs to be done when the condition is not met, you must add an Else member
to If. To do this, select If, right-click and select Add Other > Else.

7.  After adding the Else member, select Else, right-click and select Add EIWM, and then select the attribute

that you want to add. An example IF condition is as follows:

Note: You can also add nested conditions using IF-ELSE IF and IF-IF-ELSE IF and so on.

Add Unmapped Attributes

If you want to export all the attributes of an element type but have mapped only a few of them, you can use the
All not mapped attributes option to export all the attributes that have not been explicitly mapped. When you
use this option, the gateway loads all the attributes that are currently defined for the element type into the
EIWM Object Mapping area and considers them for export.

To include unmapped attributes:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the EIWM tab, select the root of the object tree Object, right-click and select Add Other > All not

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 105

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

mapped attributes. The list of all unmapped attributes is loaded.

You can review the list of attributes and modify or delete the mappings based on your need.

Tag Element Types in SVG Files

You can specify how the tag ID of an element type must appear in the SVG file through the SVG tab in the
Element Type Mappings page. The tag ID for the element type is retrieved from the mapping specified here and
the same is displayed on the SVG file when the element type is selected.

Note: Tagging is supported only for those element types that can be selected in the SVG file. Accordingly, the
settings in the SVG tab are applicable to only those element types.

To map the tag ID of an element type:

1.  On the Element Type Mappings page, select the required element type from the Current Element Type list.

2.  On the SVG tab, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the tag ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is used as the tag ID of the element type.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the tag ID is also
deleted.

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option and

select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute value is used as the tag ID for the element
type.

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a PML

expression in the PML Expression box. The result of the PML expression is is used as the tag ID for the
element type.

Important: The mapping will be lost if you move from one option to another. Therefore, before you
move to the next page, make sure that all the element types you have selected have the required
mapping for tag ID. The tag IDs for the element types without a mapping are not displayed in the SVG
file.

Specify Presentation Settings

Through the Presentation Settings page, you can specify the settings for the SVG file. You can also import the
settings from an XML file created using the old gateway. For more information about the compatibility with the
old gateway, see Compatibility with AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Gateway.

check box is cleared by default.

b.  For the Line thickness scale factor box, specify the thickness for the lines in the SVG file. The default

value is 1.

c.  For the Background Color box, click Pick Color and then choose a color for the background.

Note: You can add custom colors to the color palette. Use the Define Custom Colors button available on the
Colors dialog box to define custom colors.

In the Color Mapping area, do the following based on your need:

3.

b.  For the From column, click the Brush icon to pick a color that needs to be mapped.

4.

In the Font Mapping area, do the following based on your need:

Setting Export Options

On the Export Options page, you can specify the export options for exporting the EIWM and SVG files.

To specify the export options:

1.  For the Staging Area box, click the Browse icon and select a location for the staging area. This location will be

used for storing the EIWM and SVG files after they are exported by the gateway.

2.

In the Logging area, do the following:

a.  For the Location box, specify a location for the log files using the Browse icon.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 109

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Note: If you do not specify a location or have specified an incorrect location for the log file, no log file
will be generated.

b.  For the Level area, select the level of logging needed. The following levels of logging are available:

a.  Error: If you choose this option, only error messages are logged to the file.

b.  Warning: If you choose this option, error and warning messages are logged to the file. This option is

selected by default.

c.  Information: If you choose this option, error, warning and information messages are logged to the

file.

d.  Verbose: If you choose this option, error, warning, information and verbose messages are logged to

the file.

Setting Common Export Options

Through the Export Options page, you can specify the common export options that will be applicable to all
exports irrespective of the configuration selected. The options available in the Common export configuration
settings area are not specific to any configuration and are applicable to all export operations. The gateway uses
the export options specified in the area during the export.

To specify the common export options, do the following in the Common export configuration settings area
based on your need:

•  In the CSV area, select the Generate CSV File check box if you want to generate a CSV file along with the
EIWM and SVG files. If you select this check box, a .csv file that contains the same information as as the
EIWM file is generated and placed in the staging area. This check box is cleared by default.

•  In the Context Configuration area, enter a context name in the Context Name box. You can use the context
name to specify a context for the exported data. You can provide a nested context by providing the highest
context first followed by a "+" or "|"symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or
ROOT|PARENT|CHILD). By default, the context is PROJECTCONTEXT.

•  For the File Suffix box, enter a file suffix. Based on the file suffix you provide, AVEVA™ Asset Information

Management picks up the relevant XSLT file to process the exported EIWM. However, before you choose this
option, make sure that the relevant XSLT file is already present with AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. By default, the file suffix is _null which means that AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
will process the EIWM file without any XSLT. For more information about XSLT files, refer to the AVEVA™

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 110

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

•  Select the Generate folder.vnet file check box if you want a folder.vnet file to be generated. The folder.vnet
file contains information about the class in which the generated SVG file must be placed and the context in
which the object must be saved. By default, this check box is cleared.

•  Select the Generate trigger.start file check box if the output files (EIWM and SVG files) of the exported

elements must be picked automatically by the Import Controller for processing in AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. By default, the check box is cleared. If you select the check box, make sure that the staging
area of the output files is listed as a watch folder in the Import Controller. For more information about the
Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation
Guide.

Online documentation for Diagrams can be accessed from the Gateway Setup.

Save the Configuration

The Save configuration dialog box appears if you are creating a new configuration and have clicked Save.

Enter a name for the configuration in the Name box and click OK.

Modify a Configuration

After creating and saving a configuration, you can modify the configuration if the need arises.

To modify the configuration:

1.  Load the configuration you want to modify. For information on how to load a configuration, see Load a

Configuration.

2.  After the configuration is loaded, perform one or more of the following procedures depending on what

needs to be modified.

Note: To delete a configuration, select the configuration in the Load Configuration list and then click the
Delete icon.

3.  After you make the required changes based on your need, click Save to save the configuration. The

configuration is now modified.

Modify Element Selection

To modify the selection of elements:

•  In the Select Elements area of the Element Selection and Filters page, select the check boxes for the

element types you want to export and clear the check boxes for the element types that you do not want to
export. For more information about selecting elements, see Select Elements.

Modify Filters

For information about how to apply new filters, see Apply Filters.

To modify existing filters:

1.

In the Select Elements area, highlight the element type for which the filter needs to be modified. The list of
filters applicable to the element type are displayed.

2.  Select the row of the filter which needs to be modified, and then click the Edit Filter icon. The filter is loaded

into the Attribute Filter, Status Filter or PML Expression Filter area as the case may be.

3.  Modify the filter based on your need and then click Update Filter. The updated filter is displayed in the list of

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 113

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

filters.

Note: To delete a filter, highlight the corresponding row and then click the Delete Filter icon.

Modify Element Type Mappings

To modify the element type mappings, do the following based on your need:

1.

If you want to modify the mapping for the Object ID, see Map Object ID.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the Object ID, select the root of the tree Object, right-click and
select Remove > This. The mapping for the Object ID along with all its members is deleted. However,
remember that an element type without an Object ID is not exported. If you want to delete the mappings
only for the members, select the root of the tree Object ID, right-click and select Remove > All members.

2.

If you want to modify the mapping for the Class ID, see Map Class ID .

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the Class ID, select ClassID in the tree, right-click and select
Remove. However, remember that an element type without a Class ID is exported as UNKNOWN.

3.

If you want to modify the attributes for the element type, select the attribute in the tree and modify it
according to your need. For more information, see Add Attributes to Element Type.

Note: If you want to delete an attribute, select the attribute in the tree, right-click and select Remove.
However, remember that an attribute that is not mapped is not exported.

4.

If you want to modify the conditions for the attributes, select the condition in the tree and modify it
according to your need. For more information, see Add Conditions for Attributes.

Note: If you want to delete a condition, select the condition in the tree, right-click and select Remove > This.
The condition along with all its members is deleted. If you want to delete only the members of the condition,
select the condition in the tree, right-click and select Remove > All members.

5.

If you want to modify the tagging for the element type, see Tag Element Types in SVG Files.

Modify Presentation Settings

To modify presentation settings:

•  On the Presentation Settings page, add, modify or delete settings as needed. For more information, see Tag

Element Types in SVG Files.

Modify Export Options

To modify the export options:

•  On the Export Options page, modify the export options based on your need. For more information, see

Setting Export Options.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 114

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Modify Common Export Options

To modify the common options:

•  On the Export Options page, modify the common export options based on your need. For more information,

see Setting Common Export Options.

Note: Remember that the common export options that are specified in the Common export configuration
settings area at the time of performing the export are used by the gateway for performing the export.

Publish Data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

You can publish the data from AVEVA™ Diagrams to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management through the user
interface and through the command line.

This section describes the procedure to publish the data using the user interface. For information on how publish
the data through the command line, see Publish Data from Command Line.

The following points must be noted about the output files (EIWM and SVG):

•  The actual output for an export depends both on the classes selected in the configuration and the elements

selected for publishing. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a SITE element for publishing, all the
PIPE elements present in the SITE are exported. In this case, the number of EIWM files generated is
equal to the number of PIPEs in the SITE.

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for publishing, then only

the PIPE element is exported.

•  If you have selected SITE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for the publishing, there

will not be any output.

•  Similarly, the number of SVG files generated for an export also depends on your selection in the

configuration. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you are publishing an EQUIP element and have chosen to generate an SVG file for it, then an EIWM file

and an SVG file for the equipment are generated.

•  If you are publishing a SITE element and have chosen to generate an SVG file at the EQUIP level, then an

EIWM and an SVG for each EQUIP element present in the SITE is generated.

To publish data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management:

1.  Do one of the following:

•  To publish a single element: On the Project Explorer, locate the element you want to publish, right-click

and select Publish to AVEVA AIM.

•  To publish multiple elements: On the Tools tab, select Batch Job. On the Batch Job window, select the

check boxes for the elements you want to publish and click Publish to AVEVA AIM.

•  To publish all elements of a drawing: Open the drawing for the element you want to publish, right-click

anywhere on the drawing and select Publish to AVEVA AIM.

2.  On the Select configuration dialog box, select the configurations using which the data element needs to be

published.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 115

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Note: The Select configuration dialog box displays the list of all available configurations. You can select more
than one configuration for publishing data. However, you must make sure that the combination is valid and
meaningful for the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management user. The configuration or configurations used
for the last export is selected by default.

3.  Click Publish. The Processing export configurations dialog box displays the status of the export process.

4.  After the status is changed to Finished, you can verify the output files at the staging area location and the log

files at the location specified by you.

Note: If the staging area location is listed as a watch folder in Import Controller and the option to generate
the trigger.start file is selected, then the output files are automatically imported to AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management. Thereafter, the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management users can query and view
the data. For more information about the Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 116

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

Publish Data from Command Line

Data can also be published from the command line. For this, you must first create a token using AVEVA™
Administration. The token must contain the values for username, password and MDB . Then the token path must
be referred to in the command. For more information about tokens and how to create them, see the AVEVA
Administration User Guide or see Generate Secure Login.

Note: You must ensure that the token contains the values for username, password and MDB. An error message is
displayed if the token does not contain the values for these fields.

If you are using AVEVA's Licensing as a Service (LaaS) to license the gateway, you must export the data through
the user interface at least once before exporting from the command line. When you export the data through the
user interface using AVEVA's LaaS, you are prompted to provide your CONNECT credentials. After the credentials
are validated, you can export the data through the command line.

To export the AVEVA™ Diagrams data using the command line, you must first change to the installation folder of
AVEVA™ Diagrams using the cd command and then run the following command:
AVEVA.UE.Gateway.Diagrams.exe

The following parameters must be provided with the command:

Note: For more information about the parameters, see Setting Common Export Options.

-Proj

-SelectedElements

-CfgNames

-EIWMContext

-EIWMFileSuffix

-GenerateCSVFile

An example command is as follows:

AVEVA.UE.Gateway.Diagrams.exe -TokenPath "C:\Users\XYZ\Desktop\Login Token\
projectentry.mac" -Proj "APS" -SelectedElements "/HVAC_Diagram" -CfgNames
"DefaultConfiguration" -EIWMContext "SRP+VPD" -EIWMFileSuffix "null" -GenerateCSVFile False
-GenerateFolderVNETFIle True -GenerateTriggerStartFile True

Security Guidelines

Because the gateway produces data into file system or network folders specified by the user for loading into
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard, it needs to read, write and modify both files
and folders.

The following are the security recommendations for users using the gateway :

•  Use the principle of least privilege: Grant only Read access to the user account that is used to run the
gateway for all file and folder locations. Change the access to Read/Write only to the specific files and
folders the gateway needs to modify. For example, you can grant Read/Write access to the Staging Area and
Logs folders.

•  You do not need to adjust your Firewall settings or User Account Control settings when you install or use the

Gateway.

•  The gateway uses the host product's project defaults location (%APSDFLTS%\IEDGateway) for storing various
configurations and settings. Only project administrators must have Write access to this folder. Other users
must be given Read-only access.

Note: If the above security recommendations are not suitable for your environment, you must investigate
what is the most suitable approach for your environment and apply those practices

Generate Secure Login

You can generate a secure login (token) using AVEVA Administration:

To generate a secure login:

1.  Open AVEVA Administration.

2.  Make sure you are in the required project.

3.  Select Users and then select Create Script. Refer to the following image for specific locations.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 121

AVEVA™ Gateways
Diagrams

4.  On the Command Script Generation dialog box, enter the user name / password and MDB name from which
the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering user will be publishing information. Also enter output macro
file path where the generated token (secure login ) must be stored.

5.  After providing all the required information, select OK. The secure login is generated in the .mac format and

is available at the location specified.

After the secure login is generated, the user can use the file as -TokenPath argument:

For example:-TokenPath "D:\projectentry.mac"

The Command Script Generation dialog box can also be accessed using the Command Window tool from
AVEVA™ E3D Design or AVEVA™ Engineering using the following command:

show !!admscriptgenerate

E3D Design (Draw)

This guide provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering.

Preface

This chapter provides an introduction to this guide, specifies the intended audience, provides the summary of
chapters and lists the related documents for the guide. The chapter also provides information about product
training and how to contact technical support.

About This Guide

This guide provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering, and describes how to use the
gateway to create configurations and export the data from AVEVA™ E3D Design to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management.

Note: The information in this guide is specific to the Draw module of AVEVA™ E3D Design. For information about
how to use the gateway in the Model module, install the gateway for the module and then refer to the AVEVA
Gateway for Unified Engineering User Guide for AVEVA E3D Design 3.1 (Model) available at <installation
location of AVEVA E3D Design>\Gateways\Documentation. For information on how to install the gateway,
refer to AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering Installation Guide that is available with the installation media.

Audience

This guide is intended for users of AVEVA™ E3D Design who are responsible for installing and using the gateway.
The guide assumes that you have the functional and technical knowledge of the following:

•  AVEVA™ E3D Design

•  AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

Summary of Chapters

This guide is organized into the following chapters.

Chapter

using the gateway

Create a Configuration

Describes how to create and save configurations

Installation Guide that is available with the installation media.

Product Training Information

Technical Support

Introduction

This chapter provides an introduction to AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway).

About AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering

The gateway is an add-in to the Draw module of AVEVA™ E3D Design that enables you to publish the data,
drawings, and reports from the Draw module of AVEVA™ E3D Design to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. For this, you must first define a configuration that specifies how the data must be published. After
the configuration is defined, you can publish an element and its associated drawing and report, if any, to AVEVA™
Asset Information Management using the configuration. The gateway processes and converts data from AVEVA™
E3D Design (Draw) into a compatible file format that can be loaded into AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 125

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Note: The gateway can be used to publish the Draw data from the Draw module only.

Using the Extract, Transform and Load (ETL) process, the gateway extracts the required information from
AVEVA™ E3D Design, applies the specified mappings and filters and displays it in AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management in a user-friendly format. The gateway achieves this by creating an XML file called the Engineering
Information and Workflow Model (EIWM) file that contains the information related to the elements that are
exported. The gateway also creates an SVG file if you choose to generate graphical data for the exported items.
The EIWM and SVG files are then imported into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management using the Import
Controller, and the data is displayed in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

The gateway enables you to generate the output in various formats and for consumption by AVEVA Ingestion
Service. For information on the various output formats, see Select Output Format for Draw Data and Select
Output Format for Reports. For information on how to generate output for AVEVA Ingestion Service, see Provide
General Config Information.

Capabilities of AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering has the following capabilities:

•  As an administrator, AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to define which classes can be

selected for publishing. For information on how to define the classes for selection, see Enable Draw Classes
for Selection in the Configuration.

•  You can select the output format for data and reports. For information on the output formats supported and
how to select the required output format, see Select Output Format for Draw Data and Select Output Format
for Reports

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to publish the output for AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management or AVEVA™ Ingestion Service. For information on how to specify the output destination, see
Provide General Config Information.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to publish data using the user interface, command

line, and PML macros. For information on the alternative publishing methods, see Publish Data to AVEVA™
Asset Information Management.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to upgrade your configurations using user interface,
command line, and PML macros. For information on the alternative ways of upgrading configurations, see
Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering supports use of Regular Expressions for specifying filters and

mapping. For information on how to specify the filters and mappings using regular expressions, see Using
Regular Expressions for Filters and Mappings.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering recognizes locations specified using environment variables. For
information on how to specify locations using environment variables, see Use Environment Variables to
Specify Locations.

Compatibility with AVEVA Gateway

Starting version 1.6, the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) replaces both Draw and Reports
functionality of the AVEVA Gateway (former gateway) that was previously available with AVEVA™ E3D Design
(Draw).

The gateway enables you to use the presentation settings generated by the former gateway. For more

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 126

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

information on how to import the presentation settings, see the Importing Presentation Settings section in
Specify Presentation Settings.

Note: The gateway currently does not support colour indexes. Consequently, while importing presentation
settings from a file, the colour mappings specified using colour indexes will not be imported. As a workaround,
you can either modify the file to change the colour indexes to their respective colour names or RGB values, or
update the mapping manually using the gateway. For more information on how to specify the presentation
settings manually, see Specify Presentation Settings.

Accessing the Gateway

After installing the gateway, start AVEVA™ E3D Design with the Draw module. The Gateway Setup icon is now
available in the Asset Information Management group of the Admin tab. You can use this icon to create
configurations based on which data (and drawings) can be published to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Click Gateway Setup. The Gateway Setup window appears and is docked to the right-hand side of the AVEVA™
E3D Design window.The following image illustrates the Gateway Setup window.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 127

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Import Gateway Configuration

You can import Gateway configurations from the previous version of the host product.

To import a configuration:

1.  Click Gateway Setup,the following options are displayed:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 128

•  There are no existing configurations for current product version.

•  There are already existing configurations for other product versions.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

2.  Click Yes to navigate to Import from other product version view.

3.

Import menu displays two options:

•  Import from file

•  Import from other product versions

4.  Do one of the following:

•  If you want to import an existing zip file:

•  Choose Import from file option and select the zip file. For information on how to export the zip file,

see Export Configuration.

•  If you want to import configuration from other product versions:

•  Choose Import from other product versions option. The following pop-up window is displayed.

5.  From the Select module name drop-down list select the module name. List of the selected configuration

names are displayed.

Note: Naming rules are applicable to configuration names, that is:

•  Configuration name cannot already exist in current version.

•  Configuration name cannot be the same as other configuration on the list.

•  Configuration name cannot contain invalid characters.

If you come across any of the above listed scenarios, the tooltip of the configuration should be updated,
and the selected checkbox should be unchecked.

6.  Click Import. You are navigated to the Configuration Manager view and it displays the imported

configurations on the list.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 129

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Export Configuration

You can export the existing gateway configurations .

To export a configuration:

1.

In the Gateway Setup window, choose Export configurations to file icon.

2.  By default, all the existing configurations are selected. You can deselect any configuration if you do not want

to export.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 130

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

3.  Click Export and save the Zip file.

Before You Begin

Before you start using the gateway, you must complete the following tasks.

•  Specify AVEVA AIM Server Details

•  Enable Automapping and Choose the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Model

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 131

•  Enable Draw Classes for Selection in the Configuration

Specify AVEVA AIM Server Details

Before you start working with the gateway, you must specify which AVEVA AIM server you want to connect to.
You can do this using the Add Server functionality in the Admin tab.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

To specify the AVEVA AIM server details:

1.  On the Admin tab, click Add Server. The Dashboard Server Selection dialog box is displayed.

2.  Do one of the following:

•  If you already have one or more servers configured, select a server that you want to use for the gateway

in the Default Server list, and click Apply.

•  If you do not have a server configured, click the plus icon to add new server configuration, specify the
server name, its URL, and the portal ID in the Name, URL and PortalID boxes respectively, and click
Apply. Then select the server from the Default Server list, and then click Apply again.

The AVEVA AIM server details are now added.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 132

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Enable Automapping and Choose the AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management Model

The AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) provides an option to map classes and their attributes
automatically through the Perform automapping when adding classes check box in the Settings dialog box. The
check box enables you to specify if you want the class and attribute mappings to be done automatically and is
selected by default. Clear the check box if you do not want the mappings to be done automatically.

The gateway also enables you to specify the AVEVA AIM schema or ISM that can be used as a reference while
adding the mappings. In addition to automapping of classes, attributes and associations, the schema you specify
here is used to highlight the classes, attributes and associations of a class that are not present in the schema in
yellow. However, this is just a visual representation and does not affect the export process.

To choose an AVEVA AIM schema:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click Settings. The Settings dialog box is displayed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 133

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

2.  On the AVEVA AIM Model tab, make sure that the AVEVA AIM option is is selected. The Active AVEVA AIM

Server Information area with Active AIM Server details is displayed. Click Check Update to check for updates
or click OK.

Note: If you have not selected any active AVEVA AIM server, the AVEVA AIM Schema area is empty. Use the
Add Server functionality in the AVEVA AIM group of the Admin tab if you want to add a new server or
change the location of the server. The server you select as the default server in the Dashboard Server
Selection dialog box is set as the active server for the gateway. For more information about the Add Server
functionality and the Dashboard Server Selection dialog box, refer to the AVEVA™ E3D Design User Guide.

Alternatively, you can also specify the mappings in a .csv file and place it in the Tables folder. Click Tables

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 134

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Folder and select the file that contains the mappings. The mappings defined in the file are considered when
the !!ToMapped() and !!ToMappedSafe() PML functions are used for defining mappings.

!!ToMapped(STRING, STRING) – the first argument is the input value that you are mapping from; the second
argument is the table name (CSV file name) where the mappings are available. If this function fails to find the
mapped value in specified table, an error is logged to the log file.

!!ToMappedSafe(STRING, STRING, STRING) – This function works in a similar way except that you can define
a third argument value that can be used as the default value if the mapping is not found. In such cases, the
default value is used for mapping instead of logging an error.

The following image illustrates an example usage.

To choose ISM:

1.  On the Model schema tab, make sure that the ISM option is selected.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Note: Import from ISM feature will allow to import GEICL file. By default, global settings are changed to ISM
after importing the ISM file . It is recommended to choose the required Mapping Mode before exporting.

2.

In the Nomenclature area, perform the following actions:

a.  Click Import from ISM option and select a GEICL XML file that contains ISM class and attributes model.

b.  Select the Source nomenclature from the available dropdown menu.

c.  Select the Target nomenclature from the available dropdown menu that will be used to translate class

and attributes name from source nomenclature.

Note: Source and Target fields will be enabled and populates naming conventions only after ISM Model has
been imported.

a.  Expand ISM class and attributes model information to view the imported ISM details.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 136

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

3.

In the Project class mapping and Project attribute mapping area, perform the following actions:

a.  Select a Name source from the dropdown menu.

b.  Select an action to perform When not mapped.

Select a Logging severity of mapping process when no mapped class was found for project class.

To choose ISM:

1.  On the Model schema tab, make sure that the ISM option is selected.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 137

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

2.

In the Nomenclature area, perform the following actions:

b.  Select the Source nomenclature from the available dropdown menu.

c.  Select the Target nomenclature from the available dropdown menu that will be used to translate class

and attributes name from source nomenclature.

Note: Source and Target fields will be enabled and populates naming conventions only after ISM Model has

Enable Draw Classes for Selection in the Configuration

You can pre-define the classes that can be used in a configuration using the Classes Tree tab in the Settings
dialog box. The classes you select here are available for selection in the configuration which can then be
published.

To enable classes for selection in the configuration:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click Settings. The Settings dialog box is displayed.

2.  Click the Classes Tree tab. All the available classes for the Draw module are displayed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 139

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

3.  Select the classes that you want to be available for selection in the configuration.

Note: To select the sub classes, you must drill-down to the required class.

4.  After selecting all the required classes, click OK. The selected classes are now available for selection in the

configuration.

General Product Information

You can view Product Information from the About tab in the Settings dialog box.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 140

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Following information is available in the About tab:

Module: Gateway for Unified Engineering Module name is displayed.

Version: Currently installed Gateway for Unified Engineering version is displayed.

AVEVA Online Documentation: The link which redirects to the AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering
documentation.

Create a Configuration

A configuration contains information about how you want to publish your data. Primarily, it contains the element
types and their attributes that need to be exported. You can also specify the following information:

•  Filtering criteria, if any

•  Mapping for the classes of the element types in AVEVA™ E3D Design to their corresponding classes in

AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

•  Information such as context, characteristics, properties and associations for the elements to be exported

•  Presentation settings such as colour and font mapping, hotspotting for the drawings and so on

•  Exports options such as the location of the staging area, context configuration and logging

The configuration is created or updated when you click Save on the Gateway Setup window. A configuration may
be used for any number of exports. You can also modify and delete the configuration according to your needs.

The gateway comes with a sample configuration called DefaultConfiguration. This configuration cannot be
deleted. However, you can modify it according to your requirements. For information on how to modify a
configuration, see Modify a Configuration.

Important: The gateway does not validate the information you have provided while creating the configuration.
Therefore, while creating the configuration, you must make sure that all values such as the filtering criteria,
mappings, associations, PML expressions, IF conditions and so on are valid and meaningful to the AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management Dashboard (Asset Information Management) user. Else, data may not be exported
correctly or understood in the desired manner.

This section describes the high-level process to create a configuration.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 141

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

To create a configuration:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click the Add (+) icon to create a new configuration.

Note: Alternatively, you can load the sample configuration and then modify it according to your needs. For
information on how to load a configuration, see Load a Configuration and for information on how to modify
a configuration, see Modify a Configuration.

2.  On the General tab, provide a description for the configuration, select a logging level and provide a location
for the log file. Also, provide a staging area for the output files. For information about how to perform these
tasks, see Provide General Config Information.

3.  On the Classes tab, select the required classes and apply the required filters. For information on how to

select classes, see Select Classes, and for information on how to apply filters, see Apply Filters.

4.  On the Drawings tab, specify the mappings for the classes and graphical data. For information on how to
map items, see Map Element Types, and for information on how to map items for graphical data, see Tag
Classes in Graphical Data and for information on how to specify the presentation settings, see Specify
Presentation Settings.

5.  On the Reports tab, specify mappings for the classes in your reports, and select the output format for your
reports. For information on how to map classes for reports, see Map Items for Reports, and for information
on how to select the output format for reports, see Select Output Format for Reports.

6.  Click Save.

7.  On the Save Configuration dialog box, type a name for the configuration in the Name box and then click OK.

The configuration is created. For more information about saving the configuration, see Save the
Configuration.

Note: By default, the output is generated in the XML (EIWM) and SVG (if graphical data is being generated)
formats. If required, you can change the output format to JSON. For information on how to specify a output
format, see Select Output Format for Draw Data and Select Output Format for Reports.

Load a Configuration

Upgrade the Configuration

If you have upgraded the gateway and are loading the configuration for the first time after the upgrade, the
Batch configuration upgrade message is displayed.

Click Yes to upgrade the configuration. An upgraded configuration enables you to use all the latest features of
the gateway. If you click No, you can not use the configuration until individual configurations are upgraded to
latest.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 142

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Note: You can also upgrade the configurations using PML macros and command line. For more information, see
Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line.

Provide General Config Information

The General tab on the Gateway Setup window enables you to provide a description for the configuration,
specify a logging level and log location. You can also specify the staging areas for when exporting elements and
drawings.

To provide the general information about the configuration, do the following on the General tab:

1.

In the Basic Information area, provide a description for the configuration in the Description box.

Note: The description cannot exceed 500 characters.

2.

In the Logging area, do the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 143

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

a.  For the Level option, select the level of logging required for the configuration. The following levels are

available:

a.  Error: If you choose this option, only error messages are logged to the file.

b.  Warning: If you choose this option, error and warning messages are logged to the file. This option is

selected by default.

c.  Information: If you choose this option, error, warning and information messages are logged to the

file.

d.  Verbose: If you choose this option, error, warning, information and verbose messages are logged to

the file.

3.  For the Location box, click the Browse icon and select a location for storing the log file.

Note: If you do not specify a location for the log file, then the log file is not generated but the data is
published to the staging area. However, if you specify an incorrect location, then the data is not published at
all. The location specified here is also used for storing the summary of the export.

4.

In the Output area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to generate the output for AVEVA™ Asset Information Management, select the File System

option, and then click the Browse icon corresponding to Drawings and Reports to select a location each
for the drawings and reports. These locations will be used for storing the output files after the drawings
and reports are exported by the gateway.

Note: If you want the output files to be picked automatically for processing by AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management, you must make sure that the location is listed as watch folders in the Import
Controller and the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box is selected in the Settings > Other tab. For more
information about Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and
Dashboard Installation Guide, and for information about the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box, see
Settings for Common Export Options.

•  If you want to generate the output for AVEVA™ Ingestion Service, select the Ingestion Service option,

and then provide the URL of the AVEVA™ Ingestion Service in the End Point (URL) box and the location
on the Cloud for storing the output in the Folder box.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 144

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

For more information about how AVEVA Ingestion Service works and how to use the Cloud for storing
the output files, refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

5.

In the Options area, select the desired option from the Mapping mode dropdown list.

Additional Settings for AVEVA Ingestion Service

Additional settings are available if you want to generate the output for AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The following options are available in the Defaults section:

•  Context

•  Source

•  Target

•  Publish original

•  Publish reference

•  Publish rendition

Note: These parameters are required and used by AVEVA Ingestion Service. The gateway merely provides an

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 145

option to set these parameters before publishing the data. You must be knowledgeable about these
parameters before using them. For the descriptions of these options, and their possible and default values,
refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The gateway enables you to specify rules for setting the metadata of output files so that they can be processed
accordingly. You can specify the rules using the Metadata section:

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

To specify a rule for metadata:

1.  From the Context list, select whether the rule must be applied for files of Drawings or Reports.

2.  Click Add rule to add a new rule.

3.  Provide the pattern of the file for which the rule must be applied.

Note: You can specify the file extensions as a pattern. For example, you can specify "*.xml", "*.xlsx", or
"*.csv" based on your requirements.

4.  Click Add attribute to specify the attribute and the value.

5.  Select an attribute from the Name list and type a value for the attribute in the Value column. The rule for the

metadata attribute is added.

Note: The Name list contains attributes that are used by AVEVA Ingestion Service. The gateway merely
provides an option to set these attributes before publishing the data. You must have knowledge about these
parameters before using them. For the descriptions of these attributes, and their possible and default values,
refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The attribute values specified here override any default or common values.

6.  To add another attribute, repeat steps 4 and 5.

Note: To remove an attribute, click the cross icon corresponding to the attribute.

7.  To add another rule, repeat steps 2 through 6.

Note: To remove a rule, click the cross icon corresponding to the rule.

It is possible to override any of these AVEVA Ingestion Service options by adding a Metadata node under the
File node of EIWM mappings for the specific class. For information on how to override the AVEVA Ingestion
Service options, see Add Metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service in Add File Node.

The gateway enables you to specify the connection settings of AVEVA Ingestion Service. The following options
are available in the Connection section:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 146

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Note: Point to a field to see a description of the field and its upper and lower limits.

Select Classes

The Classes tab of the Gateway Setup window enables you to select the classes to be exported. The classes you
select here are included in the EIWM/JSON/SVG files.

The following image shows the Class selections area. The classes that have been selected in the Classes Tree tab
of the Settings dialog box are listed in this area and are available for selection in the configuration. For more
information about the Classes Tree tab, see Enable Draw Classes for Selection in the Configuration.

To select classes:

1.  Select the check box corresponding to the class you want to select for export.

Note: If you want to remove a class, clear the check box for the class. If you want to select a sub class, drill-
down the corresponding parent class. The SHEET class is selected by default.

2.  By default, the graphical data option is selected. This is indicated by the presence of an image symbol

adjacent to the SHEET class. If you do not want to generate graphical data, then click the symbol. The symbol
is struck off and removed to indicate the graphical data will not be generated.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 147

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

3.  Repeat step 1 for selecting more classes.

After selecting the classes, you can apply filters on them and specify the mappings. For information on how to
apply filters, see Apply Filters and for information on how to map items, see Map Element Types.

Class Selection Strategies

Due to the complex nature of AVEVA™ E3D Design and the associated project data, it is advised to create the
configurations so as to ensure that the publishing performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering
remains optimal.

Models in AVEVA™ E3D Design could often have large number of sub classes under a parent class. For such
projects, publishing the graphical data of the topmost parent as a single model (single SVG file) can result in
memory-related issues and sub optimal performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering. Therefore,
it is advised to create configurations in such ways that generated SVG drawings would be split into multiple files
of smaller sizes.

Dividing models into smaller parts for publishing is also beneficial in case of future model updates. Consider the
case where a large SITE has been published as one model to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management and at a
later point in time, it must be updated due to changes in source model. In such cases, the entire SITE need not be
published once again if the graphical data has been split into multiple files.

Apply Filters

After selecting the classes, you can apply filters to narrow down the results. Filters can be applied on attributes
or statuses. You can also provide a filter through a PML expression.

Note: In the Class selections area, the classes for which a filter is applied are displayed in red.

The following image shows the <Element type> class filters area.

Note: The Edit Filter and Remove Filter icons enable you to edit and remove the selected filters respectively. For
information about how to perform these tasks, see Modify a Configuration.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 148

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Attribute Filter

To add an attribute filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Attribute Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area. The available attributes of the class are displayed in the

Attribute list.

Note: If you want to apply the same filter for multiple classes, you can highlight multiple classes by holding
CTRL. In such cases, the attributes applicable for all the highlighted classes are displayed in the Attribute list.

3.  Select an attribute from the Attribute list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

7.  Repeat steps 2 through 6 to add more attribute filters.

The following image shows the Attribute Filter section.

Status Filter

To add a status filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Status Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area. The available statuses for the class are displayed in the Status

Definition list.

3.  Select a status from the Status Definition list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Status Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

7.  Repeat steps 2 through 6 to add more status filters on other element types.

The following image shows the Status Filter section.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 149

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

PML Expression Filter

To add a filter through a PML expression:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the PML Expression Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area.

3.  Type an expression in the box.

4.  Click Add filter. The filter is added.

5.  Repeat steps 2 through 4 to add more filters using PML expressions for other element types.

Note: You can also use PML regular expressions to provide the filter. For more information about using
regular expressions in the gateway, see Using Regular Expressions for Filters and Mappings.

The following image shows the PML Expression Filter section.

Map Element Types

The Class Mappings page on the the Drawings tab enable you to define the mappings for various attributes of
the selected classes. The mappings specify how the attributes of AVEVA™ E3D Design must map to the same of
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management after they are imported into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.
You can add to, modify or remove the mappings based on your need. For information on how to modify the
mappings, see Modify a Configuration.

From the class list, select the class for which you want to define mappings. If there are any default mappings
available for the class, the same are displayed in the EIWM tab.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 150

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

If you want to add your own mappings, then right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object
Mapping area. The options to add an IF condition, Object ID and automap are available. For information on how
to add an IF or a FOR-EACH condition, see Add Conditions for Attributes, and for information on how to add an
object and map it, see Map Object ID.

The Auto Map option displays all the default mappings available for the class.

Note: The EIWM Object Mapping area supports multi-selection and drag-and-drop operations. Using the multi-
selection feature, you can select multiple nodes of the same level by holding the CTRL or SHIFT key and delete
them by right-clicking and selecting Remove. Using the drag-and-drop feature, you can drag a node from one
location to another. However, multi-selection together with drag-and-drop is currently not supported.

You can perform the following tasks under the Class Mappings link:

•  Add File Node

•  Map Object ID

•  Add Attributes to Classes

•  Add Conditions for Attributes

•  Add Unmapped Attributes

This section describes the procedures to perform these tasks.

Note: You can also use regular expressions to provide the mappings. For more information about using regular
expressions in the gateway, see Using Regular Expressions for Filters and Mappings.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 151

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Add File Node

You can use the File node to specify the name of the output EIWM file for the objects. If an object is not added
to a File node, the name generated EIWM file for the object is in the following format: <Name of the exported
class>_<File Suffix>.xml

If an object is added to a File node, the generated EIWM file for the object is in the following format: <File
Name>_<File Suffix>.xml. If you have added a folder name, a folder by the given name is created in the
staging area.

To add a File node:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.  Right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object Mapping area and select Add File. The File

node is added.

4.  Select File node. The Name, Template ID and Folder name areas are displayed.

5.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the file name directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

6.  For the Template ID area, do one of the following:

Note: The value of Template ID is added to each generated EIWM file as information about the file. For
example, the template ID can be used by AVEVA™ Asset Information Management to find out which file is
the latest.

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the template ID directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 152

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

7.  (Optional) For the Folder name area, do the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the folder name directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: The nodes without a mapping are displayed in red. Invalid file name characters (* ? " < > |) are
replaced with an underscore ("_").

Adding Information to File Node

To add information to the File node, you can either modify the default mappings or specify new mappings.
Right-click the File node and select the required option.

You can add metadata, IF condition and object to a File node. For information about how to add metadata, see
below. For information about how to add an IF condition, see Add Conditions for Attributes, and for information
about how to add an object, see Map Object ID.

Adding Metadata to File Node

You can add metadata to the file by using the Add Metadata option.

To add metadata to the File node:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 153

1.  Right-click the File node, and select Add Metadata. The Metadata node is added.

2.  Select the node. The name area is displayed.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

3.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the name directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

The metadata is added to the File node.

Add Metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service

You can use the Add Metadata option to add metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service. The options you specify
here override the default options for AVEVA Ingestion Service and those specified through the General tab. For
information on how to specify the AVEVA Ingestion Service options through the General tab, see Additional
Settings for AVEVA Ingestion Service in Provide General Config Information.

The following Name values are reserved for AVEVA Ingestion Service:

•  FILE_NAME - STRING

•  CONTEXT - STRING

•  SOURCE - STRING

•  TARGET - STRING

•  PUBLISH_ORIGINAL - true/false

•  PUBLISH_REFERENCE - true/false

•  PUBLISH_RENDITION - true/false

•  DOCUMENT_IDENTIFIER - STRING

•  REGISTER_IDENTIFIER - STRING

•  FILE_REVISION_KEY - STRING

The following image shows three options for AVEVA Ingestion Service.

Map Object ID

You can modify the default mapping or specify another mapping for the object ID.

To define the object ID mapping:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

If the class has a File root, and if you want to add the object to the file, select File and right-click and
select Add Object. Else, right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object Mapping area, and
select Add Object. The Object ID with or without a default mapping is displayed.

4.  Select the root of the object tree Object. The Object ID area appears.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 155

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the Object ID directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: The objects without a mapping are displayed in red.

Resolving Duplicate Objects

At the time of creating the configuration, you can specify what needs to be done if two or more objects have the
same Object ID. The Advanced Options area enables you to specify how to handle duplicate objects:

The following options are available:

•  Keep: If you choose this option, the original object is retained in the EIWM file and all other objects are

ignored. All the values of attributes correspond to the values in the original object.

•  Append: If you choose this option, the original object is retained in the EIWM file but any missing attributes

are appended from the remaining objects. The values of the attributes correspond to the values in the
original object first, but if the attribute is not present in the original object, the value corresponds to the
attribute in the new object.

•  Replace: If you choose this option, the original object is replaced with the new object. All the values of the

attributes correspond to the values in the new object.

Add Attributes to Classes

You can add the following attributes to the classes that have been selected for export:

•  Context

•  Name

•  Revision

•  Class ID

•  Characteristic

•  Property

•  Association

The following image shows the user interface for adding attributes.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Adding Context

You can add a context to an individual object. The context you provide here overrides the common context
configured in the Other tab of the Settings dialog box. For more information about the common context, see
Specify Common Settings for Export.

An object can have only one context.

To add a context:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a context,
right-click and select Add EIWM > Context. The Context attribute is added to the object tree and the
Context ID area appears.

4.  Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Context ID attribute directly, select the Constant option and
enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Context ID attribute in the EIWM

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 157

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Context ID attribute from the value of an attribute, select the
From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the
Context ID attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Context ID attribute from a PML expression, select the From

PML option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Context ID attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed
in the tooltip. You can provide a nested context by providing the highest context first followed by a + or |
symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or ROOT|PARENT|CHILD).

Adding the Name Attribute

An object can have only one Name attribute.

To add a Name attribute:

1.  Click the Drawings tab, and then click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a name
attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Name. The Name attribute is added to the object tree and the
Name area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Name attribute directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from a PML expression, select the From PML

option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 158

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

in the tooltip.

Adding the Revision Attribute

An object can have only one Revision attribute.

To add a Revision attribute:

1.  On the Revision tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a revision
attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Revision. The Revision attribute is added to the object tree and
the Revision area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Revision attribute directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Revision attribute in the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Revision attribute from the value of an attribute, select the From

Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the
Revision attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Revision attribute from a PML expression, select the From PML
option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Revision attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed in
the tooltip.

Adding ClassID

An object can have only one class ID.

To add a class ID attribute:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.  Select the required Object ID node, right-click and select Add EIWM > ClassID.

4.  Select ClassID in the object tree. The ClassID area appears.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 159

Note: The mapping for the ClassID attribute can be added only once per object.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the class ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the mapping for the class ID is also deleted.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Important: The ClassIDs without a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed in the
tooltip. The ClassIDs which are not present in the current AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
Dashboard (Asset Information Management) schema are displayed in yellow and a warning message is
displayed in the tooltip. For information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
Dashboard (Asset Information Management) server details and to download the latest schema updates, see
Choose AVEVA AIM Schema & Enable Automapping .

Adding a Characteristic Attribute

You can add any number of Characteristic attributes to an object.

To add a Characteristic attribute:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a
characteristic attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Characteristic. The Characteristic attribute is
added to the object tree and the Name and Value areas appear.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 160

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

4.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the characteristic name directly, select the Constant option and enter
a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name field of the Characteristic attribute
in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the value is deleted from the object tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic name from the value of an attribute, select the

From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the
Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic name from a PML expression, select the From PML
option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the Value area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value of the characteristic directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Value field of the Characteristic attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from the value of an attribute, select the From

Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Value
field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from a PML expression, select the From PML option
and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Value field
of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The characteristics without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is
displayed in the tooltip. The characteristics whose name is not present in the current AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management schema are displayed in yellow and a warning message is displayed in the tooltip.
For information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management server details and to download
the latest schema updates, see Enable Automapping and Choose the AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management Model.

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to specify the object's IncidentalClassID using a dedicated
characteristic with the name IncidentalClassification. You can use this characteristic to define the
IncidentalClassID for your object. The following image depicts an example use of the
IncidentalClassification.
