The resulting EIWM file is as follows:

When adding a characteristic attribute, if you have selected the value to be derived from the From Attribute
option, you can specify if you want to omit those attributes that do not have a value. The Advanced Options area
enables you to specify what must not be exported. For more information about how to use the options, see
Advanced Options for Characteristic and Property Attributes in this section.

Adding a Property Attribute

You can add any number of Property attributes to an object.

To add a Property attribute:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a property
attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Property. The Property attribute is added to the object tree
and the Name, Value and Units areas appear.

4.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the property name directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name field of the Property attribute in the
EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the value is deleted from the object tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property name from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Name
field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property name from a PML expression, select the From PML
option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Name field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the Value area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value of property directly, select the Constant option and enter a value in the
Text box. The specified value is added to the Value field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 163

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

•  If you want to derive the value for the property from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute
option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Value field of the
Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property from a PML expression, select the From PML option and
enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Value field of the
Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

6.  For the Units area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the property units directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Units field of the Property attribute in the
EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property units from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Units
field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the property units from a PML expression, select the From PML

option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Units
field of the Property attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The properties without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed in
the tooltip. The properties whose name is not present in the current AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management schema are displayed in yellow and a warning message is displayed in the tooltip. For
information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management server details and to download the
latest schema updates, see Enable Automapping and Choose the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
Model.

Advanced Options for Characteristic and Property Attributes

When adding a property or characteristic attribute, if you have selected the value to be derived from the From
Attribute option, you can specify if you want to omit those attributes that do not have a value. The Advanced
Options area enables you to specify what must not be exported.

Do one or more of the following in the Advanced Options area depending on your requirements:

•  Select the Don't export empty/white string values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported

when the value is empty or is a white string. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export nullref attribute values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported

when the value is nullref. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export empty array values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported when

the value is an empty array. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export null/unset values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported when

the value is null or unset. This check box is cleared by default.

Adding an Association Attribute

You can add any number of Association attributes to an object.

To add an Association attribute:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 164

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add an
association attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Association. The Association attribute is added to
the object tree and the Type and SourceID areas appear.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

4.  For the Type area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the association type directly, select the Constant option and enter a value in the
Text box. The specified value is added to the Type field of the Association attribute in the EIWM
Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the value is deleted from the object tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the association type from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute

option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the Type field of
the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the association type from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter

a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Type field of the
Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the SourceID area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the source ID of the association directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the SourceID field of the Association attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute
option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the SourceID field
of the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 165

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the SourceID field of
the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Important: The associations without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is
displayed in the tooltip. The associations whose type is not present in the current AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management schema are displayed in yellow and a warning message is displayed in the tooltip. For
information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management server details and to download the
latest schema updates, see Enable Automapping and Choose the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management
Model.

Advanced Options for Association Attributes

You can set the association owner for the element through the gateway. The following options are available:

•  This object: The object on which the association attribute is defined is consider the owner of the association.
For example, if the association "is part of" is defined for a Nozzle, then the association is published as (for
example)"Nozzle is a part of Equipment".

•  Referenced Object: The object which is referenced in the association is considered the owner of the

association. For example, if the association "has parts" is referencing an Equipment, then association is
published as (for example) "Equipment has parts Nozzle"

•  Is external reference: Select the check box if you want the association to be present in the output regardless

of whether the referenced object exists or not.

Add Conditions for Attributes

You can specify IF and FOR EACH statements to fine-tune your configuration.

Note: The drag-and-drop feature is currently not supported for IF and FOR EACH statements.

Adding IF Conditions

If you want to specify any conditions based on which the objects and their attributes must be exported, you can
do so by adding an IF condition.

To add an IF condition:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to add the condition to the attributes of an object, select the root of the object tree for

which you want to add the condition, right-click and select Add control > If Condition.

•  If you want to add the condition on the objects, right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM

Object Mapping area, and select Add If Condition.

The IF condition is added to the tree and the area to add the condition appears.

4.  Click the Add icon. The PML Condition Expression box appears.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 166

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

5.  Type the condition in the box. An example condition is as follows: NAME eq 'TRA001-RECT'

6.  After providing the condition, to specify what needs to be done when the condition is met, add a member to
the If statement. To do this, in the EIWM Object Mapping area, select If, right-click and select Add EIWM,
and then select the attribute that you want to add. For information on how to add attributes, see Add
Attributes to Classes.

7.

If you want to specify what needs to be done when the condition is not met, you must add an Else member
to If. To do this, select If, right-click and select Add control > Else.

8.  After adding the Else member, select Else, right-click and select Add EIWM, and then select the attribute

that you want to add. An example IF condition is as follows:

Note: You can also add nested conditions using IF-ELSE IF, IF-IF-ELSE IF, IF-FOR EACH-ELSE and so
on. You can also use regular expressions to add these conditions.

Adding FOR EACH Statements

A FOR EACH statement enables you to specify the actions to be taken for every ARRAY item of an attribute or
PML expression (which returns an ARRAY result).

To add a FOR EACH condition:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a FOR EACH
condition, right-click and select Add control > For Each. The For Each condition is added to the object tree
and the Variable name and PML expression with array result areas appear.

a.  Enter a variable name in the Variable name box.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 167

Note: The variable name must only contain alphabets, numbers and an underscore ("_").

b.  Enter a PML expression that results an array of values in the PML expression with array result box. The

FOR EACH condition is added.

An example FOR EACH condition is as follows:

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Add Unmapped Attributes

If you want to export all the attributes of a class but have mapped only a few of them, you can use the All not
mapped attributes option to export all the attributes that have not been explicitly mapped. When you use this
option, the gateway loads all the attributes that are currently defined for the class into the EIWM Object
Mapping area and considers them for export.

To include unmapped attributes:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add the
unmapped attributes, right-click and select Tools > Append all not mapped attributes. The list of all
unmapped attributes is loaded.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 168

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

You can review the list of attributes and modify or delete the mappings based on your need.

Tag Classes in Graphical Data

Graphical data can be generated when you are exporting the elements. You can specify the presentation settings
and the mapping for tagging the classes.

When you are exporting graphical data, you can specify how the tag ID of the class must appear in the SVG file
through the SVG tab in the Class Mappings page. The tag ID for the class is retrieved from the mapping specified
here and the same is displayed on the SVG file when the class is selected.

Note: Tagging is supported for design elements and only for those classes that can be selected in the SVG file.
Accordingly, the settings in the SVG tab are applicable to only those element types. Also tagging depends on the
associated Model configuration. For more information on how the output is generated with the combination of a
Draw and an associated Model configuration, see Publish Data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

To map the tag ID and model name of a class:

1.  Click the Drawings tab, and then click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class in the class list.

3.  Click the SVG tab. The Drawing name/Tag ID and Folder name (optional) area appears depending on the

class you have selected.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 169

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

4.  Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the drawing name/tag ID and folder name directly, select

the Constant option and enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is used as the drawing name/
tag ID and folder name of the class.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the drawing name is
also deleted.

•  If you want to derive the drawing name/tag ID and folder name from the value of an attribute, select the
From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute value is used as
the drawing name/tag ID and folder name for the class.

•  If you want to derive the drawing name/tag ID and folder name from a PML expression, select the From
PML option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The result of the PML expression is
used as the drawing name/tag ID and folder name for the class.

Note: The drawing name/tag ID for the classes without a mapping are not displayed in the SVG file.

Specify Presentation Settings

Through the Presentation Settings page, you can specify the settings for the SVG file. You can also import the
settings from an XML file created using the former gateway. For more information about the compatibility with
the former gateway, see Compatibility with AVEVA Gateway.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 170

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

To import the settings from an existing file:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Presentation.

2.  Click Import to import the presentation settings.

3.  Browse to the file that contains the presentation settings, and click Open. The presentation settings are

loaded to the page.

To manually specify the settings:

1.

In the Miscellaneous area, do the following based on your requirements:

a.  For the Line thickness scale factor box, specify the thickness for the lines in the SVG file. The default

value is 1.

b.  For the Background Color box, click Pick Color and then choose a color for the background.

Note: You can add custom colors to the color palette. Use the Define Custom Colors button available on the
Colors dialog box to define custom colors.

2.

In the Color Mapping area, do the following based on your requirements:

a.  Click the Add icon to add a row.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 171

b.  For the From column, enter the RGB value or the name of the color that needs to be mapped.

c.  For the To column, enter the RGB value or the name of the color to define the mapping.

Note: You can use the Delete icon to delete the currently selected mapping.

3.

In the Font Mapping area, do the following based on your requirements:

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

a.  Click the Add icon to add a row.

b.  For the From column, select or enter a font from the list that needs to be mapped.

c.  For the To column, select or enter a font to define the mapping.

Note: You can use the Delete icon to delete the currently selected mapping.

4.

In the Remove layers area, do the following based on your requirements:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 172

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

a.  Click the Add icon to add a row.

b.  For the Layer name box, enter the name of the layer that you want to be removed from the SVG file.

Note: If the layer name mentioned here does not match the layer name of the drawing, then no layer is
removed from the SVG file.

Select Output Format for Draw Data

You can specify the format for your output for the Draw data. Currently, XML (also known as EIWM) and JSON
formats are supported for the Model data. In addition, you can also generate the output in CSV format together
with XML or JSON formats. Output is generated based on the mappings specified and the files are placed in the
staging area for all formats.

To specify the output format:

1.  On the Drawings tab, click Options.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 173

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

2.

In the Engineering Output format area, select the required output format for Draw data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

a.  From the Available Column names list, select a header column for the CSV file. The column is added

to the Column names area.

Note: The list displays all the mappings for all the selected elements. Click the Remove icon
corresponding to a header to remove the header.

b.  Repeat the above step to add more column names.

c.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 174

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

arrow keys.

d.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column
names for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view,
drawing, and so on).

Map Items for Reports

The Class mappings page on the Reports tab enables you to map the items on the reports.

The instructions for mappings items for reports is similar to Map Element Types. Similar to Draw data, you can
add a File node, map Object ID, add various attributes such as name, class ID, revision, characteristic,
property, association, and add conditions such as IF and FOR EACH. See the corresponding section under Map
Element Types for instructions on how to perform these tasks.

Select Output Format for Reports

You can specify the format for your output for the Reports data. Currently, XML (also known as EIWM) and JSON
formats are supported for the Engineering data in the reports. In addition, you can also generate the output in
CSV format together with XML or JSON formats. Reports data can be generated in PDF, CSV or XLSX formats.
Output is generated based on the mappings specified and the files are placed in the staging area for all formats.

To specify the output format for reports:

1.  On the Reports tab, click Options.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 175

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

2.

In the Report output format area, select the required output format for report data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate report data.

•  PDF: Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the PDF format.

•  CSV: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the CSV format.

•  XLSX: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the XLSX format.

3.

In the Engineering Output format area, select the required output format for Model data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 176

Note: To avoid overwriting the output files, the CSV option for engineering output format cannot be
used together with the CSV option for report output format.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

a.  From the Available Column names list, select a header column for the CSV file. The header is added

to the Column names area.

Note: The list displays all the mappings for all the selected elements. Click the Remove icon
corresponding to a header to remove the header.

b.  Repeat the above step to add more column names.

c.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the

arrow keys.

d.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column
names for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view,
drawing, and so on).

Save the Configuration

The Save configuration dialog box appears if you are creating a new configuration and have clicked Save.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 177

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Enter a name for the configuration in the Name box and click OK.

Note: The configuration name cannot contain white spaces and special characters.

Settings for Common Export Options

You can use the Settings page to specify the common export options that will be applicable to all exports
irrespective of the configuration. The gateway uses the export options specified in the Common export
configuration settings area during the export.

To specify the common export options:

1.  On the Element Selection and Filters page, click Settings.

2.

In the Settings area of the Others dialog box, do the following based on your requirements:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 178

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

•  In the Context Configuration area, enter a context name in the Context Name box. You can use the

context name to specify a context for the exported data. You can provide a nested context by providing
the highest context first followed by a "+" or "|" symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or
ROOT|PARENT|CHILD). By default, the context is PROJECTCONTEXT.

The Context Name box cannot be empty and must not contain white spaces.

•  For the File Suffix box, enter a file suffix. Based on the file suffix you provide, AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management picks up the relevant XSLT file to process the exported EIWM file. However, before you
specify a file suffix, make sure that the relevant XSLT file is present with your AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management server. By default, the file suffix is _null which means that AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management will process the EIWM file without any XSLT. For more information about XSLT files, refer to
the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

The File Suffix box cannot be empty and must not contain invalid characters for file names (\ / : * ? " < >
|) or white spaces.

•  Select the Generate 'folder.vnet' file check box if you want a folder.vnet file to be generated. The

folder.vnet file contains information about the class in which the generated SVG file must be placed and

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 179

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

the context in which the object must be saved. By default, this check box is cleared.

•  Select the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box if the output files (EIWM and SVG files) of the exported

elements must be picked automatically by the Import Controller for processing in AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management. By default, the check box is cleared. If you select the check box, make sure
that the staging area of the output files is listed as a watch folder in the Import Controller. For more
information about the Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub
and Dashboard Installation Guide.

3.

In the Performance Settings area, do the following based on your requirements:

•  Select the Optimise Memory Usage option, if you want to reduce excessive memory usage and improve

the performance of the Gateway when it exports large datasets.

•  Under Grids and Database Views section, Partition threshold and Rows per partition has pre-defined
values to improve memory consumption while publishing large grids and database views. These values
are configurable. If the published grid or database view row count exceeds Partitions threshold then,
gateway will export rows data in parts and each part contains maximum number of rows defined with
rows per partition property.

Using Regular Expressions for Filters and Mappings

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) provides three PML functions related to Regular Expressions
that can be used when applying filters and configuring mappings.

This section describes the three PML functions.

define function !!regexmatches(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is BOOLEAN

--  Description:

--  Function to test if given text string matches particular regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Text to test with given regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  TRUE  If text string matches the given regular expression.

--  FALSE  Otherwise.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexmatches(:DisplayName, 'P-[0-9]*')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> FALSE

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 180

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

‘P-067’ -> TRUE

‘P-23’ -> TRUE

‘S-067’ -> FALSE

‘D-23’ -> FALSE

define function !!regexreplaceall(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING, !replacement is
STRING) is STRING

--  Description:

--  Function that returns a string after it replaces each substring of that matches the
given regular expression with the given replacement.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to replace matches using regular expressions

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

--  3  R  STRING  Replacement string which will be used to replace all matches

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  STRING  Text with all matched regular expression patterns replaced

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexreplaceall(:DisplayName, '-[0-9]*', '-XXX')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S-XXX’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D-XXX’

define function !!regexsplit(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is ARRAY

--  Description:

--  Function that returns an array of strings which were separated using delimiter
substring or regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 181

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to split using delimiter substring or regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  Delimiter substring or regular expression to use for splitting input text

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  ARRAY  Array of string

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexsplit(:DisplayName, '-')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P’, ‘067’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P’, ‘23’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S’, ‘067’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D’, ‘23’

Use Environment Variables to Specify Locations

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering supports usage of environment variables for specifying locations in the
following instances:

•  While providing Staging Area location

Example: %aveva_design_user%\IEDGateway\StagingArea

•  When providing Logging Location

Example: %aveva_design_user%IEDGateway\Logs

Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to upgrade configurations using command line and PML
macros.

Manage Configurations Using PML Macros

The gateway provides the IEDConfigurationManager PML object that contains the following methods.

Method

GETAVAILABLE()

Returns true if configurations is supported; else,
returns false

E3D Design (Draw)

Method

Description

Upgrades a single configuration

Upgrades multiple configurations

Upgrades all available configurations

The following is an example of the IEDConfigurationManager object.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!manager= object IEDConfigurationManager()

!manager.upgrade(CfgName1)

Manage Configurations Using Command Line

The gateway provides a new command config upgrade for upgrading configurations. The command can be
used with the following options.

Option

-select "string"

-selectall "bool"

Examples:

Description

Enables you to pass configuration names for upgrade

Enables you to specify if all available configurations
must be upgraded. Overrides anything that was
passed with '-select' option.

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Draw.exe config upgrade TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -selectall true

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Draw.exe config upgrade -TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -select "CfgName1

CfgName2 CfgName3"

For information on how to upgrade a configuration using the user interface, see Load a Configuration.

Modify a Configuration

After creating and saving a configuration, you can modify the configuration.

To modify the configuration:

1.  Load the configuration you want to modify. For information on how to load a configuration, see Load a

Configuration.

2.  After the configuration is loaded, perform one or more of the following procedures depending on what

needs to be modified.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 183

Note: To delete a configuration, select the configuration in the Load Configuration list and then click the
Delete icon. You cannot delete the default configuration. However, using the Delete icon, you can reset it to
the original state.

3.  After you make the required changes based on your need, click Save to save the configuration. The

configuration is now modified.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

Modify Class Selection

To modify the selection of classes:

•  If you want to add classes, see Select Classes.

•  If you want to delete any classes,clear the check box for the corresponding class.

Modify Filters

For information about how to apply new filters, see Apply Filters.

To modify existing filters:

1.

In the Class selections area, highlight the class for which the filter needs to be modified. The list of filters
applicable to the class are displayed.

2.  Select the row of the filter which needs to be modified, and then click the Edit Filter icon. The filter is loaded

into the Attribute Filter, Status Filter or PML Expression Filter area.

Note: If you want to modify a filter that has been added to multiple classes, you can do so using the Select
Classes with Same Filter icon. Select the filter and click the Select Classes with Same Filter icon. All the
classes where the filter is applied are highlighted in the Class selections area.

3.  Modify the filter based on your need and then click Update Filter. The updated filter is displayed in the list of

filters.

Note: To delete a filter, highlight the corresponding row and then click the Delete Filter icon. If the filter has
been applied to multiple classes, the filter is deleted from all the classes.

Modify Item Mappings

To modify the item mappings, do the following based on your requirements:

1.

If you want to modify the mapping for the File node, see Map Element Types.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the File node, select the root of the tree File, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the File node along with all its members is deleted.

2.

If you want to modify the mapping for the Object ID, see Map Object ID.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the Object ID, select the root of the tree Object, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the Object ID along with all its members is deleted. However, remember
that an element without an Object ID is not exported.

3.

If you want to modify the attributes for the classes, select the attribute in the tree and modify it according to
your need. For more information, see Add Attributes to Classes.

Note: If you want to delete an attribute, select the attribute in the tree, right-click and select Remove.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 184

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

However, remember that an attribute that is not mapped is not exported.

4.

If you want to modify the conditions for the attributes, select the condition in the tree and modify it
according to your need. For more information, see Add Conditions for Attributes.

Note: If you want to delete a condition, select the condition in the tree, right-click and select Remove. The
condition along with all its members is deleted.

5.

If you want to change the presentation mappings, see Specify Presentation Settings.

6.

If you want to modify the tagging for the element type, see Tag Classes in Graphical Data.

7.

If you want to change the mappings for reports, see Map Items for Reports.

8.

If you want to change the output formats for reports, see Select Output Format for Reports.

Modify General Information

To modify the general information:

•  On the General tab, modify the description, logging and staging area details based on your requirements. For

more information, see Provide General Config Information.

Modify Common Settings for Export

To modify the common export options:

•  On the Other tab of the Settings dialog box, modify the common export options based on your

requirements. For more information, see Settings for Common Export Options.

Note: Remember that the common export options that are specified in the Other tab of the Settings dialog
box at the time of performing the export are used by the gateway for performing the export.

Publish Data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

You can publish the data from AVEVA™ E3D Design (Draw) through the user interface, the command line and
using PML macros.

Note: The data you want to publish must be saved before you can publish.

The following points must be noted about the output files (EIWM and SVG):

•  The actual output for an export depends both on the classes selected in the configuration and the elements

selected for publishing. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a SITE element for publishing, all the
PIPE elements present in the SITE are exported. In this case, the number of EIWM files generated is
equal to the number of PIPEs in the SITE.

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for publishing, then only

the PIPE element is exported.

•  If you have selected SITE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for the publishing, there

will not be any output.

•  Similarly, the number of SVG files generated for an export also depends on your selection in the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 185

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

configuration. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you are publishing an EQUIP element and have chosen to generate an SVG file for it, then an EIWM file

and an SVG file for the equipment are generated.

•  If you are publishing a SITE element and have chosen to generate an SVG file at the EQUIP level, then an

EIWM and an SVG for each EQUIP element present in the SITE is generated.

•  The output for the reports is dependent on the classes selected in the configuration, the elements selected

for publishing and the report selected for publishing.

•  If you have selected a SITE element type in the configuration, selected the SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01

element for publishing and have opted for a report on the equipments, then a report is generated only
for the equipments available in the SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01 element.

Publish Data from Command Line

Data can also be published from the command line. For this, you must first create a token using AVEVA™
Administration. The token must contain the values for username, password and MDB. Then the token path must
be referred to in the command. For more information about tokens and how to create them, see the AVEVA
Administration User Guide or see Generate Secure Login

Note: You must ensure that the token contains the values for username, password and MDB. An error message is
displayed if the token does not contain the values for these fields.

If you are using AVEVA's Licensing as a Service (LaaS) to license your product, you must export the data through
the user interface at least once before exporting from the command line. When you export the data through the
user interface using AVEVA's LaaS, you are prompted to provide your credentials. After the credentials are
validated, you can export the data through the command line.

To export the data from the command line, you must first change to the installation folder of AVEVA™ E3D
Design using the cd command and then run the following command: AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Draw.exe

The following parameters must be provided with the command:

Note: For more information about the optional parameters, see Settings for Common Export Options.

Yes

Yes

Publish Using PML Macros

You can publish data from AVEVA™ E3D Design using PML functions as well. To do this, you must first create a
macro file that contains the commands to publish. Then, you must run the macro file by dragging it to the
command window in AVEVA™ E3D Design.

The following is an example of the contents of a macro file.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!publisher = object IEDPublisher()

!publisher.SelectElement('/SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01')

!publisher.SelectConfiguration('DefaultConfiguration')

!publisher.SetOption('eiwmcontextname', 'APS')

!publisher.SetOption('eiwmsuffix', '_null')

!publisher.SetOption('generatefoldervnetfile', 'true')

!publisher.SetOption('generatetriggerstartfile', 'true')

!publisher.Publish()

!publisher.ClearSelectedElements()

!publisher.ClearSelectedConfigurations()

!publisher.Publish()

!errorsNo = !publisher.ErrorsNo()

Security Guidelines

Because the gateway produces data into file system or network folders specified by the user for loading into
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management or AVEVA Ingestion Service, it needs to read, write and modify both files

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 190

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

and folders.

The following are the security recommendations for users using the gateway :

•  Use the principle of least privilege

•  For AVEVA AIM Workhub and Dashboard: Grant only Read access to the user account that is used to run
the gateway for all file and folder locations. Change the access to Read/Write only to the specific files
and folders the gateway needs to modify. For example, you can grant Read/Write access to the Staging
Area and Logs folders.

•  For AVEVA Ingestion Service: Grant Read/Write access to target folder (Asset Id) for the specific

CONNECT account user.

•  You do not need to adjust your Firewall settings or User Account Control settings when you install or use the

Gateway.

•  The gateway uses the host product's project defaults location (%APSDFLTS%\IEDGateway) for storing various
configurations and settings. Only project administrators must have Write access to this folder. Other users
must be given Read-only access.

Note: If the above security recommendations are not suitable for your environment, you must investigate
what is the most suitable approach for your environment and apply those practices.

View Data in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

After an element is exported, the following files are generated at the staging area:

•  An XML file (also known as the EIWM file) that contains the data that has been exported

•  An SVG file that contains the graphical data

If you have added the staging area as a watch folder in the Import Controller of AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management, the data is automatically imported in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. Else, you have to
manually import the data using the Import Controller. For more information about the Import Controller, refer to
the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

After the files have been imported, AVEVA™ Asset Information Management displays the AVEVA™ E3D Design
data when queried.

Note: Contact your AVEVA™ Asset Information Management administrator if you are unable to view some of the
data in the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Troubleshooting

Performance Issues While Exporting Data

By default, each export configuration is created with the autoflush option for logging set to true. This enforces
every log message to be stored instantly in the target location without waiting for log buffer to be filled. That
could cause some performance issues on high latency network drives (i.e. Cloud UE solution) when log level is
set to Info or verbose or publishing process is producing many warnings or errors.

To resolve this problem, the configuration administrator could manually edit the configuration files and set
autoFlush attribute to false, in which case the logs are buffered in the application memory and are then

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 191

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

flushed to the log file when buffer size limit (by default 1024 bytes) is reached. Increasing the bufferSize
attribute value (recommended value in such cases is 1048576, which is 1MB of memory ) could also be helpful in
increasing the performance when large amount of information is logged.

The downside of modifying these options is the risk of losing critical error information in the output log file.

Example location for Export common configuration

D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\
ExportCommonCfg.xml

Example location of export configuration:

D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\E3D\3.1.0\
Integrity\Integrity.xml

Generate Secure Login

You can generate a secure login (token) using AVEVA Administration:

To generate a secure login:

1.  Open AVEVA Administration.

2.  Make sure you are in the required project.

3.  Select Users and then select Create Script. Refer to the following image for specific locations.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 192

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Draw)

4.  On the Command Script Generation dialog box, enter the user name / password and MDB name from which
the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering user will be publishing information. Also enter output macro
file path where the generated token (secure login ) must be stored.

5.  After providing all the required information, select OK. The secure login is generated in the .mac format and

is available at the location specified.

After the secure login is generated, the user can use the file as -TokenPath argument:

For example:-TokenPath "D:\projectentry.mac"

The Command Script Generation dialog box can also be accessed using the Command Window tool from
AVEVA™ E3D Design or AVEVA™ Engineering using the following command:

show !!admscriptgenerate

The following image highlights how the Command Script Generation dialog box can be accessed.

E3D Design (Draw)

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

E3D Design (Model)

This guide provides an overview of AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway), and describes how to
use the gateway to create configurations and export the data from AVEVA™ E3D Design to AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management.

Preface

This chapter introduces this guide, specifies the intended audience, provides the summary of chapters and lists
the related documents for the guide. The chapter also provides information about product training and how to
contact technical support.

About This Guide

This guide provides an overview of AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway), and describes how to
use the gateway to create configurations and export the data from AVEVA™ E3D Design to AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management.

Audience

This guide is intended for the administrators of AVEVA™ E3D Design who are responsible for creating
configurations using which the users publish the data to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. The guide is
also intended for the users of AVEVA™ E3D Design who publish the data to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. It is assumed that that you have the functional and technical knowledge of the following:

•  AVEVA™ E3D Design

•  AVEVA™ Asset Information Management

and save them

Product Training Information

Technical Support

Introduction

This chapter introduces AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway).

About AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering

The gateway is an add-in to AVEVA™ E3D Design that enables you to publish the data from models and reports to
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. For this, a configuration must be defined that specifies how the data
must be published. After the configuration is defined, you can publish the elements of a model and/or reports to
AVEVA AIM using the configuration. The gateway processes and converts data from AVEVA™ E3D Design into a
compatible file format that can be loaded into AVEVA AIM.

Using the Extract, Transform and Load (ETL) process, the gateway extracts the required information from
AVEVA™ E3D Design, applies the specified mappings and filters and displays it in AVEVA AIM in a user-friendly

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 196

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

format. The gateway achieves this by creating an XML file called the Engineering Information and Workflow
Model (EIWM) file that contains the information related to the models or elements that are exported. The EIWM
files are then imported into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management using the Import Controller, and the data is
displayed in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. You can also generate graphical data for the selected
elements or models in XGL or ZGL format.

The gateway also enables you to generate the output in JSON and CSV formats and make the output available for
AVEVA Ingestion Service. For information on how to select the output format, see Select Output Format for
Model Data and Select Output Format for Reports. If you want to generate output for consumption by AVEVA
Ingestion Service, see Provide General Information.

Capabilities of AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering has the following capabilities.

•  As an administrator, AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to define which classes can be

selected for publishing. For information on how to define the classes for selection, see Enable Model Classes
for Selection in the Configuration.

•  You can select the output format for data and reports. For information on the output formats supported and

how to select the required output format, see Select Output Format for Model Data and Select Output
Format for Reports.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to publish the output for AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management or AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering. For information on how to specify the output
destination, see Provide General Information.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to publish data using the user interface, command

line, and PML macros. For information on the alternative publishing methods, see Publish Data.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to upgrade your configurations using user interface,
command line, and PML macros. For information on the alternative ways of upgrading configurations, see
Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering supports use of Regular Expressions for specifying filters and

mapping. For information on how to specify the filters and mappings using regular expressions, see Provide
Filters and Mappings Using Regular Expressions.

•  AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering recognizes locations specified using environment variables. For
information on how to specify locations using environment variables, see Use Environment Variables to
Specify Locations.

Access the Gateway

To access the gateway, you must first install it. For information about how to install the gateway and its licensing
requirements, refer to AVEVA Gateway for Unified Engineering Installation Guide.

After installing the gateway, log in as an administrator, and start AVEVA™ E3D Design with the Model module.
The Gateway Setup icon is now available in the Asset Information Management group of the ADMIN tab. You
can use this icon to create configurations based on which the data can be published to AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

E3D Design (Model)

Click Gateway Setup. The Gateway Setup window appears and is docked to the right-hand side of the AVEVA™
E3D Design window.The following image shows the Gateway Setup window.

Import Gateway Configuration

You can import Gateway configurations from the previous version of the host product.

To import a configuration:

1.  Click Gateway Setup,the following options are displayed:

•  There are no existing configurations for current product version.

•  There are already existing configurations for other product versions.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 198

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

2.  Click Yes to navigate to Import from other product version view.

3.

Import menu displays two options:

•  Import from file

•  Import from other product versions

4.  Do one of the following:

•  If you want to import an existing zip file:

•  Choose Import from file option and select the zip file. For information on how to export the zip file,

see Export Configuration.

•  If you want to import configuration from other product versions:

•  Choose Import from other product versions option. The following pop-up window is displayed.

5.  From the Select module name drop-down list select the module name. List of the selected configuration

names are displayed.

Note: Naming rules are applicable to configuration names, that is:

•  Configuration name cannot already exist in current version.

•  Configuration name cannot be the same as other configuration on the list.

•  Configuration name cannot contain invalid characters.

If you come across any of the above listed scenarios, the tooltip of the configuration should be updated,
and the selected checkbox should be unchecked.

6.  Click Import. You are navigated to the Configuration Manager view and it displays the imported

configurations on the list.

Before You Begin

Before you start using the gateway, you must complete the following tasks.

•  Specify AVEVA AIM Server Details

•  Choose AVEVA AIM Schema & Enable Automapping

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 201

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  Enable Model Classes for Selection in the Configuration

Specify AVEVA AIM Server Details

Before you start working with the gateway, you must specify which AVEVA AIM server you want to connect to.
You can do this using the Add Server functionality in the Admin tab.

To specify the AVEVA AIM server details:

1.  On the Admin tab, click Add Server. The Dashboard Server Selection dialog box is displayed.

2.  Do one of the following:

•  If you already have one or more servers configured, select a server that you want to use for the gateway

in the Default Server list, and click Apply.

•  If you do not have a server configured, click the plus icon to add new server configuration, specify the
server name, its URL, and the portal ID in the Name, URL and PortalID boxes respectively, and click
Apply. Then select the server from the Default Server list, and then click Apply again.

The AVEVA AIM server details are now added.

Choose AVEVA AIM Schema & Enable Automapping

The AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) provides an option to map classes and their attributes
automatically through the Perform automapping when adding classes check box in the Settings dialog box. The

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 202

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

check box enables you to specify if you want the class and attribute mappings to be done automatically and is
selected by default. Clear the check box if you do not want the mappings to be done automatically.

The gateway also enables you to specify the AVEVA AIM schema or ISM that can be used as a reference while
adding the mappings. In addition to automapping of classes, attributes and associations, the schema you specify
here is used to highlight the classes, attributes and associations of a class that are not present in the schema in
yellow. However, this is just a visual representation and does not affect the export process.

To choose an AVEVA AIM schema:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click Settings. The Settings dialog box is displayed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 203

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

2.  On the AVEVA AIM Model tab, make sure that the AVEVA AIM option is is selected. The Active AVEVA AIM

Server Information area with Active AIM Server details is displayed. Click Check Update to check for updates
or click OK.

Note: If you have not selected any active AVEVA AIM server, the AVEVA AIM Schema area is empty. Use the
Add Server functionality in the AVEVA AIM group of the Admin tab if you want to add a new server or
change the location of the server. The server you select as the default server in the Dashboard Server
Selection dialog box is set as the active server for the gateway. For more information about the Add Server
functionality and the Dashboard Server Selection dialog box, refer to the AVEVA™ E3D Design User Guide.

Alternatively, you can also specify the mappings in a .csv file and place it in the Tables folder. Click Tables
Folder and select the file that contains the mappings. The mappings defined in the file are considered when
the !!ToMapped() and !!ToMappedSafe() PML functions are used for defining mappings.

!!ToMapped(STRING, STRING) – the first argument is the input value that you are mapping from; the second
argument is the table name (CSV file name) where the mappings are available. If this function fails to find the
mapped value in specified table, an error is logged to the log file.

!!ToMappedSafe(STRING, STRING, STRING) – This function works in a similar way except that you can define
a third argument value that can be used as the default value if the mapping is not found. In such cases, the
default value is used for mapping instead of logging an error.

The following image illustrates an example usage.

To choose ISM:

1.  On the Model schema tab, make sure that the ISM option is selected.

Note: Import from ISM feature will allow to import GEICL file. By default, global settings are changed to ISM

a.  Click Import from ISM option and select a GEICL XML file that contains ISM class and attributes model.

b.  Select the Source nomenclature from the available dropdown menu.

c.  Select the Target nomenclature from the available dropdown menu that will be used to translate class

and attributes name from source nomenclature.

Note: Source and Target fields will be enabled and populates naming conventions only after ISM Model has

a.  Expand ISM class and attributes model information to view the imported ISM details.

Enable Model Classes for Selection in the Configuration

You can pre-define the classes that can be used in a configuration using the Classes Tree tab in the Settings
dialog box. The classes you select here are available for selection in the configuration which can then be
exported to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

To enable classes for selection in the configuration:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click Settings. The Settings dialog box is displayed.

2.  Click the Classes Tree tab. All the available classes for the Model module are displayed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA™ Gateways

3.  Select the classes that you want to be available for selection in the configuration.

Note: To select the sub classes, you must drill-down to the required class.

4.  After selecting all the required classes, click OK. The selected classes are now available for selection in the

configuration.

Create a Configuration

A configuration contains information about how you want to publish your data to AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. Primarily, it contains the classes and their attributes that need to be exported. You can also specify
the following information:

•  Filtering criteria, if any

•  Mapping for the classes of AVEVA™ E3D Design to their corresponding classes in AVEVA™ Asset Information

Management

•  Information such as context, characteristics, properties and associations for the elements to be exported

•  Exports options such as the location of the staging area, context configuration and logging

The configuration is created or updated when you click Save on the Gateway Setup window. A configuration may
be used for any number of exports. You can also modify and delete the configuration according to your needs.

The gateway comes with a sample configuration called DefaultConfiguration. This configuration cannot be
deleted. However, you can modify it according to your requirements. For information on how to modify a
configuration, see Modify a Configuration.

Important: The gateway does not validate the information you have provided while creating the configuration.
Therefore, while creating the configuration, you must make sure that all values such as the filtering criteria,
mappings, associations, PML expressions, IF conditions and so on are valid and meaningful to the AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management user. Else, data may not be exported correctly or understood in the desired manner.

To create a configuration:

1.  On the Gateway Setup window, click the Add (+) icon to create a new configuration.

Note: Alternatively, you can load the sample configuration and then modify it according to your needs. For

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 208

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

information on how to load a configuration, see Load a Configuration and for information on how to modify
a configuration, see Modify a Configuration.

2.  On the General tab, provide a description for the configuration, select a logging level and provide a location
for the log file. Also, provide a staging area for the output files. For information about how to perform these
tasks, see Provide General Information.

3.  On the Classes tab, select the required classes and apply the required filters. For information on how to

select classes, see Select Classes, and for information on how to apply filters, see Apply Filters.

4.  On the Models tab, specify the mappings for the classes and graphical data. Here, you can also select the

output format for the model data. For information on how to map items for the model data, see Map Items
for Model Data, for information on how to select an output format, see Select Output Format for Model
Data.

5.  On the Reports tab, specify mappings for the classes in your reports, and select the output format for your
reports. For information on how to map classes for reports, see Map Items for Reports, and for information
on how to select the output format for reports, see Select Output Format for Reports.

6.  Click Save.

7.  On the Save Configuration dialog box, type a name for the configuration in the Name box and then click OK.

The configuration is created. For more information about saving the configuration, see Save the
Configuration.

The configuration is saved and is ready for use.

Load a Configuration

Upgrade the Configuration

If you have upgraded the gateway and are loading the configuration for the first time after the upgrade, the
Batch configuration upgrade message is displayed.

Click Yes to upgrade the configuration. An upgraded configuration enables you to use all the latest features of
the gateway. If you click No, you can not use the configuration until individual configurations are upgraded to
latest.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 209

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Note: After upgrading the configuration from version 1.4.2 to version 1.5 or later, the persisted attribute(s) may
be available for all selected classes irrespective of whether the attribute is present in the class. An error message
"Does not have attribute <attribute>" may be displayed if you are publishing using a configuration that has
persisted attribute(s). To resolve this, after upgrading the configuration, you must make sure that the persisted
attribute mapping is removed manually from all such classes where it is not applicable and save the
configuration.

Also, before upgrading the gateway, you can clear the Persist check box in the configuration for the attributes
that are present in only certain classes.

For example, consider the attribute ANGL which is applicable to only to the NOZZLE class. After upgrading the
configuration, the attribute may be incorrectly added to the other classes if the Persist check box for the
attribute is selected in the configuration. To resolve this, you can do one of the following:

- Before upgrading, make sure that the Persist check box is cleared for the attribute mapping.

- After upgrading, make sure that the attribute mapping is removed manually from all such classes where it is not
applicable, and save the configuration.

You can also upgrade the configurations using PML macros and command line. For more information, see
Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line.

Provide General Information

The General tab on the Gateway Setup window enables you to provide a description for the configuration,
specify a logging level and log location. You can also specify the staging areas for exporting models and reports.

To provide the general information about the configuration, do the following on the General tab:

1.

In the Basic Information area, provide a description for the configuration in the Description box.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 210

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Note: The description cannot exceed 500 characters.

2.

In the Logging area, do the following:

a.  For the Level option, select the level of logging required for the configuration. The following levels are

available:

a.  Error: If you choose this option, only error messages are logged to the file.

b.  Warning: If you choose this option, error and warning messages are logged to the file. This option is

selected by default.

c.  Information: If you choose this option, error, warning and information messages are logged to the

file.

d.  Verbose: If you choose this option, error, warning, information and verbose messages are logged to

the file.

3.  For the Location box, specify a location for storing the log file.

Note: If you do not specify a location for the log file, then the log file is not generated but the data is
published to the staging area. However, if you specify an incorrect location, then the data is not published at
all. The location specified here also also used for storing the summary of the export.

4.

In the Output area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to generate the output for AVEVA™ Asset Information Management, select the File System
option, and then click the Browse icons corresponding to Models and Reports to select a location each
for the models and reports. These location will be used for storing the output files after the data is
exported by the gateway.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 211

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Note: If you want the output files to be picked automatically for processing by AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management, you must make sure that the location is listed as watch folders in the Import
Controller and the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box is selected in the Settings > Other tab. For more
information about Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and
Dashboard Installation Guide, and for information about the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box, see
Specify Common Settings for Export.

•  If you want to generate the output for AVEVA Ingestion Service, select the Ingestion Service option, then
provide the URL of the AVEVA Ingestion Service in the End Point (URL) box and the location on the Cloud
for storing the output in the Folder box.

For more information about how AVEVA Ingestion Service works and how to use the Cloud for storing
the output files, refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

5.

In the Options area, select the desired option from the Mapping mode dropdown list.

Additional Settings for AVEVA Ingestion Service

Additional settings are available if you want to generate the output for AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The following options are available in the Defaults section:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 212

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  Context

•  Source

•  Target

•  Publish original

•  Publish reference

•  Publish rendition

Note: These parameters are required and used by AVEVA Ingestion Service. The gateway merely provides an
option to set these parameters before publishing the data. You must have knowledge about these
parameters before using them. For the descriptions of these options, and their possible and default values,
refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The gateway enables you to specify rules for setting the metadata of output files so that they can be processed
accordingly. You can specify the rules using the Metadata section:

To specify a rule for metadata:

1.  From the Context list, select whether the rule must be applied for files of Models or Reports.

2.  Click Add rule to add a new rule.

3.  Provide the pattern of the file for which the rule must be applied.

Note: You can specify the file extensions as a pattern. For example, you can specify "*.xml", "*.xlsx", or
"*.csv" based on your requirements.

4.  Click Add attribute to specify the attribute and the value.

5.  Select an attribute from the Name list and type a value for the attribute in the Value column. The rule for the

metadata attribute is added.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 213

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Note: The Name list contains attributes that are used by AVEVA Ingestion Service. The gateway merely
provides an option to set these attributes before publishing the data. You must have knowledge about these
parameters before using them. For the descriptions of these attributes, and their possible and default values,
refer to the documentation of AVEVA Ingestion Service.

The attribute values specified here override any default or common values.

6.  To add another attribute, repeat steps 4 and 5.

Note: To remove an attribute, click the cross icon corresponding to the attribute.

7.  To add another rule, repeat steps 2 through 6.

Note: To remove a rule, click the cross icon corresponding to the rule.

It is possible to override any of these AVEVA Ingestion Service options by adding a Metadata node under the
File node of EIWM mappings for the specific class. For information on how to override the AVEVA Ingestion
Service options, see Add Metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service in Add File Node.

The gateway enables you to specify the connection settings of AVEVA Ingestion Service. The following options
are available in the Connection section:

Note: Point to a field to see a description of the field and its upper and lower limits.

Select Classes

The Classes tab of the Gateway Setup window enables you to select the classes to be exported. The classes you
select here are included in the output files.

The following image shows the Class selections area. The classes that have been selected in the Classes Tree tab
of the Settings dialog box are listed in this area and are available for selection in the configuration. For more
information about the Classes Tree tab, see Enable Model Classes for Selection in the Configuration.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 214

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

To select classes:

1.  Select the check box corresponding to the class you want to select for export.

Note: If you want to remove a class, clear the check box for the class. If you want to select a sub class, drill-
down the corresponding parent class.

2.

If you want to generate graphical data for a class, select the check box for the class and point to the class till
you find a 3D cube symbol adjacent to it. Then click the symbol. The cube symbol is added to the class
indicating that graphical data will be generated for the selected class.

Note: If you do not want to generate the graphical data for the class, click the cube symbol again. The cube
symbol is struck off to indicate the graphical data will not be generated for the class.

3.  Repeat steps 1 and 2 for selecting more classes.

After selecting the classes, you can apply filters on them and specify the mappings. For information on how to
apply filters, see Apply Filters and for information on how to map items, see Map Items for Model Data.

The following image illustrates how the Class selections area appears after selecting graphical data.

Important: See Class Selection Strategies for some recommendations on which classes to select in the
configuration.

Apply Filters

After selecting the classes, you can apply filters to narrow down the results. Filters can be applied on attributes
or statuses. You can also provide a filter through a PML expression.

Note: In the Class selections area, the classes for which a filter is applied are displayed in red.

The following image shows the <Element Type> class filters area.

Note: The Edit Filter and Remove Filter icons enable you to edit and remove the selected filters respectively. For
information about how to perform these tasks, see Modify a Configuration.
