Attribute Filter

To add an attribute filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Attribute Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area. The available attributes of the class are displayed in the

Attribute list.

Note: If you want to apply the same filter for multiple classes, you can highlight multiple classes by holding
CTRL. In such cases, the attributes applicable for all the highlighted classes are displayed in the Attribute list.

3.  Select an attribute from the Attribute list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

7.  Repeat steps 2 through 6 to add more attribute filters.

The following image shows the Attribute Filter section.

Status Filter

To add a status filter:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the Status Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area. The available statuses for the class are displayed in the Status

Definition list.

3.  Select a status from the Status Definition list.

4.  Select a condition from the Condition list.

5.  Type a value for the Status Value box.

6.  Click Add Filter. The filter is added.

7.  Repeat steps 2 through 6 to add more status filters on other element types.

The following image shows the Status Filter section.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 217

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

PML Expression Filter

To add a filter through a PML expression:

1.  Click the + icon to expand the PML Expression Filter section.

2.  Highlight the class in the Class selections area.

3.  Type an expression in the box.

4.  Click Add filter. The filter is added.

5.  Repeat steps 2 through 4 to add more filters using PML expressions for other element types.

Note: You can also use PML regular expressions to provide the filter. For more information about using
regular expressions in the gateway, see Provide Filters and Mappings Using Regular Expressions.

The following image shows the PML Expression Filter section.

Map Items for Model Data

The Class Mappings page on the the Models tab enables you to define the mappings for various attributes of the
selected classes. The mappings specify how the attributes of AVEVA™ E3D Design must map to the same of
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management after they are imported into AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.
Some mappings are added by default based on your project and the type of data you want to publish. You can
add to, modify or remove the mappings based on your need. For information on how to modify the mappings,
see Modify a Configuration.

From the class list, select the class for which you want to define mappings. If there are any default mappings
available for the class, the same are displayed in the EIWM tab. If you want to add your own mappings, then
right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object Mapping area. The options to add an IF condition,
an Object ID , a file and automap are available.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 218

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

For information on how to add an IF condition, see Add IF and FOR EACH Statements, for information on how to
add an object and map it, see Map Object ID, and for information on how to add a file node, see Add File Node.

The Auto Map option displays all the default mappings available for the class.

Note: The EIWM Object Mapping area supports multi-selection and drag-and-drop operations. Using the multi-
selection feature, you can select multiple nodes of the same level by holding the CTRL or SHIFT key and delete
them by right-clicking and selecting Remove. Using the drag-and-drop feature, you can drag a node from one
location to another. However, multi-selection together with drag-and-drop is currently not supported.

You can perform the following tasks under the Class Mappings link:

•  Add File Node

•  Map Object ID

•  Add Attributes to Classes

•  Add IF and FOR EACH Statements

•  Add Unmapped Attributes

This section describes the procedures to perform these tasks.

Note: You can also use regular expressions to provide the mappings. For more information about using regular
expressions in the gateway, see Provide Filters and Mappings Using Regular Expressions.

Add File Node

You can use the File node to specify the name of the output EIWM file for the objects. If an object is not added
to a File node, the name generated EIWM file for the object is in the following format: <Name of the exported
class>_<File Suffix>.xml

If an object is added to a File node, the generated EIWM file for the object is in the following format: <File
Name>_<File Suffix>.xml. If you have added a folder name, a folder by the given name is created in the
staging area.

To add a File node:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.  Right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object Mapping area and select Add File. The File

node is added.

4.  Select File node. The Name, Template ID and Folder name areas are displayed.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 219

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

5.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the file name directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

6.  For the Template ID area, do one of the following:

Note: The value of Template ID is added to each generated EIWM file as information about the file. For
example, the template ID can be used by AVEVA™ Asset Information Management to find out which file is
the latest.

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the template ID directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

7.  (Optional) For the Folder name area, do the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the folder name directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: The nodes without a mapping are displayed in red. Invalid file name characters (* ? " < > |) are
replaced with an underscore ("_").

Adding Information to File Node

To add information to the File node, you can either modify the default mappings or specify new mappings.
Right-click the File node and select the required option.

You can add metadata, IF condition and object to a File node. For information about how to add metadata, see
below. For information about how to add an IF condition, see Add IF and FOR EACH Statements, and for
information about how to add an object, see Map Object ID.

Note: Click Remove to remove the File node.

Adding Metadata to File Node

You can add metadata to the file by using the Add Metadata option.

To add metadata to the File node:

1.  Right-click the File node, and select Add Metadata. The Metadata node is added.

2.  Select the node. The name area is displayed.

3.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the name directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

The metadata is added to the File node.

Add Metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service

You can use the Add Metadata option to add metadata for AVEVA Ingestion Service. The options you specify
here override the default options for AVEVA Ingestion Service and those specified through the General tab. For
information on how to specify the AVEVA Ingestion Service options through the General tab, see Additional
Settings for AVEVA Ingestion Service in Provide General Information.

The following Name values are reserved for AVEVA Ingestion Service:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 221

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  FILE_NAME - STRING

•  CONTEXT - STRING

•  SOURCE - STRING

•  TARGET - STRING

•  PUBLISH_ORIGINAL - true/false

•  PUBLISH_REFERENCE - true/false

•  PUBLISH_RENDITION - true/false

•  DOCUMENT_IDENTIFIER - STRING

•  REGISTER_IDENTIFIER - STRING

•  FILE_REVISION_KEY - STRING

The following image shows three options for AVEVA Ingestion Service.

Map Object ID

You can modify the default mapping or specify another mapping for the object ID.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 222

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

To define the object ID mapping:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

If the class has a File root, and if you want to add the object to the file, select File and right-click and
select Add Object. Else, right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM Object Mapping area, and
select Add Object. The Object ID with or without a default mapping is displayed.

4.  Select the root of the object tree Object. The Object ID area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the Object ID directly, select the Constant option and enter

a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: The objects without a mapping are displayed in red.

Resolving Duplicate Objects

At the time of creating the configuration, you can specify what needs to be done if two or more objects have the
same Object ID. The Advanced Options area enables you to specify how to handle duplicate objects:

The following options are available:

•  Keep: If you choose this option, the original object is retained in the EIWM file and all other objects are

ignored. All the values of attributes correspond to the values in the original object.

•  Append: If you choose this option, the original object is retained in the EIWM file but any missing attributes

are appended from the remaining objects. The values of the attributes correspond to the values in the
original object first, but if the attribute is not present in the original object, the value corresponds to the
attribute in the new object.

•  Replace: If you choose this option, the original object is replaced with the new object. All the values of the

attributes correspond to the values in the new object.

Add Attributes to Classes

You can add the following attributes to the classes that have been selected for export:

•  Context

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 223

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  Name

•  Revision

•  Class ID

•  Characteristic

•  Property

•  Association

Note: Right-click on any nod and select Remove to remove the node.

Adding Context

You can add a context to an individual object. The context you provide here overrides the common context
configured in the Other tab of the Settings dialog box. For more information about the common context, see
Specify Common Settings for Export.

An object can have only one context.

To add a context:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a context,
right-click and select Add EIWM > Context. The Context attribute is added to the object tree and the
Context ID area appears.

4.  Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Context ID attribute directly, select the Constant option and
enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Context ID attribute in the EIWM
Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Context ID attribute from the value of an attribute, select the
From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the
Context ID attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Context ID attribute from a PML expression, select the From

PML option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the
Context ID attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed
in the tooltip. You can provide a nested context by providing the highest context first followed by a + or |
symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or ROOT|PARENT|CHILD).

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 224

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Adding the Name Attribute

An object can have only one Name attribute.

To add a Name attribute:

1.  Click the Models tab, and then click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a name
attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Name. The Name attribute is added to the object tree and the
Name area appears.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the Name attribute directly, select the Constant option and enter a
value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the value is deleted from the object
tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from the value of an attribute, select the From
Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the Name attribute from a PML expression, select the From PML

option and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Name
attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The attributes without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is displayed
in the tooltip.

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a revision
attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Revision. The Revision attribute is added to the object tree and
the Revision area appears.

Adding ClassID

An object can have only one class ID.

To add a class ID attribute:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.  Select the required Object ID node, right-click and select Add EIWM > ClassID.

4.  Select ClassID in the object tree. The ClassID area appears.

Note: The mapping for the ClassID attribute can be added only once per object.

Do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the class ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the

Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a

area.

Adding a Characteristic Attribute

You can add any number of Characteristic attributes to an object.

To add a Characteristic attribute:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The default mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the

EIWM Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a
characteristic attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Characteristic. The Characteristic attribute is
added to the object tree and the Name and Value areas appear.

4.  For the Name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value for the characteristic name directly, select the Constant option and enter
a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Name field of the Characteristic attribute
in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the value is deleted from the object tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic name from the value of an attribute, select the

From Attribute option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The same is added to the

Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Name field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the Value area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the value of the characteristic directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the Value field of the Characteristic attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from the value of an attribute, select the From

field of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the characteristic from a PML expression, select the From PML option
and enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Value field
of the Characteristic attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

Note: The characteristics without a value or a mapping are displayed in red and an error message is
displayed in the tooltip. The characteristics whose name is not present in the current AVEVA™ Asset
Information Management schema are displayed in yellow and a warning message is displayed in the tooltip.
For information on how to view the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management server details and to download
the latest schema updates, see Choose AVEVA AIM Schema & Enable Automapping.

Advanced Options for Characteristic and Property Attributes

When adding a property or characteristic attribute, if you have selected the value to be derived from the From
Attribute option, you can specify if you want to omit those attributes that do not have a value. The Advanced
Options area enables you to specify what must not be exported.

Do one or more of the following in the Advanced Options area depending on your requirements:

•  Select the Don't export empty/white string values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported

when the value is empty or is a white string. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export nullref attribute values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported

when the value is nullref. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export empty array values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported when

the value is an empty array. This check box is cleared by default.

•  Select the Don't export null/unset values check box if you do not want the attribute to be exported when

the value is null or unset. This check box is cleared by default.

You can add any number of Association attributes to an object.

To add an Association attribute:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add an
association attribute, right-click and select Add EIWM > Association. The Association attribute is added to

Object Mapping area.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Such values are displayed in yellow in the
list. Click the Delete icon to delete the selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the
list, the value is deleted from the object tree as well.

•  If you want to derive the association type from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute

option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the Type field of
the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the association type from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter

a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the Type field of the
Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

5.  For the SourceID area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the source ID of the association directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the SourceID field of the Association attribute in
the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute
option and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the SourceID field
of the Association attribute in the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the value for the source ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and
enter a PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the SourceID field of

Advanced Options for Association Attributes

You can set the association owner for the element through the gateway. The following options are available:

•  This object: The object on which the association attribute is defined is consider the owner of the association.
For example, if the association "is part of" is defined for a Nozzle, then the association is published as (for
example)"Nozzle is a part of Equipment".

•  Referenced Object: The object which is referenced in the association is considered the owner of the

association. For example, if the association "has parts" is referencing an Equipment, then association is
published as (for example) "Equipment has parts Nozzle"

•  Is external reference: Select the check box if you want the association to be present in the output regardless

of whether the referenced object exists or not.

Add IF and FOR EACH Statements

You can specify IF and FOR EACH statements to fine-tune your configuration.

Note: The drag-and-drop feature is currently not supported for IF and FOR EACH statements.

Adding IF Conditions

If you want to specify any conditions based on which the objects and their attributes must be exported, you can
do so by adding an IF condition.

To add an IF condition:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to add the condition to the attributes of an object, select the root of the object tree for

which you want to add the condition, right-click and select Add control > If Condition.

•  If you want to add the condition on the objects, right-click anywhere on the empty space in the EIWM

Object Mapping area, and select Add If Condition.

The IF condition is added to the tree and the area to add the condition appears.

4.  Click the Add icon. The PML Condition Expression box appears.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 233

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

5.  Type the condition in the box. An example condition is as follows: NAME eq 'TRA001-RECT'

6.  After providing the condition, to specify what needs to be done when the condition is met, add a member to
the If statement. To do this, in the EIWM Object Mapping area, select If, right-click and select Add EIWM,
and then select the attribute that you want to add. For information on how to add attributes, see Add
Attributes to Classes.

7.

If you want to specify what needs to be done when the condition is not met, you must add an Else member
to If. To do this, select If, right-click and select Add control > Else.

8.  After adding the Else member, select Else, right-click and select Add EIWM, and then select the attribute

that you want to add. An example IF condition is as follows:

Note: You can also add nested conditions using IF-ELSE IF, IF-IF-ELSE IF, IF-FOR EACH-ELSE and so
on. You can also use regular expressions to add these conditions.

Adding FOR EACH Statements

A FOR EACH statement enables you to specify the actions to be taken for every ARRAY item of an attribute or
PML expression (which returns an ARRAY result).

To add a FOR EACH condition:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add a FOR EACH
condition, right-click and select Add control > For Each. The For Each condition is added to the object tree
and the Variable name and PML expression with array result areas appear.

a.  Enter a variable name in the Variable name box.

Note: The variable name must only contain alphabets, numbers and an underscore ("_").

b.  Enter a PML expression that results an array of values in the PML expression with array result box. The

FOR EACH condition is added.

An example FOR EACH condition is as follows:

Add Unmapped Attributes

If you want to export all the attributes of a class but have mapped only a few of them, you can use the All not
mapped attributes option to export all the attributes that have not been explicitly mapped. When you use this
option, the gateway loads all the attributes that are currently defined for the class into the EIWM Object
Mapping area and considers them for export.

To include unmapped attributes:

1.  On the Models tab, click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class from the class list. The mappings for the class, if any, are displayed in the EIWM

Object Mapping area on the EIWM tab.

3.

In the EIWM Object Mapping area, select the root of the object tree for which you want to add the
unmapped attributes, right-click and select Tools > Append all not mapped attributes. The list of all
unmapped attributes is loaded.

You can review the list of attributes and modify or delete the mappings based on your need.

Select Output Format for Model Data

You can specify the format for your output for the Model data. Currently, XML (also known as EIWM) and JSON
formats are supported for the Model data. In addition, you can also generate the output in CSV format together
with XML or JSON formats. Output is generated based on the mappings specified and the files are placed in the
staging area for all formats.

To specify the output format:

1.  On the Models tab, click Options.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 235

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

2.

In the Graphical output format area, select the required output format for graphical data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate graphical data.

•  XGL: Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XGL format.

•  ZGL: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the ZGL format.

•  RVM: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the RVM format.

3.

In the Engineering Output format area, select the required output format for Model data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None:Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 236

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

4.  From the Available Column names list, select a header column for the CSV file. The column name is added to

the Column names area.

Note: The list displays column names generated from characteristics and properties defined in EIWM class
mappings for all selected elements.

a.  Repeat the above step to add more column names.

b.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the arrow

keys.

5.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column names

for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view, drawing, and so on).

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 237

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

6.  (Optional) Select the Evaluate All Positions WRT /* check box to specify if the elements must be evaluated

from their root nodes in AVEVA™ E3D Design. By default, the check box is cleared.

Generate Graphical Data

Graphical data can be generated when you are exporting the elements. You can specify the presentation settings
and the mapping for tagging the classes.

Tag Classes in Graphical Data

When you are exporting graphical data, you can specify how the tag ID of the class must appear in the XGL/ZGL/
RVM file through the Graphics tab in the Class Mappings page. The tag ID for the class is retrieved from the
mapping specified here and the same is displayed on the XGL/ZGL/RVM file when the class is selected.

Note: Tagging is supported only for those classes that can be selected in the XGL/ZGL/RVM file. Accordingly, the
settings in the Graphics tab are applicable to only those element types.

To map the tag ID and model name of a class:

1.  Click the Models tab, and then click Class Mappings.

2.  Select the required class in the class list.

3.  Click the Graphics tab. The Tag ID, Model name and Folder name areas appear.

4.  For the Tag ID area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the tag ID directly, select the Constant option and enter a

value in the Text box. The specified value is used as the tag ID of the class.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the tag ID is also
deleted.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 238

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option and

select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute value is used as the tag ID for the class.

•  If you want to derive the tag ID from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a PML
expression in the PML Expression box. The result of the PML expression is used as the tag ID for the
class.

Note: When the graphical output is set to RVM, the Tag ID is same as the element name, for example
/D1201 or /SITE-PIPING-AREA01. As a result, the Tag ID area is not available when the graphical output is
set to RVM. The tag IDs for the classes without a mapping are not displayed in the XGL/ZGL/RVM file.

5.  For the Model name area, do one of the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the model name directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is used as the model name of the class.

Note: You can add the value to the list by clicking the Add icon. Click the Delete icon to delete the
selected value from the list. When you delete a value from the list, the mapping for the tag ID is also
deleted.

•  If you want to derive the model name from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option
and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute value is used as the model name for
the class.

•  If you want to derive the model name from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a

PML expression in the PML Expression box. The result of the PML expression is used as the model name
for the class.

6.  (Optional) For the Folder name area, do the following:

•  If you want to specify the mapping value for the folder name directly, select the Constant option and

enter a value in the Text box. The specified value is added to the EIWM Object Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from the value of an attribute, select the From Attribute option

and select an attribute from the Attribute name list. The attribute is added to the EIWM Object
Mapping area.

•  If you want to derive the mapping value from a PML expression, select the From PML option and enter a
PML expression in the PML Expression box. The PML expression is added to the EIWM Object Mapping
area.

Note: Invalid file name characters (* ? " < > |) are replaced with an underscore ("_").

Specify Presentation Settings

Through the Presentation page, you can specify the settings for the graphics file.

To specify the presentation settings:

1.  Click the Models tab.

2.  On the Presentation page, do one or more of the following based on your requirements:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  Click Get settings from graphical view if you want to reuse the settings provided in View > Settings >
Graphical View of AVEVA™ E3D Design.All the settings defined in the Graphical View dialog box of
AVEVA™ E3D Design are loaded in the Presentation page of AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering.
You can then modify the settings based on your requirement.

•  Select or clear the check boxes for Centerlines, Holes, Plines and Tubes depending on whether you want

to add these components in the graphics files.

•  Modify the levels for Pipe, Nozzle, Hvac, Cable, Structure, and Other depending on how many levels of

these components you want to be included in the graphics files.

•  Turn the Insulation translucency and Obstruction translucency On or Off depending on whether you

want to view the insulation/obstruction translucency in the generated output.

•  Adjust the Density factor slider according to the required density.

Note: The density factor setting is applicable only for XGL/ZGL file. The setting is ignored in RVM files.

Map Items for Reports

The Class mappings page on the Reports tab enables you to map the items on the reports.

The instructions for mappings items for reports is similar to Map Items for Model Data. Similar to Model data,
you can add a File node, map Object ID, add various attributes such as name, class ID, revision, characteristic,
property, association, and add conditions such as IF and FOR EACH. See the corresponding section under Map
Items for Model Data for instructions on how to perform these tasks.

Select Output Format for Reports

You can specify the format for your output for the Reports data. Currently, XML (also known as EIWM) and JSON
formats are supported for the Engineering data in the reports. In addition, you can also generate the output in
CSV format together with XML or JSON formats. Reports data can be generated in PDF, CSV or XLSX formats.
Output is generated based on the mappings specified and the files are placed in the staging area for all formats.

To specify the output format for reports:

2.

The following formats are supported:

•  None: Select this option if you do not want to generate report data.

•  PDF: Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the PDF format.

•  CSV: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the CSV format.

•  XLSX: Select this option if you want to generate the output in the XLSX format.

3.

In the Engineering Output format area, select the required output format for Model data:

The following formats are supported:

•  None:Select this option if you do not want to generate an output file with the engineering data.

•  XML (EIWM): Selected by default, this option enables you to generate the output in the XML format

(also known as EIWM).

•  JSON: Select this option if you want to generate the output in JSON format.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

•  CSV: (Optional) Select this box if you want to generate the output in CSV format together with one of the

above formats. If you have selected this option, do the following:

used together with the CSV option for report output format.

class mappings for all selected elements.

c.  (Optional) After adding the required column names, you can rearrange their order by using the

d.  When Auto-append columns from context is enabled, gateway will automatically generate column
names for all engineering data that is extracted based on specific context (Grid, Database view,

e.  (Optional) Select the Evaluate All Positions WRT /* check box to specify if the elements must be

Save the Configuration

The Save configuration dialog box appears if you are creating a new configuration and have clicked Save.

Enter a name for the configuration in the Name box and click OK.

Note: The configuration name cannot contain white spaces and special characters.

Class Selection Strategies

Due to the complex nature of AVEVA™ E3D Design and the associated project data, it is advised to create the
configurations so as to ensure that the publishing performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering
remains optimal.

Models in AVEVA™ E3D Design could often have large number of sub classes under a parent class (for example,
SITE). For such projects, publishing the engineering and graphical data of an entire SITE as a single model (single
XGL/ZGL file) can result in memory-related issues and sub optimal performance of the AVEVA™ Gateway for
Unified Engineering. Therefore, it is advised to create configurations in such ways that generated models would
be split into multiple files of smaller sizes, for example by ZONE elements or by disciplines.

Consider a configuration that has the SITE class selected as the topmost element for both engineering and
graphical data as shown in the following image.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 243

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

If you have selected a SITE (for example, SITE-PIPE-AREA01) for publishing with the above configuration, the
AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering would generate one EIWM file with the engineering data and one XGL/
ZGL file with the graphical data. Such configuration may run without any memory issues if the selected SITEs are
not too large and complex in graphical model. However, very often a SITE has dozens or even hundreds of ZONEs
which are heavy in 3D model on their own.

The alternative approach is to select the lower classes under SITE as the sources of graphical data as shown in
the following image.

Selecting a SITE (for example, SITE-PIPE-AREA01) for publishing with such configuration would still generate one
large EIWM file for the engineering data but the graphical data would be separated into multiple files based on
number of ZONEs.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 244

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

If you have selected a ZONE (for example, ZONE-PIPE-AREA01) for publishing with the same configuration, then
gateway would generate the same number of EIWM and XGL/ZGL files based on number of ZONEs. Such strategy
of selection would have much less impact on memory usage during the publishing process.

Dividing models into smaller parts for publishing is also beneficial in case of future model updates. Consider the
case where a large SITE has been published as one model to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management and at a
later point in time, it must be updated due to changes in source model. In such cases, the entire SITE need not be
published once again if the graphical data has been split into multiple files.

Specify Common Settings for Export

You can use the Settings dialog box to specify the common export options that will be applicable to all exports
irrespective of the configuration. The gateway uses the export options specified in the Other tab of the Settings
dialog box during the export.

To specify the common export options:

1.  Click Settings.

2.  Click the Other tab.

3.

In the Context Configuration area, do the following based on your requirements:

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 245

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  Enter a context name in the Context Name box. You can use the context name to specify a context for the
exported data. You can provide a nested context by providing the highest context first followed by a + or |
symbol and so on. For example, ROOT+PARENT+CHILD (or ROOT|PARENT|CHILD). By default, the context is
Avngate.

Note: The Context Name box cannot be empty and must not contain white spaces.

•  For the File Suffix box, enter a file suffix. Based on the file suffix you provide, AVEVA™ Asset Information

Management picks up the relevant XSLT file to process the exported EIWM file. However, before you specify
a file suffix, make sure that the relevant XSLT file is present with your AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management server. By default, the file suffix is _Avngate which means that AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management will process the EIWM file without any XSLT. For more information about XSLT files, refer to the
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

Note: The File Suffix box cannot be empty and must not contain invalid characters for file names (\ / : * ? " <
> |) or white spaces.

•  Select the Generate 'folder.vnet' file check box if you want a folder.vnet file to be generated. The

folder.vnet file contains information about the class in which the generated XGL/ZGL file must be placed
and the context in which the object must be saved. By default, this check box is cleared.

•  Select the Generate 'trigger.start' file check box if the output files (EIWM and XGL/ZGL files) of the exported
elements must be picked automatically by the Import Controller for processing in AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management. By default, the check box is cleared. If you select the check box, make sure that the staging
area of the output files is listed as a watch folder in the Import Controller. For more information about the
Import Controller, refer to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation
Guide.

•  Performance Configuration - This section contains any settings related with balancing the performance and

memory in case of experiencing issues.

•  "Optimize Memory Usage" - Enable this option to sacrifice the performance in favor of using less

memory for publishing large data sets.

Note: This option will affect only performance of publishing models.

•  Under Grids and Database Views section, Partition threshold and Rows per partition has pre-defined
values to improve memory consumption while publishing large grids and database views. These values
are configurable. If the published grid or database view row count exceeds Partitions threshold then,
gateway will export rows data in parts and each part contains maximum number of rows defined with
rows per partition property.

Provide Filters and Mappings Using Regular Expressions

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering (gateway) provides three PML functions related to Regular Expressions
that can be used when applying filters and configuring mappings.

This section describes the three PML functions.

define function !!regexmatches(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is BOOLEAN

--  Description:

--  Function to test if given text string matches particular regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 246

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Text to test with given regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  TRUE  If text string matches the given regular expression.

--  FALSE  Otherwise.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexmatches(:DisplayName, 'P-[0-9]*')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> FALSE

‘P-067’ -> TRUE

‘P-23’ -> TRUE

‘S-067’ -> FALSE

‘D-23’ -> FALSE

define function !!regexreplaceall(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING, !replacement is
STRING) is STRING

--  Description:

--  Function that returns a string after it replaces each substring of that matches the
given regular expression with the given replacement.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to replace matches using regular expressions

--  2  R  STRING  The regular expression to which this string is to be matched

--  3  R  STRING  Replacement string which will be used to replace all matches

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  STRING  Text with all matched regular expression patterns replaced

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexreplaceall(:DisplayName, '-[0-9]*', '-XXX')

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 247

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P-XXX’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S-XXX’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D-XXX’

define function !!regexsplit(!text is STRING, !regex is STRING) is ARRAY

--  Description:

--  Function that returns an array of strings which were separated using delimiter
substring or regular expression.

--

------------------------------------------------------------------------

--

-- Function Type:  Function/Procedure

-- Arguments:

--  [#] [R/RW] [Data Type] [Description]

--  1  R  STRING  Input text to split using delimiter substring or regular expression

--  2  R  STRING  Delimiter substring or regular expression to use for splitting input text

-- Return:

--  [Data Type] [Description]

--  ARRAY  Array of string

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Example:

!!regexsplit(:DisplayName, '-')

Depending of attribute :DisplayName value, function will return as follows:

‘P+22’  -> ‘P+22’

‘P-067’ -> ‘P’, ‘067’

‘P-23’ -> ‘P’, ‘23’

‘S-067’ -> ‘S’, ‘067’

‘D-23’ -> ‘D’, ‘23’

Upgrade Configurations Using PML Macros and Command Line

AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering enables you to upgrade configurations using command line and PML
macros.

Manage Configurations Using PML Macros

The gateway provides the IEDConfigurationManager PML object that contains the following methods.

Method

GETAVAILABLE()

ISSUPPORTED(STRING)

REQUIRESUPGRADE(STRING)

UPGRADE(STRING)

UPGRADE(ARRAY)

UPGRADEALL()

Description

Returns an array of available configurations names

Returns true if configurations is supported; else,
returns false

Returns true if configuration requires upgrade; else,
returns false

Upgrades a single configuration

Upgrades multiple configurations

Upgrades all available configurations

The following is an example of the IEDConfigurationManager object.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!manager= object IEDConfigurationManager()

!manager.upgrade(CfgName1)

Manage Configurations Using Command Line

The gateway provides a new command config upgrade for upgrading configurations. The command can be
used with the following options.

Option

-select "string"

-selectall "bool"

Examples:

Description

Enables you to pass configuration names for upgrade

Enables you to specify if all available configurations
must be upgraded. Overrides anything that was
passed with '-select' option.

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Model.exe config upgrade TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -selectall true

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 249

•  AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Model.exe config upgrade -TokenPath "C:\Token.mac" -select "CfgName1

CfgName2 CfgName3"

For information on how to upgrade a configuration using the user interface, see Load a Configuration.

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Modify a Configuration

After creating and saving a configuration, you can modify the configuration.

To modify the configuration:

1.  Load the configuration you want to modify. For information on how to load a configuration, see Load a

Configuration.

2.  After the configuration is loaded, perform one or more of the following procedures depending on what

needs to be modified.

Note: To delete a configuration, select the configuration in the Load Configuration list and then click the
Delete icon. You cannot delete the default configuration. However, using the Delete icon, you can reset it to
the original state.

3.  After you make the required changes based on your need, click Save to save the configuration. The

configuration is now modified.

Modify Class Selection

To modify the selection of classes:

•  If you want to add classes, see Select Classes.

•  If you want to delete any classes,clear the check box for the corresponding class.

Modify Filters

For information about how to apply new filters, see Apply Filters.

To modify existing filters:

1.

In the Class selections area, highlight the class for which the filter needs to be modified. The list of filters
applicable to the class are displayed.

2.  Select the row of the filter which needs to be modified, and then click the Edit Filter icon. The filter is loaded

into the Attribute Filter, Status Filter or PML Expression Filter area.

Note: If you want to modify a filter that has been added to multiple classes, you can do so using the Select
Classes with Same Filter icon. Select the filter and click the Select Classes with Same Filter icon. All the
classes where the filter is applied are highlighted in the Class selections area.

3.  Modify the filter based on your need and then click Update Filter. The updated filter is displayed in the list of

filters.

Note: To delete a filter, highlight the corresponding row and then click the Delete Filter icon. If the filter has
been applied to multiple classes, the filter is deleted from all the classes.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 250

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Modify Item Mappings

To modify the item mappings, do the following based on your requirements:

1.

If you want to modify the mapping for the File node, see Map Items for Model Data.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the File node, select the root of the tree File, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the File node along with all its members is deleted.

2.

If you want to modify the mapping for the Object ID, see Map Object ID.

Note: If you want to delete the mapping for the Object ID, select the root of the tree Object, right-click and
select Remove. The mapping for the Object ID along with all its members is deleted. However, remember
that an element without an Object ID is not exported.

3.

If you want to modify the attributes for the classes, select the attribute in the tree and modify it according to
your need. For more information, see Add Attributes to Classes.

Note: If you want to delete an attribute, select the attribute in the tree, right-click and select Remove.
However, remember that an attribute that is not mapped is not exported.

4.

If you want to modify the conditions for the attributes, select the condition in the tree and modify it
according to your need. For more information, see Add IF and FOR EACH Statements.

Note: If you want to delete a condition, select the condition in the tree, right-click and select Remove. The
condition along with all its members is deleted.

5.

If you want to change the presentation mappings, see Specify Presentation Settings.

6.

If you want to change the mappings for reports, see Map Items for Reports.

7.

If you want to change the output formats for reports, see Select Output Format for Reports.

Modify General Information

To modify the general information:

•  On the General tab, modify the description, logging and staging area details based on your requirements. For

more information, see Provide General Information.

Modify Common Settings for Export

To modify the common export options:

•  On the Other tab of the Settings dialog box, modify the common export options based on your

requirements. For more information, see Specify Common Settings for Export.

Note: Remember that the common export options that are specified in the Other tab of the Settings dialog
box at the time of performing the export are used by the gateway for performing the export.

Publish Data

You can publish the data from AVEVA™ E3D Design to AVEVA™ Asset Information Management through the user
interface, the command line and using PML macros.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 251

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

Note: The data you want to publish must be saved before you can publish.

The following points must be noted about the output files:

•  The actual output for an export depends both on the classes selected in the configuration and the elements

selected for publishing. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a SITE element for publishing, all the
PIPE elements present in the SITE are exported. In this case, the number of EIWM files generated is
equal to the number of PIPEs in the SITE.

•  If you have selected PIPE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for publishing, then only

the PIPE element is exported.

•  If you have selected SITE class in the configuration and selected a PIPE element for the publishing, there

will not be any output.

•  Similarly, the number of XGL/ZGL files generated for an export also depends on your selection in the

configuration. Some examples are as follows:

•  If you are publishing an EQUIP element and have chosen to generate an XGL/ZGL file for it, then an XGL/

ZGL file for the equipment are generated.

•  If you are publishing a SITE element and have chosen to generate an XGL/ZGL file at the EQUIP level,

then an XGL/ZGL for each EQUIP element present in the SITE is generated.

•  The output for the reports is dependent on the classes selected in the configuration, the elements selected

for publishing and the report selected for publishing.

•  If you have selected a SITE element type in the configuration, selected the SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01

element for publishing and have opted for a report on the equipments, then a report is generated only
for the equipments available in the SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01 element.

Publish Using User Interface

You can publish the data by clicking Gateway in the Export group of the Interfaces tab of AVEVA™ E3D Design.

To publish data using user interface:

1.  Click Interfaces > Gateway. The Gateway window appears.

Note: You can also right-click an element in the Model Explorer and select Publish to AVEVA AIM to pen the
Gateway window.

2.

In the Model Explorer of AVEVA™ E3D Design, select the element that you want to export.

3.

In the Elements tab, select the type of element you want to export. The elements you select here are
published for the Model data.

The following types are available:

•  CE: Current element

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 252

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

•  CE Members: Current element members

•  Current Selection: Current selection in AVEVA™ E3D Design

•  Current Collection: Current collection in AVEVA™ E3D Design

•  Drawlist: Current drawing in AVEVA™ E3D Design

Note: The icons Add Element, Remove Element and Remove All Elements enable you to add and remove
elements.

4.

If you want to publish the reports, click the Reports tab, and do the following:

a.  Select a report from the Please select an item list. This list contains Product, Project and User options

and resembles the available reports in the Tools > Run Reports functionality.

Note: If you want add an additional location to the list, click the folder icon and select the folder of your
choice. The location is added to the list. You can use the Remove and Remove all icons to remove the
selected reports.

b.  Repeat the above step to add more reports.

c.  After the required reports are added, select the Selected Elements check box for the report for which
you want to restrict the output according to the elements selected for export in the Elements tab.

5.  The Configuration selection area lists all the configurations available. Select the Selected check box

corresponding to the configurations you want to use.

You can select more than one configuration. However, you must make sure that the combination of the
configurations is meaningful to the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management user. Also, to avoid the
overwriting of files, you must make sure that the staging areas within the configurations are not the same.

Note: The outdated configurations, if any, are displayed using a red exclamation mark with the following
tooltip: "This configuration is outdated. Please contact your configuration administrator for upgrade." For
information about outdated configurations, see Load a Configuration.

6.  After you have selected all the required elements and reports, click Publish to publish the selected data.

Note: If you are publishing the data for AVEVA Ingestion Service, on clicking Publish, you will be prompted
for your AVEVA Connect credentials. The data is published only after the credentials are validated.

The publish process begins. The progress bar displays the progress of the Publish process along with the
element that is currently being published. Simultaneously, the Log View area displays the log entries for the
Publish process. The log entries are displayed according to the logging level defined in the selected
configuration(s).

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 253

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

After the Publish process is complete, the data is published and the output files are generated at the

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 254

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

locations mentioned as staging areas. A summary for the export is generated at the location mentioned for
the log files.

Publish Data from Command Line

Data from AVEVA™ E3D Design can be published from the command line.

For this, you must first create a token using AVEVA Administration. The token must contain the values for
username, password and MDB. Then, the token path must be referred to in the command. For more information
about tokens and how to create them, see the AVEVA Administration User Guide or see Generate Secure Login.

Note: You must ensure that the token contains the values for username, password and MDB. An error message is
displayed if the token does not contain the values for these fields.

If you are using AVEVA's Licensing as a Service (LaaS) to license your product, you must export the data through
the user interface at least once before exporting from the command line. When you export the data through the
user interface using AVEVA's LaaS, you are prompted to provide your credentials. After the credentials are
validated, you can export the data through the command line.

To export the data from the command line, you must either change to the installation folder of AVEVA™ E3D
Design using the cd command, or provide the full path of the installation location, and then run the following
command: AVEVA.Gateway.UE.Model.exe

The following parameters must be provided with the command:

Note: For more information about the optional parameters, see Specify Common Settings for Export. The values
specified through command line override the values set in the configuration.

Parameter

Note: Any errors about the export are added to the log file. The location of the log file is displayed on the screen.
You can cancel the command line export using CTRL+C.

Publish Using PML Macros

You can publish data from AVEVA™ E3D Design using PML functions as well. To do this, you must first create a
macro file that contains the commands to publish. Then, you must run the macro file by dragging it to the
command window in AVEVA™ E3D Design.

The following is an example of the contents of a macro file.

import 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

using namespace 'AVEVA.NET.Gateways.IED.PmlExtensions'

!publisher = object IEDPublisher()

!publisher.SelectElement('/SITE-EQUIPMENT-AREA01')

!publisher.SelectConfiguration('DefaultConfiguration')

!publisher.SetOption('eiwmcontextname', 'APS')

!publisher.SetOption('eiwmsuffix', '_null')

!publisher.SetOption('generatefoldervnetfile', 'true')

!publisher.SetOption('generatetriggerstartfile', 'true')

!publisher.Publish()

!publisher.ClearSelectedElements()

!publisher.ClearSelectedConfigurations()

!publisher.Publish()

!errorsNo = !publisher.ErrorsNo()

E3D Design (Model)

Security Guidelines

Because the gateway produces data into file system or network folders specified by the user for loading into
AVEVA™ Asset Information Management or AVEVA Ingestion Service, it needs to read, write and modify both files
and folders.

The following are the security recommendations for users using the gateway :

•  Use the principle of least privilege

•  For AVEVA AIM Workhub and Dashboard: Grant only Read access to the user account that is used to run
the gateway for all file and folder locations. Change the access to Read/Write only to the specific files
and folders the gateway needs to modify. For example, you can grant Read/Write access to the Staging
Area and Logs folders.

•  For AVEVA Ingestion Service: Grant Read/Write access to target folder (Asset Id) for the specific

CONNECT account user.

•  You do not need to adjust your Firewall settings or User Account Control settings when you install or use the

Gateway.

•  The gateway uses the host product's project defaults location (%APSDFLTS%\IEDGateway) for storing various
configurations and settings. Only project administrators must have Write access to this folder. Other users
must be given Read-only access.

Note: If the above security recommendations are not suitable for your environment, you must investigate
what is the most suitable approach for your environment and apply those practices.

View Data in Asset Information Management (AIM)

After an element is exported, the output files are generated at the staging area:

If you have added the staging area as a watch folder in the Import Controller of AVEVA™ Asset Information
Management, the data is automatically imported in AVEVA™ Asset Information Management. Else, you must
manually import the data using the Import Controller. For more information about the Import Controller, refer to
the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management Workhub and Dashboard Installation Guide.

After the files have been imported, AVEVA™ Asset Information Management displays the AVEVA™ E3D Design
data when queried.

Note: Contact your AVEVA™ Asset Information Management administrator if you are unable to view some of the
data in the AVEVA™ Asset Information Management.

Troubleshooting

Performance Issues While Exporting Data

By default, each export configuration is created with the autoflush option for logging set to true. This enforces
every log message to be stored instantly in the target location without waiting for log buffer to be filled. That
could cause some performance issues on high latency network drives (i.e. Cloud UE solution) when log level is
set to Info or verbose or publishing process is producing many warnings or errors.

To resolve this problem, the configuration administrator could manually edit the configuration files and set

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 261

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

autoFlush attribute to false, in which case the logs are buffered in the application memory and are then
flushed to the log file when buffer size limit (by default 1024 bytes) is reached. Increasing the bufferSize
attribute value (recommended value in such cases is 1048576, which is 1MB of memory ) could also be helpful in
increasing the performance when large amount of information is logged.

The downside of modifying these options is the risk of losing critical error information in the output log file.

Example location for Export common configuration

D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\
ExportCommonCfg.xml

Example location of export configuration:

D:\Users\Public\Documents\AVEVA\Projects\E3D3.1\AvevaPlantSample\apsdflts\IEDGateway\E3D\3.1.0\
Integrity\Integrity.xml

Generate Secure Login

You can generate a secure login (token) using AVEVA Administration:

To generate a secure login:

1.  Open AVEVA Administration.

2.  Make sure you are in the required project.

3.  Select Users and then select Create Script. Refer to the following image for specific locations.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 262

AVEVA™ Gateways
E3D Design (Model)

4.  On the Command Script Generation dialog box, enter the user name / password and MDB name from which
the AVEVA™ Gateway for Unified Engineering user will be publishing information. Also enter output macro
file path where the generated token (secure login ) must be stored.

5.  After providing all the required information, select OK. The secure login is generated in the .mac format and

is available at the location specified.

After the secure login is generated, the user can use the file as -TokenPath argument:

For example:-TokenPath "D:\projectentry.mac"

The Command Script Generation dialog box can also be accessed using the Command Window tool from
AVEVA™ E3D Design or AVEVA™ Engineering using the following command:

show !!admscriptgenerate

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI-enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

Page 5

AVEVA™ Unified Engineering
Welcome to AVEVA Unified Engineering

Welcome to AVEVA Unified Engineering

Welcome to the AVEVA Unified Engineering documentation!

This publication is primarily intended for administrators and users to use AVEVA Unified Engineering to run cloud
projects or local projects within the required components (modules).

This publication assumes that you have access to CONNECT and AVEVA Unified Engineering - Spectrum and the
AVEVA Unified Engineering is uploaded to the cloud. Further, this publication assumes that administrators know
their organization's requirements and are familiar with the concept of component subscriptions and component
permissions. Users are expected to know how to use AVEVA’s cloud components that their organization has
subscribed to.

AVEVA Unified Engineering is a hybrid cloud solution. On the one hand, it allows you to access the latest versions
of AVEVA applications with significant reduction on administration and IT infrastructure requirements and
everything hosted ‘on-premise'. On the other hand it empowers your teams to collaborate openly and
continuously to build the foundation of your digital twin in the cloud.

AVEVA Unified Engineering enables a ‘data-centric’ approach. The teams work around a single data model
allowing for continuous updating and coordination of the project data. AVEVA Unified Engineering offers a
capability to work on the same data at the same time. You can use AVEVA Unified Engineering for complex
engineering and design of capital projects, across disciplines, locations, and organizations securely in the Cloud.

We hope that you will find this publication useful and that it answers all your queries about getting started with
AVEVA Unified Engineering.

If you have been directed to this publication from the AVEVA Administration release notes, please be aware that
AVEVA Unified Engineering is used in all instructions and examples. The instructions and examples also apply to
AVEVA Administration.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

AVEVA Unified Engineering for Users

Information is provided to setup and configure AVEVA Unified Engineering with user access.

•  Get Started with AVEVA Unified Engineering.

•  Use AVEVA Unified Engineering.

Get Started with AVEVA Unified Engineering

Information is provided to setup and configure AVEVA Unified Engineering.

•  Requirements.

•  MSIX Installation Technology for AVEVA Products.

•  Installation Window of AVEVA Unified Engineering.

Requirements

Before installing AVEVA Unified Engineering, check the:

•  Hardware Requirements for AVEVA Unified Engineering.

•  Software Requirements for AVEVA Unified Engineering.

•  Licensing Requirements for AVEVA Unified Engineering.

You must setup the requirements to make sure that the working environment allows the application to run
correctly.

Hardware Requirements for AVEVA Unified Engineering

Graphics Card

AVEVA Unified Engineering requires 3D graphics hardware, capable of running DirectX 12, to ensure optimum
performance for both design and drafting. DirectX 12 is included as an integral part of the Windows operating
systems.

AVEVA strongly recommends that appropriate hardware is used for AVEVA Unified Engineering.

If appropriate graphics hardware is not detected on entry, AVEVA Unified Engineering may still be used but the
following warning is displayed and certain graphical effects, including the use use of laser data is limited.

© 2015-2025 AVEVA Group Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Page 7

AVEVA™ Unified Engineering
AVEVA Unified Engineering for Users

Following extensive testing of current market Graphics Cards and AVEVA Unified Engineering, a recommended
configuration is summarized in the table below.

Graphics Card

DirectX Support

Desktop

Laptop

NVIDIA RTX, Quadro RTX or similar.
For example, NVIDIA RTX A series
A4500 and A4000.

NVIDIA RTX, Quadro RTX or similar.
For example, NVIDIA RTX A Series
A3000 and A2000.

"DirectX 12

"Shader Model 5.1

"DirectX 12

"Shader Model 5.1

AVEVA strongly recommends that clients should test their chosen card or laptop in their own environment before
purchase.

Availability and support of graphics cards changes frequently; a full updated list of graphics cards that have been
verified with AVEVA Unified Engineering is available. For more information, see Knowledge and Support Center.

To ensure the optimal performance of the graphical memory, so that, when utilizing laser data, it is
recommended that the Physical Processor is altered (where employing a NVIDIA graphics card).

Using the NVIDIA Control Panel and setting a GPU allows an increase in PhysX processing and may improve
overall performance.

AVEVA Unified Engineering for Users

32 GB of high speed RAM. Additional RAM increases

The amount of disk space should be configured

To secure data and improve performance, the use of

drives are preferred.
