Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

7

4. The role of DIPDE

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

8

5. High-Impact Worksites for DIPDE

Starting in 2012, the DIPDE will be working hard in the wake of the
post-Fukushima accident and in preparation for the ten-yearly
inspections (extending the life of power plants over 10 years).

SBO diesel generator (DUS in French)

VD4 900 – 4th ten-yearly 900 MW outages

Steam Generators Replacements

How to optimize future studies?

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

9

6. How we manage modifications of nuclear power plants
Or a little history « again »

The U.S. Army's nuclear power program
operated pressurized water reactors (REP)
from 1954 to 1974.

The CPO, CPY, P4 and P'4 reactors are REP
technology and were designed in the 1960s
(American Westinghouse license)

60s design

3D, or not 3D, that is the question

The N4 stage, for “new 4 loops ”1, is the third
and final stage in France's second-generation
nuclear reactor fleet.
The project was born in 1979 and initiated in
1982. The N4 level marks the francization of
reactorscements on the
company's
the
primary system

80s design

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

10

NUCLEAR POWER | FRANCE

EDF leverages AVEVA E3D Design and PCM to revolutionize
the design processes for France's nuclear plants.

Challenges
• Suffered from complex and Time-Consuming Design updates
• Design Offices struggled to consolidate and manage data
• Limited Collaboration and hazardous remote access

Solution
• Deployed AVEVA E3D Design and PCM to be the go-to CAD tool on every operating

nuclear plant in France

Results
• Increased Efficiency and Reduced Downtime
• Higher Data Accuracy and Consistency
• Enhanced Team Collaboration and Project Management
• Sustainability and Safety Advancements

© 2024 AVEVA Group Limited or its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

7. Build the future of work

Starting in 2014, DIPDE plans to remodel in 3D its historic nuclear power plants.

2 scenarios:
1. Remodel its entire nuclear power plants
2. Remodel only future modifications to nuclear power plants

Unlike EDVANCE, DIPDE does not build new nuclear power plants, but only modifies existing ones.

Support and Skills
Management

Communication,
Coordination and
Transformation

Need to go on site

Entering controlled areas

Some equipment is difficult
to access

Access to the premises is not
always possible

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

12

7. Build the future of work

1. Scenario with AVEVA E3D Design Alone

While AVEVA E3D Design is powerful on its own, it has certain limitations without Point Cloud Manager:

•

Reliance on Legacy Data and Assumptions

• Manual Clash Detection

•

•

Increased Site Visits and Downtime

Limited Visualization and Stakeholder Engagement

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

13

7. Build the future of work

2. Scenario with AVEVA Point Cloud Manager (PCM) + AVEVA E3D Design

Using both tools in tandem significantly enhances the design process:

•

•

•

•

•

Accurate As-Built Documentation

Seamless Integration for Clash Detection

Faster Decision-Making and Reduced Downtime

Enhanced Maintenance and Safety Planning

Improved Visualization and Stakeholder Communication

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

14

7. Build the future of work

Scenario 2 is possible with the arrival of E3D Design and PCM on the market.
We chose to model only the modifications to our existing nuclear power plants. Scenario 1 is far too
expensive for EDF.
Thanks to AVEVA, we can now use point clouds for industrial applications.

The hybrid digital model was born (MNH ->Maquette Numérique Hybride)

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

15

15

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

16

8. To summarise

➢ Today’s results for the DIPDE

o Restore confidence in our historical designs, as laser scans represent

the as-built state

o Reduce the need for design teams to travel to power plants

o Prepare maintenance work for major refurbishment and post-

fukushima projects

o Decrease the exposure to radiation of people working on nuclear

power plants

Fleet Engineering, Decommissioning and Environment Division (DIPDE) - ©EDF 2024 - This document is the property of EDF and may not be distributed without written permission.

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI -enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

© 2024 AVEVA Group Limited or its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI -enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

www.aveva.com

© 2025 AVEVA Group Limited or its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.




# SOURCE: vendor_aveva\Extension Namespaces in ISM Rev 1.3.pdf
---

Extension Namespaces ISM Rev 1.3

Melinda Hemstead : Data Consultant

March 2021 | Version 1.3

Extension Namespaces via the ISM Template

Example Class Library.xlsx

To add specific company data or assign standards to your Class Library
you are required to add specific information into the Extension
Namespaces tab.
ISM will then recognise and assign your data correctly.
The prefixes are added into column headers which then become your
extensions.

Note: If you want to add nomenclature mapping to your Class Library and view your
CFIHOS Id’s, Company specific information (example Tag Code), please review
Nomenclature Mapping in ISM Rev 1.3

Extension Namespaces directly into ISM

To add Extension Namespaces directly into ISM, lock your Class Library
for Editing and click on the ‘eye’ icon (Details)
Click on to the Extension Namespaces tab and choose +Add

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Extension Namespaces directly into ISM

Add the Prefix, Name, Description and a unique uri, clicking add and
close to produce the namespace.

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Applying the Namespace in the Template

Example Class Library.xlsx

You may need to add extra columns
to link Company Standards, Industry
Standards, SAP Numbers etc.
These columns require the addition
of the prefix Extension Namespace
(same format) that you added into
the Namespace tab in order for ISM
to be able to read the columns.

Example:
Adding in extra columns to capture
CFIHOS Id’s and Names

cfihos:Id!
cfihos:name!

Format:
‘Extension Namespace’:’Data
header’!

Added Company or Standards Information Shown in ISM

The addition of new data into the
newly added columns is viewed in
ISM in the Extensions leaf...

Applying the Namespace Directly into ISM

Applying the Namespace column
directly into ISM once you have
added it as per previous slide
Right click on Intrinsic and choose
+ Add Namespace, selecting the
new added information

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Applying the Namespace Directly into ISM

Once added right click on the
Namespace and choose to either add
a new header (+ Add Node) (if there
are multiple columns of information
to add in) or if only the value is being
added choose + Add Property

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Applying the Namespace Directly into ISM

This shows Nodes were added to give
sections under the Namespace

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Applying the Namespace Directly into ISM

These show where only a
Property/Properties were added to
these Extension Namespaces

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA
AVEVA is a global leader in engineering and industrial software driving digital transformation across
the entire asset and operational life cycle of capital-intensive industries.

The company’s engineering, planning and operations, asset performance, and monitoring and
control solutions deliver proven results to over 16,000 customers across the globe. Its customers are
supported by the largest industrial software ecosystem, including 4,200 partners and 5,700 certified
developers. AVEVA is headquartered in Cambridge, UK, with over 4,400 employees at 80 locations in
over 40 countries.
aveva.com

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.




# SOURCE: vendor_aveva\Exporting in ISM Rev 1.3.pdf
---

ISM Exporting a Class Library

Melinda Hemstead, Data Consultant

March 2021| Version 1.3

Exporting your Class Library

To Export your Class Library,
firstly click on the Export
icon
Click on the down arrow
menu on ‘Export as’ to
reveal the list of available
exports.
Click on the export your
require.

*ISM Standard Excel Format Exports
in the same format as your load
template

Choosing your Export Options

ISM Standard Format reveals a number of
available options to check or uncheck if not
required.
Once happy with your choice click on the
Export button
This will bring your Class Library export in excel
format into the bottom left hand corner of the
screen

Further Options

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Choose from the Options section
the correct options for your
export.
If you have a composition tick
the Include External Content.
If you have a language fragment
tick to include language
translation.
Preserve inheritance is always
ticked.

Further Options

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

If you have ran a validation
report or have merged Class
Libraries, then you may wish to
include one of these in an
export.
One on Include Change Tracking
– check the box to include and
changes that may have taken
place when producing a merge.
You can choose to Export the full
Class Library with Changes or
Export only the changed rows.

On Include Validation Issues – if
you have run a Validation over
your Class Library you may wish
to Export the issues you are
being shown – again you can
Export all or issued rows only.

Further Options

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Sheets to Include – you
can export all or part of
your Class Library

Further Options

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

You can choose none,
all or part of your
extensions to export

Export Permissible Grid

Permissible Grid on
Connect exports your
Class Library in a grid
format.
There are 4 available
options for this format

Once chosen there are
no further options to
choose from

*Permissible Grid
requires Disciplines in
your Class Library to
produce a report to its
full potential

The hierarchical grid
displays the classes to
their parent class
relationship.
Classes along the
horizontal and
Attributes on the
vertical. Where they
meet this can be
depicted by a letter – R,
O N, P or X. X is if no
presence has been set

Hierarchical Grid

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

One Sheet Per Discipline Grid

The One Sheet Per
Discipline Grid splits
the Disciplines into
separate tabs
This displays the
attributes and Classes
as previously shown –
Class Horizontal and
Attributes vertically.

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Ordered By Discipline Grid

The Order By Discipline
Grid displays all
Disciplines in one tab
and splits the Classes
into their Disciplined
sections
This displays the
attributes and Classes
as previously shown –
Class Horizontal and
Attributes vertically.

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

The Permissible Grid (no options)

The Permissible Grid
with no options
assigned produces the
Disciplines split into a
new column.
This displays the
attributes and Classes
as previously shown –
Class Horizontal and
Attributes vertically.

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Attributes – Characteristics and Properties

 Attributes can be represented in the

system as Characteristics and
Properties

 Characteristics tend to be a single value
with a basic data type such as string,
integer, real or date – They can then be
searched in the system.

 Properties store a

measurement/numeric values. This can
be associated with a Unit Of Measure.

Copyright © 2014 AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

 Permissible UOMs and Systems of UOM
are not automatically setup so they
must be defined before they can be
assigned to <property> tags.

Third Party Gateways - Data Processor

Copyright © 2013 AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

PDMS and Marine Gateway

 The PDMS and Marine Gateways are used to publish the

following data from the appropriate AVEVA design system
to AVEVA NET Portal.

– Convert the 3D Models into .rvm files.

– The .rvm files are streamed into AVEVA NET using VizStream technology.
– The model and corresponding tags are hotspotted in the .rvm file.

– The Drawings are converted into .svg files

– The drawings and corresponding tags are hotspotted in the .svg file

– The Gateways are fully integrated with the design system and contain a

powerful mapping engine that allows mapping of;

– Object Classifications
– Object Attributes
– Object aliases

Copyright © 2013 AVEVA Solutions Limited and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Thank you

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA

AVEVA is a world leader in industrial software, providing engineering and operational solutions across multiple industries,
including oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, power and utilities, marine, renewables, and food and beverage. Our
agnostic and open architecture helps organizations design, build, operate, maintain and optimize the complete lifecycle
of complex industrial assets, from production plants and offshore platforms to manufactured consumer goods.

Over 20,000 enterprises in over 100 countries rely on AVEVA to help them deliver life’s essentials: safe and reliable
energy, food, medicines, infrastructure and more. By connecting people with trusted information and AI -enriched
insights, AVEVA enables teams to engineer efficiently and optimize operations, driving growth and sustainability.

Named as one of the world’s most innovative companies, AVEVA supports customers with open solutions and the
expertise of more than 6,400 employees, 5,000 partners and 5,700 certified developers. The company is headquartered
in Cambridge, UK.

Learn more at www.aveva.com

www.aveva.com

1) Don’t automate a broken process
Standardizing and simplifying processes should be done prior to automating
complexity, but be wary of overfitting the standard

Fast

Impossible

Good

Standardized
Operating
Cheap
Model

Standardized process– same
way of work across factories.
Eliminate complexity by design
and simplify rollout across sites and
enable easier knowledge sharing
Example : OEE Definition

Unified namespace – model driven
implementation will speed up future
rollouts and bring benefits of scale.
We roll out new reports in hours
instead of months now.

Template driven –TCO for
maintenance and feature
development will be reduced vs.

2) Fail forward

Model

Agile – E2E application design
combined with modular

feedback cycles to ensure
validation 🡪🡪 ‘fail forward’

Continuous data improvement –
Data quality will be a challenge
Data does not need to be 100%
correct to bring value (e.g. Trend can
bring insights) but data should not be
trusted 100% either (drift / calibration /
human errors ...). A plan to
continuously improve data is critical.
Requires data ownership

Data availability – Not all data will
be digitally available, but full
automonous control will require a
digital shadow of all controlled
parameters. The requires continued
investment

12

Value extracted is directly proportional to having
good data AND doing something with this data

DECIDE

ACTION

V

D

A

1.

timeliness

information

1. Pick a top loss
2. Decide

countermeasures

1. Implement

countermeasures

learned

Poor data quality → Wrong decisions → Incorrect actions → Poor results

No one to analyse the data → No insights → No actions → No results

IDA model for improving productivity using information. Information, Decisions and Actions drive Results.
Source: https://www.oee.com/improve-oee/

The value delivery formula requires access to right information
with talent able to conduct the analysis.
1 use case can pay for the entire digital transformation project

result will be 0.

applications need to feel like an

takes longer than the former paper-

---

Importing and Editing a Class Library in
ISM Rev 1.2

Melinda Hemstead : Data Consultant

April 2019 | Version 1.2

Import

Click on the Import icon, choose your Class Library template – make
sure ‘Import as new standard’ is the chosen option.
Save your Class Library.
Note: if you Id is not unique ISM will not allow you to import your CL
until the ID has been amended

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Extension Namespaces

A set of tools is displayed – Details (eye icon), Unlock, Save, Export,
Delete and Close.

The Class Library shows the audit trail for its initial save.

Locking your Class Library for editing reveals the extra editing tools –
Merge, Clear, Validation.

Editing Tools

With your Class Library locked you can Merge another Class
Library or Standard into your current Class Library.

You can Clear the contents of your Class Library, keeping the
structure in place and reload an update

You can Validate your Class Library using the built in validators
(which can be configured if required)

linkedin.com/company/aveva

@avevagroup

ABOUT AVEVA
AVEVA is a global leader in engineering and industrial software driving digital transformation across
the entire asset and operational life cycle of capital-intensive industries.

The company’s engineering, planning and operations, asset performance, and monitoring and
control solutions deliver proven results to over 16,000 customers across the globe. Its customers are
supported by the largest industrial software ecosystem, including 4,200 partners and 5,700 certified
developers. AVEVA is headquartered in Cambridge, UK, with over 4,400 employees at 80 locations in
over 40 countries.
aveva.com

© 2019 AVEVA Group plc and its subsidiaries. All rights reserved.

Reviewing data of fleet of around 50+ trucks every day
vs. once a week due to separate data sources.

N E W M O N T   C O R P O R A T I O N

13

Value Story Fuel Savings

lean Fuel Burn due to Low Boost Pressure

• Investigating engine to ensure it performs correctly on
the haul cycle, the two major clues are Boost pressure
and Exhaust temperature difference between the right
and left exhaust temperatures.

• As you will notice there is a large difference in exhaust
temperatures and a variance of over 12 kPa in boost
pressures.

• The first clue is the boost pressure. The maintenance
team needs to investigate the engine for boost leaks.
Boost leaks will reduce power from the truck, and it will
burn an additional 10-20 liters of additional fuel per hour.

Low Boost pressure causing more
leaner fuel burn

Slow truck across the fleet

N E W M O N T   C O R P O R A T I O N

14

Engine which are burning
high fuel and having
boost related issues

operating conditions and operation
during same area of mine

Fuel Usage Reduction up to 59,000 Liters
per month has been identified which
eluates to 5% Fuel consumption

Fuel Optimization & Sustainability:
Newmont's $1.5M Diesel Savings and
3% Uptime Improvement

Challenge

• Absence of the single source of truth for the mobile fleet data
• Use of the analytics in the field
• Data interoperability was minimal
• Change Management

Solutions

• PI System was implemented to capture mobile fleet data and

various analytics were written and enabled by SME's

• To implement action on insights generated by PI Analytics, change

management, coaching and mentoring was critical

Result

• Availability Improvement - 3%
• Fuel Reduction Opportunity Identified - 5% ($1.5 M)
• Emission Reduction Opportunity Identified - Significant Tons of

CO²

•  Data Driven decision making

N E W M O N T   C O R P O R A T I O N

1 Introduction  
1.1 Overview 
 
This document contains all the Training Exercises to accompany ISM training 
presentation. The material assumes that trainees attending the course will be 
familiar with the subject matter, common terms and principals involved in this 
domain. The course trainer will inform trainees of the appropriate exercises to 
complete based upon the accompanying presentation materials that are being 
delivered during the training. 
1.2 Prerequisites 
 
▪ 
Familiar with Ms Windows, Ms Excel and web browsers (IE or Chrome) 
▪ 
Access to the AVEVA ISM site 
▪ 
Access to the training material 
 
1.3 Using this guide 
 
Certain text styles are used to indicate special situations throughout this document, 
here is a summary; 
Menu pull downs and button press actions are indicated by bold dark turquoise 
text. 
Information the user should Key-in will be red in bold Italics. 
Annotation for trainee’s benefit: 
 
 
 Refer to other documentation 
System prompts should be bold and italic in inverted commas i.e. 'Choose function' 
Example files or inputs will be in the courier new font, colours and styles used 
as before. 
 
  
 Additional information, optionally in red for high importance 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 8 of 128 
Acronyms and conventions 
 
CL – Class Library 
ISM – Information Standards Manager 
RClick – stands for pressing the right mouse button 
Adding your initials to the end of any id when adding a file or folder into ISM 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 9 of 128 
2 ISM User Interface 
 
2.1 ISM user interface 
 
▪ 
Main Viewer – [1] 
o Large viewing area that allows selected items to be fully displayed in 
Information Standards Manager. 
▪ 
Side Bar – [2] 
o Access to key functions such as searching and browsing which will 
expand when they are selected. When not in use, the functions can be 
collapsed back into the side bar. 
▪ 
Catalogue – [3] 
o The catalogue provides tools to view and manage the Standards and 
Class Libraries loaded into ISM. It also displays the active Standard (if 
any) as the top-most in the Catalogue window. 
▪ 
Top Bar – [4]  
o Largely dynamic area containing separate tabs for selected items. 
Some static tools i.e. the Current User ID         
, Help and the 
Selected Item are shown here.  
▪ 
On-line Help - [5] 
o Details about Information Standards Manager including definitions, 
functions and descriptions of the icons and behaviour of ISM.  
▪ 
Catalogue Quick-Guide - [6] 
o 
This is available to view when no standards are active. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 10 of 128 
▪ 
Select the Info button  
  in the top right corner of the screen. 
▪ 
The Help page contains details about definitions, functions and descriptions 
of the icons and ISM functionalities. 
▪ 
Click  About tab 
The About page will tell you which versions of the Configurators are available 
in the installation of Information Standards Manager that is open. 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Deleted Class Libraries – [7] 
o 
This area contains all Class libraries you have deleted. They can be 
restored from this area.

3 Basic Import 
The Training Folder must be available for completing the exercises. 
Before we explore the User Interface, we need to load a Class Library into ISM to 
populate the system with data.  
▪ 
In the Catalogue panel select Import icon. 
 
 
 
We will now be presented with the import window where we can browse for a source 
file to load information into the system. For this initial import we have an Excel 
workbook that we are going to load – the spreadsheet conforms to the AVEVA ISM 
Standard Excel Format. 
▪ 
Make sure Import as new Class Library is selected (the default option). 
▪ 
Select Choose File and navigate to ISM Training\Import\My First Class 
Library.xlsx 
▪ 
Click Open. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The spreadsheet that we imported is now set as the active Class Library in 
Information Standards Manager, it can be explored and managed in the system.  
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 12 of 128 
 
 
 
 
 
Save the CL to the system and create the first entry in the audit trail.  
▪ 
Click the Save As icon 
. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Add your initials to the ID value. 
▪ 
Leave the default Name and Description values. 
▪ 
Click Save As. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The Standard will be saved in the system and the audit trail will be updated for this 
instance. The Standard’s number will be incrementally updated for each saved 
session. We will visit the way you can manually update the audit numbering later in 
the training.  
 
 
The default details are 
populated from fields in 
the excel source workbook 
that we’ve imported, but 
the ID will contain your 
initials at the end of the 
string. 
 A newly imported CL is prefixed as ‘unsaved’. Unless the CL is saved for the 
first time, it will be lost in case of another CL import or a session renewal.  
 The CL cannot be locked for editing until it has been saved first. 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

3.1 The Catalogue Structure 
 
With the First Class Library saved it is a good opportunity to investigate the 
Catalogue structure by clicking the Back Arrow in front of the file name: 
 
 
 
The catalogue structure has some predefined folders. The two most important are: 
• 
Personal – the default folder for newly created / imported Class Libraries.  
By default, Class Libraries stored here are visible only the owner. 
• 
Public – the Class Libraries stored here are accessible to Everyone within the 
ISM Instance. 
When hovering over the folder names a brief description of the folders is displayed: 
 
Folder settings can be investigated by RClick the folder names: 
 
 Changes made to the CL are non-volatile which means the CL will stay 
permanently locked with changes pending despite of the session reset, machine 
or SQL Server restart etc. Only the user that has locked the CL can unlock it 
either by saving or discarding the pending changes.  
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 14 of 128 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Click on Properties - the properties of the predefined folders ‘Catalogue Shortcut…’ 
can’t be changed: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
However, under the ‘Referenced Content…’ option you can assign access to people 
to Read, Modify, Delete, Manage Permissions and Change Parent. 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 15 of 128 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The changes can be applied to: 
• 
Newly created Descendant Folders 
• 
The content of the folders (Descendant Shortcuts)   
New groups and users can be added as information consumers. Permissions for 
each group and user can be tailored in detail.  
 
The Subfolder can be created by RClick the target folder name – top level grouping: 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

Import from File … and Create New …  functions are available also at the folder 
level.  When launched from here, the new items imported or created will be stored 
in the target folder.

4.3 Exporting Favorites 
 
Using the same Class Library add some Classes to a Favorites List. 
▪ 
First create a new List  
Id = C_WP_(plus your initials - eg – C_WP_MH) 
Name = Compressor Work Package 
Description = Compressor Work Package for Contractor 
▪ 
Go to your Browse area and open the Compressor Class section 
▪ 
Choose items to ‘Add to Favorites’ – clicking on the star icon 
▪ 
Click on your Favorites and expand the list. 
▪ 
Select the Export icon and choose the Standard Export. 
Export 
▪ 
Open your Export 
 
*Note when exporting from your Favorites list it will focus the Functional 
Class and Functional Class Attributes (if added) into those specific tabs, but 
it will also bring the rest of the Class Library information in. 
You can focus only ONLY exporting the Functional Class and Functional Class 
Attributes of your Favorites by ‘Restricting Export Content’ 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

4.4 Restricting Export Content 
 
If required, it is possible to configure the export from ISM to a very specific set of 
parameters for a particular business purpose. 
For example, you may want to analyse the Attributes within a CL or set of Standards 
and look at this information in isolation. 
Using the First Class Library 
▪ 
Select Export in the ISM Standard Excel format  
▪ 
Create a new configuration by selecting the “+” icon from the Available 
Configurations block# 
 
 
 
▪ 
Enter a name for your Export, “Attributes”  and click the Create button  
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Clear all selections from the following blocks (by toggling the All selection of 
each block you can quickly select/deselect corresponding items): 
o Sheets to Include 
o Extension Sheets to Include 
o Extension Namespaces to Include  
▪ 
From the block Sheets to include select only two items: 
o ISM Attributes 
▪ 
Click the Export button 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 27 of 128 
▪ 
View the exported spreadsheet containing just the data specified in the 
selection. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Close the exported spreadsheets

 at the top-level Class or Attribute folders.  
 
You can open the nodes by Rclick and choosing the option – Open Node Details 
91 
This opens the next level – in this case Compressors

When we select items from the Breakdown structure, the main viewing area of ISM 
will be updated with the details of the selected item, and its relationships within the 
loaded CL. We can see this on screen with the ‘Cables – Cables All’ class that we 
selected. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The Details view will show the corresponding values of the selected class. Other 
tabs are available to show further information about the selected class. These tabs 
are dynamic and will change according to the type of information that has been 
selected in the system. 
 
 
▪ 
Within the Cables token click on Permissible Attributes.

The Permissible Attribute Asset Number will now be displayed in the main viewing 
area with the associated details for this item. 
 
 
 
 
Permissible = An entity that represents a 
reference to another entity. Permissible 
entities will derive values from the referenced 
entity but allows for some values to be 
overridden on the reference itself. What values 
can be overridden depends on the type of 
permissible entity.

5.2 Searching 
 
An alternative way of locating information that has been loaded into ISM is through 
the search function. We will look at the main search feature now but will revisit it 
later in the training whilst exploring other functionalities. 
▪ 
Click on Search  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
In the search window enter Unit and click again Search. 
 
 
The search has returned all results which match anything in the active Standard with 
the term ‘unit’.  
▪ 
Select the Subsea Production Controls – Electrical Distribution Unit token from 
the search results. 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

The selected functional class will open with all the information for the item 
displayed on screen and several tabs for related items/links also available.

, from the search results 
(please observe the icon type). 
 
The Lookup List Unit will open with all the associated information for the selected 
item. 
▪ 
Select another result, attribute Unit 
, from the search results. 
 
The Attribute Unit will open with all the associated information for the selected 
item.

AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 44 of 128 
The CL will now be saved, no longer locked and the audit trail will be updated to: 
• 
Showing a new revision of the CL.  
• 
The details incl. our comment will be visible. 
• 
The items that were previously highlighted as green (for new) or yellow (for 
changed) will no longer be highlighted. 
• 
The items (if any) that were previously highlighted as red (for deleted) won’t 
be existing any more. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
As an Audit Trail is built up within a CL it does allow a user to view the earlier 
versions at any saved point. For instance, we can view the first version of this Class 
Library before the remaining fragments were imported in. 
▪ 
Click on version 0.0.0.1. 
▪ 
Click on Classes. 
▪ 
Expand Functionals > Root of Functional Classes. 
 
We can see several classes that were part of the first fragment we imported. 
 
▪ 
Click on Documents at the end of the Classes list 
There is no information about the documents as it was not part of the import at 
that stage of the CL history. Any saved version of a CL (and its Audit Trail) is 
accessible in the ISM.  
 
 
When merging multiple files that contain 
overlapping information, the information 
from the last imported file takes 
precedence i.e. if two files contain 
overlapping information about the same 
Attribute Class, the information from the 
last imported file will overwrite the 
existing (previously imported) information.  
If multiple files contain 
complimentary 
information, they can be 
imported in any order 
(which is what we have just 
done in this exercise). 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 45 of 128 
7 Managing Standards 
7.1 Tagging a Class Library for Release 
 
Modifying or enhancing a CL might be a lengthy process, potentially involving 
contributions from many different Teams. During this process, it might be practical 
to “Unlock and Save” the CL many times before getting to a point to release the CL 
to or hand it over to another person. 
As we have already seen every time the CL is saved the version number is 
incremented giving a work in progress (a.k.a. Build) versions of the CL i.e. 1.0.0.1. 
When a CL has reached a state where it is ready for deployment it can be “Tagged as 
Released”. This will allow a user to specify the version of the CL and provide clear 
visibility of the released version of the CL.  
▪ 
Navigate to the Composed of Fragments CL we created earlier. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Click on the Tag as Released icon 
 
 
You will notice the system auto 
numbering has allocated it as version 
0.0.0.2 following the change we made 
by importing the additional fragments 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

▪ 
Some export formats will not be available unless corresponding 
validator signatures are present. 
▪ 
Other software might also refuse to accept the CL unless 
corresponding validator signatures are present. 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
To continue the exercise (we are in this instance omitting the validation alert), 
specify a version number of your choice (i.e. 2.0.0) and Click Release. 
The tagged release will appear in the Audit Trail of the CL with the release number 
we specified. The blue background will emphasise the released version of the CL. 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Prior to releasing the CL ISM is alerting the user if no internal validations have 
been committed.  Although it’s strongly recommended to run validations after 
any major update it’s possible to release a non-validated version. Mind the 
potential consequences however: 
 The validation topic is addressed later in this material.

7.2 Deleting and relocating 
 
Although there is a Delete button available the CLs are not truly deleted from the 
system. The Delete function removes the CL from the user interface, however it is 
possible to restore them to the Catalogue and review the details/Audit Trail of the 
deleted CLs. 
To relocate the CL (i.e. remove from the current location):  
▪ 
In the Catalogue RClick ‘My First Class Library (Xml)’ and select Remove from 
Folder option. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
It will end up in the Undesignated Folder 
 
To delete the CL make it active first. 
▪ 
Then select the Delete  
  option.  
 
 
 
As stated in the dialog box the status of the library will now be updated to 
‘deleted’. Click on the X to close the Class Library.  
This will return you to the main catalogue view and the deleted Library will no 
longer be available in the list of Standards.  
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 48 of 128 
 
▪ 
Select another CL (to remove the one we deleted from the active library section 
of the Catalogue) if you so wish. 
▪ 
Return to the main catalogue page 
 
The deleted CL is no longer visible in the system.  We can toggle the visibility of 
standards via the standard list filter feature. 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Click on the Standard List Filter icon (making sure you have selected the folder the 
Catalogue was deleted from) > Show Deleted Standards 
▪ 
All deleted standards will now be displayed in the Catalogue list. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
A deleted CL can be explored and searched in the same way as a ‘normal’ CL. 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 49 of 128 
▪ 
Select the CL we recently deleted and explore its contents. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
To re-instate the CL select Undelete 
 
▪ 
Refresh the page  
 
The Audit Trail for the CL will now be updated showing the latest version of the 
library (undeleted). A deleted entry will be shown as an older Build. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

8 Editing 
 
8.1 Editing - Introduction 
 
During these exercises, we will create a new standard from scratch and edit within 
ISM directly.  
A creation process will help to reveal the way that ISM constructs a CL and 
subsequent exercise steps will show how can we incorporate extensions to meet the 
requirements of external systems. During this process we will also show the export 
capabilities of the system which indicate how easily you can extract a CL or its 
fragment. You can then edit the contents in excel before re-importing the changes 
- introducing the principle of using Ms Excel as a CL editor, and also edit the 
contents directly into ISM. 
 
▪ 
In the Catalogue view click on the Create New icon 
. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Enter the following details in the Create New Standard form: 
o ID = Corporate.Standard.aveva.com 
o Name = Corporate Class Library 
o Description = A corporate CL containing definitions common to all 
projects. 
▪ 
Click Create. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 51 of 128 
 
An empty CL will now exist in the system having no values for the Functional, 
Document, Attribute values etc. Despite of that we can see the structure of the CL 
by exporting the template we’ve just created and viewing it in Excel. 
 
▪ 
Click the Export icon from the Catalogue view to export the template. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Select ‘AVEVA ISM Standard Excel Format’ in the Export as field. 
▪ 
Unselect the Zip Exported File option (if required). 
▪ 
Unselect the Include Grouping of Rows option 
▪ 
Click Export. 
 
 
The exported Excel workbook will be saved in the browser’s default download 
folder and it will contain about 24 tabs where the information for a CL can be 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 52 of 128 
captured. The export will only contain ISM worksheets (no extensions) and very 
basic information because there is no actual class information yet captured in this 
library. The structure of a library however with the worksheets and columns is 
clearly visible.   
▪ 
Navigate to the bottom of your ISM or to your Downloads folder (the place the 
export file is stored). 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Open the ‘Corporate Class Library.xlsx’ we created above. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

At this point in the exercise we are going to demonstrate a basic concept of using 
Excel as a CL editor. An empty Excel template will be populated with some values 
that will start the process of adding some classes in our CL. 
▪ 
In Excel select the ISM Document Classes worksheet. 
▪ 
Enter the following information into the appropriate worksheet columns to set 
the root class for Document Classes. 
 Id = ISM_ROOT_DOCUMENT 
 Name = Root of Document Classes 
 Abstract = true 
 
 
▪ 
Continue entering the following information into the appropriate worksheet 
columns to populate the first Document class. 
 Id = 3D MODEL 
 Name = 3D Model 
 DocumentType = 3D MODEL 
 Extends = ISM_ROOT_DOCUMENT 
 
 
▪ 
Enter the following information into the appropriate worksheet columns to 
populate a second Document class. 
 Id = BLOCK WIRING DIAGRAM 
 Name = Block Wiring Diagram 
 DocumentType = BLOCK WIRING DIAGRAM 
 Extends = ISM_ROOT_DOCUMENT 
 
 
Next, we will add some Attributes into your ISM Attributes tab and group them 
under the header MECHANICAL ATTRIBUTES 
▪ 
Enter the following information as shown 
Flange: 
 Id = AT0001 
 Name = Flange

 Description = Flange 
 GroupId = MECHANICAL ATTRIBUTES 
 Gasket: 
 Id = AT0002 
 Name = Gasket 
 Description = Gasket 
 GroupId = MECHANICAL ATTRIBUTES

Select in Excel File > Save As

Add _new to the filename and save to your Import folder 
▪ 
Close Excel.

.

▪ 
Select Choose File and navigate to the Import folder > Corporate Class 
Library_New.xlsx. 
▪ 
Click Open.

8.2 Editing - Excel as an Editor 
 
▪ 
Import the CL from  
ISM Training\Editing\Excel Editor.xlsx 
▪ 
Save As a new CL. 
▪ 
Export the CL in AVEVA ISM Standard Excel format making sure it is not 
zipped. 
 
▪ 
Unselect option “Include Grouping of Rows”  
 
▪ 
Click Export 
▪ 
Open the exported spreadsheet and activate ‘ISM Attributes’ tab. 
 
We will now make some changes in Excel to edit the CL information and use the 
_Action column to control the behaviour of the import process when we load the 
changes back into ISM. 
▪ 
Select the ‘ISM Attributes’ sheet and sort it by the ID column 
▪ 
Make the following changes according to the accompanying screenshot: 
o Edit the ‘AREA_’ Id value to AREA (row 9) 
o Add Required Field to the Description column of AREA entry (row 9) 
o Add CHANGE  to the _ACTION column for the ASSET_ Id (Row 10) 
o Change the Presence column value to Optional (Row 10) 
o Add IGNORE to the _ACTION column for the BODY TYPE Id (Row 14) 
o Change the Body Type Name value to New Name (Row 14) 
o Add DELETE to the _ACTION column for the BOOM TYPE Id (Row 17) 
o Insert a new row at Row 20 and Add the value NOTE  to the _ACTION 
column and the value Questions should be raised if the Certificate of 
Conformity is missing  to the Id column. 
▪ 
Add _Changed  suffix to the filename and click Save (the save location should 
have defaulted to the Downloads folder). 
▪ 
Save the changes to the spreadsheet. 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 57 of 128

▪ 
The AREA ID will be changed 
▪ 
The AREA description field will have a new entry 
▪ 
The ASSET_ Presence value will be changed to ‘Optional’ 
▪ 
BODY TYPE will not have a ‘New Name’ (as it been instructed to be ignored by 
the import process). 
▪ 
BOOM TYPE will be deleted from the system 
▪ 
The information in row 20 will not be present in ISM (It will be ignored as it is 
a note in the excel version of the CL). 
▪ 
Close the Excel as ISM Editor CL. 
▪ 
Return to ISM  Catalogue view.

Lock the Excel as Editor CL

Select the import icon which is now visible 
  
o Make sure Merge into the active CL is selected.

Editor_Changed.xlsx  (the file you’ve saved in the previous step). 
▪ 
Click Open.

.

Select Modification Filters and then click on search 
.

9 Extensions 
 
9.1 Enabling Extensions 
 
ISM is designed to model Information Standards on a conceptual level without being 
subjected to any constraints from other software systems. ISM can maintain 3rd 
party’s software configuration data in its CL.  
When we have been working with the Standards up to this point we have only been 
working on the standard ISM configurations and the default ISM worksheets. 
Potentially several configurations can be supported according to the Configurators 
that have been enabled globally within ISM. In the following exercises we will start 
to use the extension/configurator functionality to control the contents of the 
Standards and the output of the exports from ISM.  
Firstly, we will review the current configuration of the CL. 
▪ 
From the Catalogue open the Corporate Class Library we created earlier and 
Lock it for editing. 
▪ 
Then click on Details. 
 
 
 
▪ 
Select the Extension Namespaces tab from the details page. 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 75 of 128 
This page will list all the extension namespaces that are enabled in the selected CL. 
At this stage, depending on the ISM version, the list may be empty or may have 
some default extensions like “schema” and “schemainstance”. 
We can now add  
 a new extension namespace to meet the requirements of 
the CFIHOS elements.  
(ISM Training\Extensions\Enabling Extensions.txt contains the text 
below which you can copy and paste). 
▪ 
Prefix = cfihos 
▪ 
Name = CFIHOS  
▪ 
URI = http://schemas.uspi.org/CFIHOS/aveva.ism.extensions/2014/11 
▪ 
Description = CFIHOS Properties 
 
 
 
▪ 
Click Add and Close. 
The CFIHOS extension will now be added to our CL  
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 76 of 128 
 
 
▪ 
Click Close to exit the Details window. 
▪ 
Select Export from the catalogue panel and choose ISM Standard Excel…  
as the export format.  
▪ 
The new options will be shown in the selection window: 
o The new section “Extensions Namespaces to include” will appear.   
▪ 
Select this option “CFIHOS”  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Make sure the Zip Exported File option is unselected. 
▪ 
Click Export. 
▪ 
Open the exported Corporate CL from the download folder. 
 
We can see the CFIHOS extension namespace appeared in the ISM Extension 
Namespaces Tab.  
 
▪ 
Close the Excel and go back to ISM. 
▪ 
In the active class library catalogue view select the Unlock and Save Changes 
icon 
. 
 The namespace URI needs to be literally correct (including proper letter casing) 
otherwise it will not work. 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 77 of 128 
▪ 
Enter a comment when prompted i.e. Added CFIHOS Namespace and click on 
Save and Unlock.

10 Comparing Class Libraries 
 
One of the features of Information Standard Manager is the ability to compare the 
content in Class Libraries across versions, projects or different files. 
10.1 Comparing Class Libraries from different files 
 
▪ 
Using what we have already learned import as a new CL: 
ISM Training\Comparing\Original Version.xlsx  
▪ 
Click Save As  
 (accept the default details). 
▪ 
Lock the Standard for editing 
. 
 
Now that we have loaded a standard, we can begin the comparison process. The 
first part of this process is to clear the content – This will mark all the content as 
deleted so the change tracking in the comparison feature won’t be compromised.  
▪ 
Select the Clear icon.  
 
 
▪ 
Select Clear on the warning message. 
 
 
We can now merge another standard to utilise the search tools to identify any 
differences between the two standards. 
▪ 
Return to the Catalogue view. 
▪ 
Select the Import icon 
. 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 81 of 128 
▪ 
Merge into the active CL. 
▪ 
Select Choose File and navigate to  
ISM Training\Comparing\Modified Version.xlsx  
▪ 
Click Open. 
 
When merging with the cleared standard, all definitions that exist in both “Original” 
and “Modified” will be resurrected. The result will therefore be: 
• 
Definitions that exists in both standards will be resurrected and unmodified. 
• 
Changes in “Modified” will be applied to the resurrected definitions. 
• 
Definitions existing in “Original” but not in “Modified” will remain deleted. 
• 
Definitions existing in “Modified” but not in “Original” will be added. 
 
The differences that we will discover between the two spreadsheets can be analysed 
in the following ways: 
Creating an Excel comparison report:  
▪ 
Select Export icon 
 from the Catalogue window. 
▪ 
Make the following selections in the Export CL form: 
o Export as AVEVA ISM Standard Excel Format 
o Include Change Tracking All and Changed rows only 
o Sheets to include All 
o Select Export when ready. 
 
 
 
o Open the comparison report in Excel. 
o The ISM Attributes tab will resemble the following: 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 82 of 128 
 
 
Modified cells’ comments include the original value of the corresponding cell. Cells’ 
colour coding is described below.  
Comparing differences using the GUI 
▪ 
In the Side Bar click on Search 
. 
▪ 
Select Modification Filters and then click on search 
. 
 
 
 
 
Tokens showing differences between the two libraries will be displayed in the 
search window which will include diverse colors and icons according to the 
information they represent.  
The colors and icons the system uses to highlight the changes above will be used 
consistently throughout the system according to information held in various Class 
Libraries. 
 
Icon 
Description 
 
The entity has been derived from elsewhere 
 
The entity is modified 
 
One of the entity ancestors are modified 
 
The entity is added 
 
The entity is deleted 
 
The entity is obsoleted 
 
The entity is a permissible entry representing a reference to 
another entity. The permissible may allow for some or all of the 
source entity values to be overridden 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 83 of 128 
 
Colour 
Description 
 
The entity or value has been derived from elsewhere, e.g. an 
inherited permissible attribute. 
 
The entity or value have been modified 
 
 
The derived entity or value have been modified in an ancestor 
 
The entity or value have been added 
 
The entity of value has been deleted 
 
▪ 
Select the Attribute Abbreviated Tag Description from the results. 
▪ 
Hide the search window by selecting the search icon again. 
 
The details of the selected token are displayed in the main viewing area and, in this 
case, the modified values are highlighted in yellow. 
 
 
 
 
The other search results will display the differences between the two Class Libraries 
that have been loaded for comparison. 
▪ 
Return to the active CL. 
▪ 
Select Unlock and Discard Changes 
 in the Standard now we have 
completed the comparison analysis.  
▪ 
Select Unlock and Discard Changes in the warning messages. 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

10.2 Comparing Class Libraries (loaded in ISM) 
 
As well as comparing Class Libraries by importing another CL from outside of 
Information Standards Manager we can also compare Class Libraries which have 
been loaded, and are maintained, in the system.  
As part of the last exercise we have already loaded the first library (Original Version) 
into the system. For this part of the exercise we need to add the modified library 
spreadsheet into the system to show how to compare two Class Libraries 
maintained in Information Standards Manager. 
▪ 
Using what we have already learned import as a new CL: 
ISM Training\Comparing\Modified Version.xlsx. 
▪ 
Click Save As  
 (accept the default details). 
▪ 
The Modified Version is the active CL. 
 
 
 
▪ 
Return to the Catalogue view. 
▪ 
On the Comparing – Original Version CL entry we previously loaded select the 
down arrow.  
▪ 
From the menu that opens select Compare with Active CL. 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 85 of 128 
A new record will open where we can search and review the identified changes 
between the two Class Libraries. 
▪ 
In the Side Bar click on Search 
. 
▪ 
Select Modification Filters and then click on Search. 
 
We will see the same results as the previous exercise (the same rules are applied 
when comparing Libraries) and explore the results in the same way. 
 
▪ 
Close the CL comparison 
. 
 
The details of the differences between the two libraries will be cleared and you will 
be returned to the catalogue view. 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

11 Configuration of Validation 
 
Although we can generate non-validated exports it’s strongly recommended to 
validate the CL prior to any export to external systems. The set of available 
validators depends on the ISM configuration. Validators are built as plugin modules 
that can be added/removed to/from the system at any time. A positively validated 
CL can be signed by validators. To validate the CL, it must be active and locked for 
modifications.  
As a result of the validation, the CL is marked up with any issues that have been 
found to contravene the validation rules we have applied. In the first instance we 
can see the first of these very easily as the CL name has been annotated to indicate 
a validation issue. 
Let’s validate the Configuration of Excel Export CL: 
▪ 
Make sure the CL is locked. Then select Validate option: 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Select the validator as shown and tick the CL signature on successful validation. 
We are going to select 3 of the validators for this exercise, however it is 
recommended to only validated one set of rules at any one time when you have 
a large amount of data. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 87 of 128 
▪ 
Then click Validate. 
▪ 
A validation error message will be displayed. For details click View Issues. 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The Errors will be marked-up with the error flag (the red wave underscore): 
 
  
 
Among errors there are may be also other issues of the lower severity but worth 
paying attention to. 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 88 of 128 
There are four levels of validation severity. 
Icon 
Description 
 
Error 
 
Warning 
 
Suggestion 
 
Hint 
 
To reveal any other issues in the CL we can use the search functionality to return 
the validation results. 
▪ 
Select the Search button from the Side Bar 
. 
▪ 
In the search dialogue check the Validation filters option Warning and click 
search. 
 
The search will return several results from the CL we imported earlier. It is possible 
to use the search function to filter by different validation error levels and whether to 
include descendants (i.e. if a Permissible Attribute has issues, the owning class is 
said to have Descendant issues).  
▪ 
In the Search Validation filters section deselect All and select only Errors. 
▪ 
Click Search. 
 
 
We will now see results in the system that meets our new criteria. 
▪ 
Click on Inlet Connection Type token to display the details of the entity. 
 
We can see the Validation error at different levels on the token. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 89 of 128 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Hover the cursor over the errors and the validation error summary will be 
displayed. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 For items having multiple issues, the item will be annotated with the most 
severe issue. 
 Right clicking on the validation error indicators will also bring up menu items 
showing the full validation issue summary. 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 90 of 128 
 
▪ 
None – The rule is disabled. 
▪ 
Hint – The rule is applied with the severity “Hint”. 
▪ 
Suggestion – The rule is applied with the severity “Suggestion”. 
▪ 
Warning – The rule is applied with the severity “Warning”. 
▪ 
Error – The rule is applied with the severity “Error”. 
▪ 
No selection option – The rule is applied with its default severity.  
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The error is caused by the missing Validation Type.  Once the Validation Rule is 
set there must be a Validation Type selected also. 
 
▪ 
Choose the Enumeration option from the available Validation Types. The 
Validation Rule will change to Enumeration List with the corresponding list 
Selected. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 You can alter these settings to fit your policy; 
 Rules having a severity suffixed by “(Derived)” will derive the severity setting 
from the corresponding rule in group “Basic Rules/Default Security 
Levels/Common Properties”. So, if the corresponding rule in group “Basic 
Rules/Default Security Levels/Common Properties” has been altered, the value 
preceding “(Derived)” might be misleading. This discrepancy will be 
consolidated next time the validator runs through.  
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 91 of 128 
 
▪ 
Another issue is related to Safety Tag Function Naming Element of the Safety 
Equipment Naming and Numbering Template 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The root cause of the error is the missing Padding Character. In case there is a 
difference between the min and max number of characters, the padding 
character must be set.  
 
 
▪ 
Let’s define a “0” as the Padding character: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 92 of 128 
▪ 
With the (optional) Padding character added, the current Validation Regular 
Expression [a-z,A-Z]{2,3}  is inconsistent and should be changed to 
  _?[a-z,A-Z]{2,3} .  For the sake of this exercise it’s irrelevant and we won’t 
touch the RegEx.  
 
▪ 
Once all the errors have been resolved we can Unlock and Save the CL to commit 
the changes to ISM. 
▪ 
Lock the CL and re-run the validation and request the validators signatures to 
be added in case of positive validations. 
▪ 
In the catalogue view the earlier validation issues shall be removed first and then 
the new validation started.  
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Make sure the “Sign the Class Library on successful validation” is selected 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The “blue result” is positive in the sense of it lacking errors.   It is recommended 
however to go through the remaining warnings and at least understand their 
meaning and potential consequences in the future. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 93 of 128 
▪ 
Let’s Close the dialog box. And open the Approved By  tab from the Details 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The CL is now validated and signed by three validators. 
▪ 
Save and Unlock the CL with the “Validated” comment 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
We can Tag as Released the CL according to chapter 7.1: 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 94 of 128 
▪ 
Since the CL has been signed no warnings about missing validations are 
displayed any more. 
▪ 
Validation signatures are valid and visible as long as the CL stays unlocked. 
▪ 
As soon as the CL is locked, and any change is applied the validation signatures 
are no longer valid. 
▪ 
Validated CL can now be exported in different formats. Such as ANWH 5.1, if it is 
available:

12 Inconsistency issues 
 
ISM performs basic consistency check of the imported data. In case of major errors, 
the import process is stopped, errors are shown and corrections are requested prior 
to data import. 
12.1 Importing inconsistent workbook 
 
The following example shows an attempt to load a file which has several 
inconsistencies. 
▪ 
Using what we have already learned import 
ISM Training\Validation\Inconsistent Class Library.xlsx as a new CL.  
 
If you are creating a new Class library a message will appear outlining the issues 
ISM encountered trying to load the data and the CL will not be created. 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 96 of 128 
 
▪ 
Context - Contains the name of the sheet where the issue was 
detected. 
▪ 
Name - The Column Header of the cell causing the issue. 
▪ 
Position - The row number in the sheet where the issue was detected. 
▪ 
Error - An explanation of the problem. 
 
If you are importing an inconsistent workbook into an existing CL, the CL may only 
be partially updated (depending on the nature of the inconsistencies). In that case 
you might choose to ‘Unlock and Discard Changes’ 
 to get the CL back to a 
known state. 
To fix the errors open the source file Inconsistent Class Library.xlsx in Excel and 
save it using the _fixed prefix.   
▪ 
Navigate to the error locations (shown by the sheet names) and analyse the 
descriptions. The file has some hints helping to fix the issues. 
▪ 
The first error is caused by the missing value in the Id column. Fix it by entering 
CABL as Id.  
 
 
 
▪ 
 
▪ 
The second error is caused by missing (or misspelled in this case) column 
name.  Correct the name to Class_Id  
 
 
 
 
▪ 
The following five errors have the same root cause as the previous one. As soon 
the column name will be renamed to Class_Id these errors will be gone. 
▪ 
The remaining error is caused by the missing value in the Id column. Fix it by 
entering ISOMETRIC DRAWING as Id.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 The sheet will list the details of the error 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 97 of 128 
▪ 
Save the Excel Inconsistent Class Library_fixed.xlsx  and import as a new class 
library.  
 
The CL should be imported without errors. 
 
 
 
 
The (failed) validation result will resemble the one from the earlier example.: 
 
 
 
 
Fixing the import errors doesn’t automatically fix other inconsistencies in the CL. You 
can perform a validation check even on the unsaved Class library 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

13 Policy Configuration Rules 
 
As well as running the validation rules for different configurators it is also possible 
to manage the validation rules to apply specific policies within ISM. These can be 
applied across Standards to set different layers of validation according to 
requirements i.e. Standard ISM layer, Specific software (Validation configuration) 
layer, corporate standards (across all projects) layer, project (specific to a particular 
project) layer.  
▪ 
Using what we have already learned Import and Save the CL 
ISM Training\Editing\Basic Rules Class Library.xlsx  
Unlock the CL and click onto Details of the newly created CL 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Open the CL Policy Configuration tab and CL validation rules: Class Library – 
Rules targeting the Class Library root definition 
The property Description is defined by default as Hint.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 99 of 128 
This means that the validation process will show a Hint over each empty 
Description field.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Change the above setting from Hint to Warning 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Close the CL Policy Configuration dialog window. 
▪ 
Select Unlock and Save Changes  
 to the CL with the message Missing Class 
Description changed to Warning. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 100 of 128 
14 Composing Class Libraries through inheritance 
 
In the previous chapter we have changed the CL Basic Rules Class Library. Now we 
will use the ISM Composition functionality to create an inheritance hierarchy among 
Class Libraries.  We’ll add the CL Validate with Basic Rules on top of the Corporate 
CL.  
▪ 
For testing that functionality open the Corporate CL created earlier during the 
training. Lock it for editing 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Check Details  
  / CL Policy Configuration tab and CL validation rules. The 
Description property is defined by default as Hint.  
 
 
▪ 
 
▪ 
Select the Composition window that is currently empty and click the Add button. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Search for and select CL Validate with Basic Rules. Then click Add and Close button. 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 101 of 128 
▪ 
A modified Composition window will be shown: 
 
 
 
 
 
   
▪ 
To investigate the modifications, open CL Policy Configuration tab and CL 
validation rules. The Description property definition has been inherited (blue 
background) as Warning from the Validate with Basic Rules CL.  
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
  
▪ 
As a result of the composition, all other definitions originated from Validate with 
Basic Rules have been inherited by the Corporate CL.  
▪ 
As an example, open a Documents hierarchy. There are two “native” classes (3D 
Model and Block Wiring Diagram) that we’ve created manually earlier. All other 
classes have a blue background indicating that they’ve been inherited from the 
upper CL. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪ 
Unlock and save the Corporate CL with comment Composed with CL “Validate 
with Basic Rules” 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 102 of 128 
This example shows the power of creating class libraries through inheritance. 
Enforcing high-level libraries and policy configurations to be inherited by the lower 
level libraries facilitates creation of consistent and complete data models.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx 
Page 103 of 128

16 Managing Schemas 
 
Clear the Catalogue from active CL and select Import function. 
 
Navigate to training folder ISM Training\Schemas\‘Pumps For Schema.xlsx’ and 
import as a new class library. 
Save the CL with default settings. 
This CL has no ISO-15926 extensions defined for Pumps.  
 
The purpose of this exercise is to create an ISO-15926 Mapping section that will be 
displayed on the Details page as its own section. Note this section will appear 
against every class but will only be completed for those classes that have ISO 15926 
assigned. 
  
 
 
AVEVA ISM Training_Workbook_Exercises V1.9.docx

Applying a Configuration Schema like the one defined in previous chapters, requires 
some configuration steps in the ISM. To accomplish that, we’ll use the Manage 
Configurations button         from the Side Bar.  The ISO Mapping CS Configuration 
Node is automatically created based on the Schema Definition. Navigate to the ISO 
Mapping CS node and RClick it to add a Configuration based on this Schema. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Add a Configuration with following parameters and Create when ready.

▪ 
Name= ISO15926 Mapping CN  
▪ 
Description= ISO Mapping Configuration Node

The functional classes tab also now contain the 3 new columns from the schema: 
 ISO15926:id -> iso_mapping_cn:iso_pr_id

 ISO15926:definition -> iso_mapping_cn:iso_pr_def

import/merge method.
